## PAGE 00616: Displaying Features

- page_id: `306283250`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/306283250/Displaying+Features

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) API provides a unified and transaction-safe mechanism for visualizing symbols on diagrams. It simplifies diagram population by exposing high-level display operations for structural and behavioral model elements.

The utility supports multiple display strategies for hierarchical visualization, nested representation, and tree-view organization of symbols.

### Overview

The [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) utility provides APIs for displaying:

- Nested Features
- Nested Parts
- Nested Actions
- Tree-View Features
- Tree-View Parts
- Ports
- Parameters
- Exposed Features
- Nested Members
- Tree View Memebers

The API is designed for programmatic diagram manipulation workflows where model elements must be visualized dynamically without manually creating symbols.

Typical use cases include:

- Visualizing feature hierarchies
- Displaying nested compositions
- Rendering behavioral decompositions
- Creating tree-oriented visualizations for complex systems

Each display operation:

- Accepts either: ** A single PresentationElement ** A Collection< PresentationElement >
- Returns the PresentationElements that were successfully displayed
- Automatically executes inside a editing transaction
- Works directly with diagram presentation elements instead of underlying semantic elements

All Displaying APIs should be invoked when no active session is open. 
These APIs internally perform diagram modifications and layout-related operations that are designed to execute without an active session.

### General Usage Pattern

Most display operations follow the same usage pattern:

1. Obtain the target[PresentationElement](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/PresentationElement.html)

2. Invoke the appropriate display operation

3. Process the returned displayed symbols if additional layout or styling is required

#### Single Element

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayFeaturesNestedView(symbol);]]>
```

#### Multiple Elements

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayFeaturesNestedView(List.of(symbol1, symbol2));
]]>
```

### 1. Displaying Nested Features

Nested feature display visualizes features directly inside the owning presentation element.

Features may include:

- PartUsages
- ReferenceUages
- PortUasges
- ItemUsages etc
- Other owned structural features

Nested visualization is typically used when representing the internal structure of a SysML v2 definition or usage element.

#### 1.1 Display Nested Features (Non-Recursive)

Displays only the immediate nested features. This operation does not traverse deeper feature hierarchies.

#### Single Element

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayFeaturesNestedView(symbol);
]]>
```

#### Multiple Elements

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayFeaturesNestedView(List.of(symbol1, symbol2));]]>
```

#### 1.2 Display Nested Features (Recursive)

Displays all nested features recursively through the containment hierarchy. This operation is useful when the feature structure spans multiple levels.

#### Single Element

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayFeaturesNestedViewRecursively(symbol);]]>
```

#### Multiple Elements

```java
symbols = ...; //List of  symbols for which you want to display nested features
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayFeaturesNestedViewRecursively(symbols);]]>
```

### 2. Displaying Tree-View Features

Tree-view feature display visualizes feature ownership hierarchically using a tree-oriented structure.

This visualization mode is useful when:

- Feature hierarchies are deeply nested
- Structural readability is important
- The model contains large ownership chains

Compared to standard nested display, tree-view layout improves clarity for complex feature structures.

#### 2.1 Tree-View Features (Non-Recursive)

Displays only direct child features in tree-view layout.

```java
symbols = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayFeaturesTreeView(symbols);]]>
```

#### 2.2 Tree-View Features (Recursive)

Displays the complete feature hierarchy using tree-view layout.

```java
symbols = ...; 
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayFeaturesTreeViewRecursively(symbols);]]>
```

### 3. Displaying Nested Parts

Nested parts display visualizes part symbols directly inside the owning presentation element.

#### 3.1 Display Nested Parts (Non-Recursive)

Displays only directly owned parts.

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayPartsNestedView(symbol);]]>
```

#### 3.2 Display Nested Parts (Recursive)

Displays all nested parts recursively.

```java
displayed =Displaying.displayPartsNestedViewRecursively(symbol);]]>
```

### 4. Displaying Tree-View Parts

Tree-view parts display visualizes part hierarchies using a tree layout instead of standard nested containment visualization.

This display flavor is recommended for large structural hierarchies where deeply nested symbols become difficult to read.

#### 4.1 Tree-View Parts (Non-Recursive)

Displays direct child parts using tree-view layout.

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayPartsTreeView(symbol);]]>
```

#### 4.2 Tree-View Parts (Recursive)

Displays the complete part hierarchy recursively using tree-view layout.

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayPartsTreeViewRecursively(symbol);]]>
```

### 5. Displaying Nested Actions

Nested actions visualize actions within structures.

#### 5.1 Display Nested Actions (Non-Recursive)

Displays only direct nested actions.

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayActionsNestedView(symbol);]]>
```

#### 5.2 Display Nested Actions (Recursive)

Displays nested actions recursively through the hierarchy.

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayActionsNestedViewRecursively(symbol);]]>
```

### 6. Displaying Ports

Displays ports using the default port display configuration.

This operation is intended specifically for port visualization and does not recursively traverse nested structures.

#### Single Element

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayPorts(symbol);]]>
```

#### Multiple Elements

```java
symbols = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayPorts(symbols);]]>
```

### 7. Displaying Parameters

Displays parameters using the default parameter display configuration. This operation is intended specifically for parameters visualization and does not recursively traverse nested structures.

```java
symbols = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayParameters(symbols);// for multiple target elements

PresentationElement symbol= ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayParameters(symbol);// for single target elements]]>
```

### 8. Displaying Exposed Features

Displays exposed features using the default configuration for any diagram.

```java
targets = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayExposedFeatures(targets);// for multiple target diagrams

Diagram target= ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayExposedFeatures(target);// for single target diagram]]>
```

### 9. Displaying Nested Members for Package

Nested member display visualizes SysML v2 member hierarchies directly inside the owning Package symbol.

#### 9.1 Displaying Nested Members (Non-Recursive)

Displays only the immediate nested members of the target presentation elements. This operation does not recursively traverse deeper ownership hierarchies.

```java
symbols = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayMembersNestedView(symbols);// for multiple target diagrams

PackageShape symbol= ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayMembersNestedView(symbol);// for single target diagram]]>
```

#### 9.2 Displaying Nested Members (Recursive)

Displays nested members recursively through the full ownership hierarchy. This operation is useful for deep structural decomposition visualization.

```java
symbols = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayMembersNestedViewRecursively(symbols);// for multiple target diagrams

PackageShape symbol = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayMembersNestedViewRecursively(symbol);// for single target diagram]]>
```

### 10. Displaying Tree View Members

Tree-view member display visualizes membership hierarchies using a tree-oriented layout. Compared to standard nested display, tree-view layout improves readability for large or deeply nested structures.

#### 10.1 Tree View Members (Non-Recursive)

Displays immediate members in tree-view layout.

```java
symbols = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayMembersTreeView(symbols);]]>
```

#### 10.2 Tree View Members (Recursive)

Displays the complete member hierarchy recursively using tree-view layout. This operation is recommended for visualizing large SysML v2 decomposition hierarchies.

```java
symbols = ...;
Collection displayed = Displaying.displayMembersTreeViewRecursively(symbols);]]>
```

### 11. Notes and Best Practices

Returned presentation elements can be used for:

- Styling
- Filtering
- Selection
- Diagram analysis

The target presentation element must belong to an existing diagram before invoking display operations.

### 12. Summary

The [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) utility provides a concise and consistent API for diagram population across structural and behavioral modeling scenarios.

It supports:

- Standard nested visualization
- Tree-view hierarchical visualization
- Recursive traversal
- Batch display operations
- Transaction-safe execution

Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayPorts(symbols);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1>7. Displaying Parameters</h1><p>Displays parameters using the default parameter display configuration. This operation is intended specifically for parameters visualization and does not recursively traverse nested structures.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c4dcecd-8ae4-4312-b241-54ef7b898e68"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PresentationElement> symbols = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayParameters(symbols);// for multiple target elements

PresentationElement symbol= ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayParameters(symbol);// for single target elements]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1>8. Displaying Exposed Features</h1><p> Displays exposed features using the default configuration for any diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b474f675-5d5c-4fb7-9f33-773a933033ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Diagram;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<Diagram> targets = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayExposedFeatures(targets);// for multiple target diagrams

Diagram target= ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayExposedFeatures(target);// for single target diagram]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1>9. Displaying Nested Members for Package</h1><p>Nested member display visualizes SysML v2 member hierarchies directly inside the owning Package symbol.</p><h2>9.1 Displaying Nested Members (Non-Recursive)</h2><p>Displays only the immediate nested members of the target presentation elements. This operation does not recursively traverse deeper ownership hierarchies.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2dc0a52-0693-4984-a523-f112ed6428b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PackageShape;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PackageShape> symbols = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayMembersNestedView(symbols);// for multiple target diagrams

PackageShape symbol= ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayMembersNestedView(symbol);// for single target diagram]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>9.2 Displaying Nested Members (Recursive)</h2><p>Displays nested members recursively through the full ownership hierarchy. This operation is useful for deep structural decomposition visualization.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d030003-0df8-4c34-b1cf-f8dd19f82768"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PackageShape;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PackageShape> symbols = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayMembersNestedViewRecursively(symbols);// for multiple target diagrams

PackageShape symbol = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayMembersNestedViewRecursively(symbol);// for single target diagram]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1><br />10. Displaying Tree View Members </h1><p>Tree-view member display visualizes membership hierarchies using a tree-oriented layout. Compared to standard nested display, tree-view layout improves readability for large or deeply nested structures.</p><h2>10.1 Tree View Members (Non-Recursive)</h2><p>Displays immediate members in tree-view layout.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb48bfea-a843-4244-b043-93b855c701a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PackageShape;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PackageShape> symbols = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayMembersTreeView(symbols);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>10.2 Tree View Members (Recursive)</h2><p>Displays the complete member hierarchy recursively using tree-view layout. This operation is recommended for visualizing large SysML v2 decomposition hierarchies.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb02cc75-13c5-4ac2-8ded-ac3cd49c1cc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PackageShape;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PackageShape> symbols = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayMembersTreeViewRecursively(symbols);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1>11. Notes and Best Practices</h1><p>Returned presentation elements can be used for:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bb81b563-e3e9-443f-aac4-e6a41e58cbaa">Styling</li><li data-uuid="c1c0a8f7-8b65-4e38-8463-4a842ebfb3ff">Filtering</li><li data-uuid="9e4c9ff5-8156-4f41-b136-51ab6f1bc241">Selection</li><li data-uuid="1e1a7f36-b379-4765-a4eb-70f104497796">Diagram analysis</li></ul><p>The target presentation element must belong to an existing diagram before invoking display operations.</p><h1>12. Summary</h1><p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html">Displaying</a> utility provides a concise and consistent API for diagram population across structural and behavioral modeling scenarios.</p><p>It supports:</p><ul><li data-uuid="1309f441-b6dd-44c8-8fef-c8f8215c0bc4">Standard nested visualization</li><li data-uuid="5d77b139-3dc8-45c8-8adc-d009872108ff">Tree-view hierarchical visualization</li><li data-uuid="f63957ef-d25d-4625-8df4-c2d8bcc97aea">Recursive traversal</li><li data-uuid="9d0e60bc-29be-44b2-b40c-923f0dd62015">Batch display operations</li><li data-uuid="0045f6a1-c14a-4d5d-9965-44c0a9121b45">Transaction-safe execution</li></ul>
````

- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayAllPorts

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#collectAvailablePorts

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayPorts(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement, java.util.List<com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdports.Port>)

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayPorts(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement, java.util.List<com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdports.Port>, boolean, boolean, boolean

###### **Example: Displaying unnamed ports**

```java
ports = DisplayPorts.collectAvailablePorts(view, false, false);
	List unnamedPorts = ports.stream().filter(port -> port.getName().isEmpty()).toList();
	DisplayPorts.displayPorts(view, unnamedPorts);

	sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]>
```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\displayports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell> We provide an API to simplify displaying of Ports on Symbols. Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayAllPorts</em> to display all ports or <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#collectAvailablePorts</em> to get available ports and then <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayPorts(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement, java.util.List&lt;com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdports.Port&gt;)</em> or <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayPorts(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement, java.util.List&lt;com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdports.Port&gt;, boolean, boolean, boolean</em>) to display just some of them.<br /><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example: Displaying unnamed ports<br /></strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27d9045e-ee43-4a27-b18b-9e033face031"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
	sessionManager.createSession(project, getName());

	PresentationElement view = ...; // A symbol for which you want to display ports

	List<Port> ports = DisplayPorts.collectAvailablePorts(view, false, false);
	List<Port> unnamedPorts = ports.stream().filter(port -> port.getName().isEmpty()).toList();
	DisplayPorts.displayPorts(view, unnamedPorts);

	sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li style="font-style: italic;"><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\displayports</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58e45d31-88de-4344-8caa-c6382236cc70"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00618: Displaying related elements in the diagram

- page_id: `249573490`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573490/Displaying+related+elements+in+the+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:17:17.105+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools allow displaying symbols that are related to a given symbol via relationships. The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayRelatedSymbols* class provides API methods for this. Using this class you can control any of the following behaviors for displaying the related symbols logic:

- What relationship types should be included.
- The depth of a relationship tree.
- Whether or not existing symbols should be reused.

###### **Example: Displaying related generalizations and interface realizations**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\displayrelated

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tools allow displaying symbols that are related to a given symbol via relationships. The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayRelatedSymbols</em> class provides API methods for this. Using this class you can control any of the following behaviors for displaying the related symbols logic:</p><ul><li>What relationship types should be included.</li><li>The depth of a relationship tree.</li><li>Whether or not existing symbols should be reused.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example: Displaying related generalizations and interface realizations</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27d9045e-ee43-4a27-b18b-9e033face031"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Display related");
 
    Set linkTypes = new HashSet();
    linkTypes.add(new LinkType(Generalization.class));
    linkTypes.add(new LinkType(InterfaceRealization.class));
    
    DisplayRelatedSymbolsInfo info = new DisplayRelatedSymbolsInfo(linkTypes);
    info.setDepthLimited(true);
    info.setDepthLimit(3);
 
    PresentationElement view = ...; // A symbol for which you need to invoke the displaying related symbols action.
    DisplayRelatedSymbols.displayRelatedSymbols(view, info);
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\displayrelated</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58e45d31-88de-4344-8caa-c6382236cc70"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=309365419 space=DEVG version=18 -->
## PAGE 00619: Displaying Relations

- page_id: `309365419`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/309365419/Displaying+Relations
- version_number: 18
- version_date: `2026-05-28T22:14:28.091+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Managing Presentation Elements > Displaying Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) utility class provides APIs for automatically displaying relationships between elements on a diagram.

These APIs can be used to:

- Display all relationships connected to elements
- Display only relationships between selected elements
- Exclude specialization relationships
- Automatically populate diagrams with related connections

All APIs operate on [PresentationElement](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/PresentationElement.html) instances that are already displayed on a diagram.

All Displaying APIs should be invoked when no active session is open. 
These APIs internally perform diagram modifications and layout-related operations that are designed to execute without an active session.

### Displaying All Relations

Displays all relationships connected to the provided presentation elements, including specialization relationships.

```java
targets = ...;
Collection displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllRelations(targets); // for multiple target elements

PresentationElement target = ...;
Collection displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllRelations(target); // for single target element]]>
```

### Displaying All Relations Excluding Specializations

Displays all relationships connected to the provided presentation elements while excluding specialization relationships. 
This is useful for reducing diagram complexity when inheritance relationships are not relevant.

```java
targets = ...;
Collection displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllRelationsExcludingSpecializations(targets);

PresentationElement target = ...;
Collection displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllRelationsExcludingSpecializations(target);]]>
```

### Displaying In-Between Relations

Displays only the relationships between the provided presentation elements.Unlike `displayAllRelations`, this API does not display relationships connected to external elements. 
This helps keep diagrams focused and avoids unnecessary clutter.

```java
targets = ...;
Collection displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllInBetweenRelations(targets);]]>
```

### Displaying In-Between Relations Excluding Specializations

Displays only the relationships between the provided presentation elements while excluding specialization/generalization relationships. 
This provides a focused relationship view with reduced visual complexity.

```java
targets = ...;
Collection displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllInBetweenRelationsExcludingSpecializations(targets);]]>
```

### Summary

The [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) relation APIs provide a simple way to automatically visualize relationships on diagrams.

Using these APIs, developers can:

- Populate diagrams programmatically
- Reduce manual diagram editing
- Filter specialization relationships
- Generate cleaner and more focused visualizations

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html">Displaying</a> utility class provides APIs for automatically displaying relationships between elements on a diagram.</p><p>These APIs can be used to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e4afbe61-2d34-450d-9386-9d6fca307b9c">Display all relationships connected to elements</li><li data-uuid="9d5f2f26-195a-4eb8-88c5-8a7a9d6cf33f">Display only relationships between selected elements</li><li data-uuid="6576d01e-81ee-4a2d-8465-0554b63b11be">Exclude specialization relationships</li><li data-uuid="2d888169-d1a0-4e0d-a1ef-0b20ba66b29b">Automatically populate diagrams with related connections</li></ul><p>All APIs operate on <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/PresentationElement.html">PresentationElement</a> instances that are already displayed on a diagram. <br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30b3ff17-4741-4430-9b7f-3376b1a824e3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">All Displaying APIs should be invoked when no active session is open.</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">These APIs internally perform diagram modifications and layout-related operations that are designed to execute without an active session.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1>Displaying All Relations</h1><p>Displays all relationships connected to the provided presentation elements, including specialization relationships.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89161a3f-3b6e-4d2e-aefd-7a1b4a083b24"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PresentationElement> targets = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllRelations(targets); // for multiple target elements

PresentationElement target = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllRelations(target); // for single target element]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1 class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly m-0">Displaying All Relations Excluding Specializations</h1><p class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly m-0">Displays all relationships connected to the provided presentation elements while excluding specialization relationships. <br />This is useful for reducing diagram complexity when inheritance relationships are not relevant.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d703b05b-5362-4e78-845b-47e5f375ff1c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PresentationElement> targets = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllRelationsExcludingSpecializations(targets);

PresentationElement target = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllRelationsExcludingSpecializations(target);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1>Displaying In-Between Relations</h1><p>Displays only the relationships between the provided presentation elements.Unlike <code>displayAllRelations</code>, this API does not display relationships connected to external elements.<br />This helps keep diagrams focused and avoids unnecessary clutter.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db2d4bc8-4115-4713-9bbb-64f2724b05cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PresentationElement> targets = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllInBetweenRelations(targets);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1>Displaying In-Between Relations Excluding Specializations</h1><p>Displays only the relationships between the provided presentation elements while excluding specialization/generalization relationships.<br />This provides a focused relationship view with reduced visual complexity.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a9bb92f-37f2-40fb-adf5-bad4e158e590"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Displaying;

Collection<PresentationElement> targets = ...;
Collection<PresentationElement> displayedRelations = Displaying.displayAllInBetweenRelationsExcludingSpecializations(targets);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1>Summary</h1><p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html">Displaying</a> relation APIs provide a simple way to automatically visualize relationships on diagrams.</p><p>Using these APIs, developers can:</p><ul><li data-uuid="dea7d7f3-799a-4662-a55f-66af339f7415">Populate diagrams programmatically</li><li data-uuid="d8a4fc48-9885-4d12-8bdb-a6ac53adf68b">Reduce manual diagram editing</li><li data-uuid="012a353e-7971-4366-9cbe-e8a8569c3528">Filter specialization relationships</li><li data-uuid="2e9294ce-ed14-46b6-8c41-c69d8b692870">Generate cleaner and more focused visualizations</li></ul><p><br />  </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312246468 space=DEVG version=11 -->
## PAGE 00620: Displaying Swimlanes

- page_id: `312246468`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/312246468/Displaying+Swimlanes
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2026-06-11T20:34:20.242+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Managing Presentation Elements > Displaying Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page documents the swimlane-related display operations provided by the [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) utility class.

These APIs provide a convenient way to visualize performers, swimlanes, and action allocations within SysML v2 behavior diagrams. They automatically create and organize the required diagram elements to improve readability and help communicate behavioral responsibility and ownership.

Typical use cases include:

- Visualizing performer responsibilities
- Organizing actions into swimlanes
- Displaying performer allocations
- Creating behavior-centric diagram views
- Enhancing activity and action decomposition diagrams

All operations:

- Accept either a single PresentationElement or a collection of PresentationElement s
- Return the PresentationElements that were successfully displayed
- Automatically manage the required diagram update transactions
- Should be invoked outside an active MagicDraw session

All Displaying APIs should be invoked when no active session is open. 
These APIs internally perform diagram modifications and layout-related operations that are designed to execute without an active session.

#### Displaying Swimlanes and Performed Actions

Displays performer swimlanes and the actions performed by those performers.

This operation is useful when visualizing behavioral responsibility within a system model.

##### Single Element

```java
displayed = Displaying.displaySwimlanesAndPerformedActions(target);
]]>
```

##### Multiple Elements

```java
targets =List.of(target1, target2);
Collection displayed = Displaying.displaySwimlanesAndPerformedActions(targets);
]]>
```

##### Behavior

This operation:

- Creates missing performer swimlanes when required
- Displays actions performed by those performers
- Organizes displayed actions under the appropriate swimlane

#### Displaying Missing Performers

In some situations, performer swimlanes already exist on a diagram, but not all performers are represented.

The following APIs allow additional performers to be added to an existing swimlane structure.

##### Display Missing Performers on the Left/Right

Displays performers that are not currently represented on the left/right side of a performer header.

##### Parameters

`headerShape` : Performer header shape where the performers will be added 
`performers` : Performers to display

##### Examples

```java
presentationElements =
            Displaying.displaySwimlanesAndPerformedActions(performAction);

    // Find the header shape corresponding to the specified performer (switchPart)
    // among the displayed swimlane presentation elements.
    PerformerHeaderShape switchPartHeaderShape =
            findHeaderShapeForPerformer(presentationElements, switchPart);

    if (switchPartHeaderShape != null)
    {
        // Create a new part usage(performer) representing the flight controller
        // owned by the rescueDrone part.
        PartUsage flightController =
                createPartUsage(rescueDrone, "flightController");

        // Display the flightController as a missing performer to the left
        // of the specified performer header shape.
        Collection displayed =
                Displaying.displayMissingPerformersOnLeft(
                        switchPartHeaderShape,
                        List.of(flightController));
    }
}

private static PerformerHeaderShape findHeaderShapeForPerformer(Collection extends PresentationElement> presentationElements, PartUsage performer)
{
	return presentationElements.stream()
		.filter(PerformerHeaderShape.class::isInstance)
		.map(PerformerHeaderShape.class::cast)
		.filter(header -> header.getModelElement() == performer)
		.findFirst()
		.orElse(null);
}]]>
```

```java
displayed = Displaying.displayMissingPerformersOnRight(performer, List.of(propulsionSystem));
    }
}]]>
```

##### Typical Usage

Use this operation when:

- Expanding an existing swimlane structure
- Adding newly introduced performers
- Maintaining performer ordering within a diagram

#### Summary

The swimlane-related APIs of the [Displaying](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html) utility provide a high-level mechanism for visualizing performer responsibilities and behavioral structures in SysML v2 diagrams.

These APIs support:

- Swimlane creation
- Performer visualization
- Performed action display
- Owned action display
- Incremental performer addition
- Transaction-safe diagram updates

Using these operations helps create clearer behavioral diagrams that communicate performer responsibilities and action organization in a consistent and scalable manner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page documents the swimlane-related display operations provided by the <a class="external-link" style="text-align: left;" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html">Displaying</a> utility class.</p><p>These APIs provide a convenient way to visualize performers, swimlanes, and action allocations within SysML v2 behavior diagrams. They automatically create and organize the required diagram elements to improve readability and help communicate behavioral responsibility and ownership.</p><p>Typical use cases include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c8caf3a2-4128-4e3a-867b-e07db14ad639">Visualizing performer responsibilities</li><li data-uuid="c966a836-7977-4452-802f-c32e7899e73d">Organizing actions into swimlanes</li><li data-uuid="4ecded60-0239-499c-9af2-388ae1581172">Displaying performer allocations</li><li data-uuid="674705e3-8880-44c1-8076-9eeb4bbd5c34">Creating behavior-centric diagram views</li><li data-uuid="3f3a66c1-321d-4731-ba31-52005d9fef03">Enhancing activity and action decomposition diagrams</li></ul><p>All operations:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2ff2eb5d-08da-49a6-adc4-2523ca75d3b6">Accept either a single <code>PresentationElement</code> or a collection of <code>PresentationElement</code>s</li><li data-uuid="1e5850d2-6777-4e53-b2da-e8d4e7e5b5d0">Return the <code>PresentationElements</code> that were successfully displayed</li><li data-uuid="8e4157e3-6ea5-4d34-a9fd-47311a042d19">Automatically manage the required diagram update transactions</li><li data-uuid="4c41c1be-dafb-4e5d-af2c-37d58ea69b10">Should be invoked outside an active MagicDraw session</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9823493-8bb3-4871-9bf4-dd5915b4c3e6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">All Displaying APIs should be invoked when no active session is open.</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">These APIs internally perform diagram modifications and layout-related operations that are designed to execute without an active session.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Displaying Swimlanes and Performed Actions</h2><p>Displays performer swimlanes and the actions performed by those performers.</p><p>This operation is useful when visualizing behavioral responsibility within a system model.</p><h3>Single Element</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38bcf6eb-2949-4782-bf84-2cbd4a65cfb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import java.util.Collection;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.diagram.ui.actions.display.Displaying;

PresentationElement performActionSymbol = createDiagramAndShapeElementFor(partsPackage, locatePersonPerformAction);// the performAction symbol for which DisplaySwimlanes can be invoked.
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displaySwimlanesAndPerformedActions(target);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Multiple Elements</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab8d6ede-5651-4771-a06e-a8d6b9cc8976"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import java.util.Collection;
import java.util.List;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.diagram.ui.actions.display.Displaying;

Collection<PresentationElement> targets =List.of(target1, target2);
Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displaySwimlanesAndPerformedActions(targets);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Behavior</h3><p>This operation:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f90354bc-6d27-424a-8099-206056538920">Creates missing performer swimlanes when required</li><li data-uuid="23e677e1-5c2b-4691-a77c-aad68d180b84">Displays actions performed by those performers</li><li data-uuid="836b41a0-d33d-4c37-91da-ef72417d30cf">Organizes displayed actions under the appropriate swimlane</li></ul><h2>Displaying Missing Performers</h2><p>In some situations, performer swimlanes already exist on a diagram, but not all performers are represented.</p><p>The following APIs allow additional performers to be added to an existing swimlane structure.</p><h3>Display Missing Performers on the Left/Right</h3><p>Displays performers that are not currently represented on the left/right side of a performer header.</p><h3>Parameters</h3><p><code>headerShape</code> : Performer header shape where the performers will be added <br /><code>performers</code> : Performers to display </p><h3>Examples</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8991f982-e4ad-48a3-93a0-13b7486faef7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import java.util.Collection;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.diagram.ui.actions.display.Displaying;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PerformerHeaderShape;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PerformersShape;

// Create a shape for the perform action.
PresentationElement performAction =
        createDiagramAndShapeElementFor(partsPackage, locatePersonPerformAction);

if (performAction != null)
{
    // Display the swimlanes and performed actions associated with the action.
    // The returned collection contains the newly displayed presentation elements.
    Collection<PresentationElement> presentationElements =
            Displaying.displaySwimlanesAndPerformedActions(performAction);

    // Find the header shape corresponding to the specified performer (switchPart)
    // among the displayed swimlane presentation elements.
    PerformerHeaderShape switchPartHeaderShape =
            findHeaderShapeForPerformer(presentationElements, switchPart);

    if (switchPartHeaderShape != null)
    {
        // Create a new part usage(performer) representing the flight controller
        // owned by the rescueDrone part.
        PartUsage flightController =
                createPartUsage(rescueDrone, "flightController");

        // Display the flightController as a missing performer to the left
        // of the specified performer header shape.
        Collection<PresentationElement> displayed =
                Displaying.displayMissingPerformersOnLeft(
                        switchPartHeaderShape,
                        List.of(flightController));
    }
}

private static PerformerHeaderShape findHeaderShapeForPerformer(Collection<? extends PresentationElement> presentationElements, PartUsage performer)
{
	return presentationElements.stream()
		.filter(PerformerHeaderShape.class::isInstance)
		.map(PerformerHeaderShape.class::cast)
		.filter(header -> header.getModelElement() == performer)
		.findFirst()
		.orElse(null);
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09aa03b3-2bb8-4983-92ad-52aa7f98dcde"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import java.util.Collection;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.diagram.ui.actions.display.Displaying;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PerformerHeaderShape;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PerformersShape;

// Find the performers swimlane shape associated with the perform action.
PerformersShape shape = performAction.getPresentationElements()
        .stream()
        .filter(PerformersShape.class::isInstance)
        .map(PerformersShape.class::cast)
        .findFirst()
        .orElse(null);

if (shape != null)
{
    // Locate the header corresponding to the switchPart performer.
    PerformerHeaderShape performer =
            findHeaderShapeForPerformer(shape.getAllHeaders(), switchPart);

    if (performer != null)
    {
        // Create a new performer part that is not currently displayed.
        PartUsage propulsionSystem =
                createPartUsage(rescueDrone, "propulsionSystem");

        // Display the missing performer to the right of the selected performer.
        Collection<PresentationElement> displayed = Displaying.displayMissingPerformersOnRight(performer, List.of(propulsionSystem));
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Typical Usage</h3><p>Use this operation when:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f9d0fcb2-ba86-4c57-9cb9-292760b2cac6">Expanding an existing swimlane structure</li><li data-uuid="51fc770f-4132-45ba-af54-1cec9b9183eb">Adding newly introduced performers</li><li data-uuid="ecada36f-0437-46e1-9fb3-8f3a8a6f06bc">Maintaining performer ordering within a diagram</li></ul><h2>Summary</h2><p>The swimlane-related APIs of the <a class="external-link" style="text-align: left;" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Displaying.html">Displaying</a> utility provide a high-level mechanism for visualizing performer responsibilities and behavioral structures in SysML v2 diagrams.</p><p>These APIs support:</p><ul><li data-uuid="1188ed16-5d6a-4804-ad00-a0e4e0614908">Swimlane creation</li><li data-uuid="b814bca9-1138-4839-a208-d5077401ee7f">Performer visualization</li><li data-uuid="11b26d9a-5766-4fb7-8ef2-bf4b6c69625e">Performed action display</li><li data-uuid="881a11be-1840-4e7c-9727-cf5d4110dfaa">Owned action display</li><li data-uuid="98f75c42-b7f1-4093-ac64-1401f6a5aba1">Incremental performer addition</li><li data-uuid="0003b6e6-25e7-4c4a-95c0-137830232f8d">Transaction-safe diagram updates</li></ul><p>Using these operations helps create clearer behavioral diagrams that communicate performer responsibilities and action organization in a consistent and scalable manner.</p>
````

## PAGE 00621: Distributing Resources

- page_id: `249573359`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573359/Distributing+Resources
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:37.719+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General
- labels: ['resource-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

515448144

515448146

515448145

In this section, you will find information about how to share your created resources with other users. The easiest way to distribute data is to store resources in the one zip archive and import files to your modeling tool with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MT'].

You can distribute the following types of resources:

- Custom Diagrams
- Profiles
- Templates
- Samples and Documentation
- Plugins

You can distribute the one resource type or you may distribute the whole set of resources.

515448143

**Related pages**

## PAGE 00622: Eclipse Development Setup

- page_id: `298094777`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/298094777/Eclipse+Development+Setup
- version_number: 15
- version_date: `2026-04-09T19:40:15.135+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SysML v2 plugin for CEA/CSM/MSOSA/MCSE includes a fully functional Eclipse example project. This project demonstrates how to interact with the SysML v2 metamodel using the Open API.

It serves as a reference implementation and a starting point for developing custom plugins.

The tool and the SysML v2 plugin need to be installed before we proceed with setting up the environment.

#### **Locating the Eclipse Project**

After installing the SysML v2 plugin via the Resource Manager, the Eclipse project archive is available at:

**<tool.install.dir>*\openapi\ide\md_sysml2_openapi-…-ide-eclipse.zip.*

#### **Importing the Eclipse Projects**

The archive contains three related Eclipse projects bundled in a single ZIP file.

To import them:

1. Open Eclipse
2. Select File → Import → Existing Projects into Workspace
3. Choose Select archive file and point to the ZIP file
4. Import all projects

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Configuring the MagicDraw Installation Path**

The projects rely on an Eclipse Linked Resource variable:

MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY

Set this variable to your installation directory. This allows Eclipse to resolve all required Open API JAR dependencies.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Running the Ant Build Script**

Before compiling the projects, you must initialize dependencies:

1. Locate the build.xml script in the MagicDraw project
2. Run the script to link the required API JAR files from your installation into the workspace

After the build completes:

- Refresh the workspace (F5) or
- Restart Eclipse

Once refreshed, Eclipse will rebuild the classpath and all error markers should be resolved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Launching MagicDraw with the Example Plugin**

After the projects compile successfully, you can run MagicDraw directly from Eclipse:

- Use the provided launch configuration in the Launch project
- This configuration loads:
  - Plugins from the installation directory
  - The SysML v2 example plugin from your workspace

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Ready for Development**

Your Eclipse environment is now ready for:

- Developing SysML v2 plugins
- Running and debugging directly from Eclipse
- Exploring example Open API usage

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

refreshed, Eclipse will rebuild the classpath and all error markers should be resolved.</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="94"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-4-1_21-43-40.png" ## PAGE 00623: Editing API

- page_id: `298095411`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/298095411/Editing+API
- version_number: 20
- version_date: `2026-04-16T05:07:04.503+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Model Modification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html)` API is the recommended way to perform model modifications. It provides built-in support for **command tracking** and **undo/redo**.

All changes executed through this API are:

- Recorded as a [command](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Command.html) Each execution is captured as a named command
- Stored in command history Enables undo and redo of model changes

This ensures that model modifications are **safe, consistent, and traceable**.

#### **Getting an Editing Instance**

Obtain an `[Editing](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html)` instance from the current project:

```java

```

#### **Executing Model Changes**

Use the `execute` method to perform model modifications.

- The provided name identifies the command in the undo/redo history
- All changes inside the lambda are executed within a single transaction

```java
{
    // model changes here
});]]>
```

#### **Best Practices**

- Prefer [Editing.execute(...)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html#execute(java.lang.String,java.lang.Runnable)) for all model changes
- Use meaningful command names (they appear in undo/redo history)
- Group related changes into a single execution block
- Avoid modifying the model outside of an execution context

#### **When to Use This API**

Use the Editing API for:

- Creating, updating, or deleting model elements
- Batch modifications that should be treated as one operation
- Any changes that must support undo/redo

For lower-level control over sessions, see: **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SessionManager API' space='']**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing</a></code> API is the recommended way to perform model modifications. It provides built-in support for <strong>command tracking</strong> and <strong>undo/redo</strong>.</p><p>All changes executed through this API are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f90e3587-c8c0-4dc2-9a05-9f4c4b874fea">Recorded as a <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Command.html">command</a></strong><br />Each execution is captured as a named command</li><li data-uuid="d1be5e0f-3f28-4f50-a209-3b0d43322eab">Stored in <strong>command history</strong><br />Enables undo and redo of model changes</li></ul><p>This ensures that model modifications are <strong>safe, consistent, and traceable</strong>.</p><h2><span><strong>Getting an Editing Instance</strong></span></h2><p>Obtain an <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html">Editing</a></code> instance from the current project:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb723b17-8711-4e6f-955d-d4733302f21a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing;

Editing editing = Editing.getInstance(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><span><strong>Executing Model Changes</strong></span></h2><p>Use the <code>execute</code> method to perform model modifications.</p><ul><li data-uuid="aff01d1a-acda-4a5b-b65d-8490edbd7a28">The provided name identifies the command in the undo/redo history</li><li data-uuid="b5990400-dda1-47d2-ac52-a983172f69cb">All changes inside the lambda are executed within a single transaction</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0dd73da-f721-4f42-b599-6db263d8463a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing;

Editing editing = Editing.getInstance(project);

editing.execute("Create element", () -> {
    // model changes here
});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><span><strong>Best Practices</strong></span></h2><ul><li data-uuid="e4f0f243-fcd3-498a-945a-ccfcac735c5e">Prefer <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html#execute(java.lang.String,java.lang.Runnable)">Editing.execute(...)</a></code> for all model changes</li><li data-uuid="2a46f50e-7f67-4c7d-93ca-a0750dc54979">Use meaningful command names (they appear in undo/redo history)</li><li data-uuid="82b316be-0c26-4616-a807-6dc9c6699bc6">Group related changes into a single execution block</li><li data-uuid="9c5e6c91-e0e6-42ec-9b17-784439858144">Avoid modifying the model outside of an execution context</li></ul><h2><span><strong>When to Use This API</strong></span></h2><p>Use the Editing API for:</p><ul><li data-uuid="548e2c26-a28c-402f-aabd-2994eaddb9b3">Creating, updating, or deleting model elements</li><li data-uuid="d393cae1-6cb6-46b0-a986-fb3b3be3d9cc">Batch modifications that should be treated as one operation</li><li data-uuid="c919b600-7e13-4aaf-ba08-2a1cdf6f66e7">Any changes that must support undo/redo</li></ul><p>For lower-level control over sessions, see: <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></strong></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573531 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00624: Editing hyperlinks

- page_id: `249573531`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573531/Editing+hyperlinks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:18:12.781+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Managing navigation in model > Managing hyperlinks
- labels: ['hyperlink-text', 'hyperlink-text-active', 'hyperlink-model', 'hyperlink-model-active']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hyperlinks are implemented using stereotypes and tag values. The *HyperlinkOwner*stereotype has the following its properties (tags):

- hyperlinkText - all simple hyperlinks
- hyperlinkTextActive - an active simple hyperlink
- hyperlinkModel - all hyperlinks to a model element
- hyperlinkModelActive - an active hyperlink to a model element

You can use generic profiling API to edit/add/remove hyperlinks to model elements. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with stereotypes and tagged values' space=''] for more details.

Recommended way is to use utility class *com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils*. This class provides utility methods for adding/removing/retrieving***com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink* from model Element,

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with stereotypes and tagged values' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Hyperlinks are implemented using stereotypes and tag values. The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">HyperlinkOwner </em>stereotype has the following its properties (tags):</p><ul><li><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">hyperlinkText  </em>- all simple hyperlinks</li><li><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">hyperlinkTextActive </em>- an active simple hyperlink</li><li><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">hyperlinkModel </em>- all hyperlinks to a model element</li><li><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">hyperlinkModelActive </em>- an active hyperlink to a model element</li></ul><p>You can use generic profiling API to edit/add/remove hyperlinks to model elements. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with stereotypes and tagged values" /></ac:link> for more details.</p><p>Recommended way is to use utility class <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils</em>. This class provides utility methods for adding/removing/retrieving<em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink</em> from model Element, </p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85facba2-7e1a-4a0e-a0f9-4ea748c74ea8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with stereotypes and tagged values" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
