<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573420 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00625: Editing model elements

- page_id: `249573420`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573420/Editing+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:36:31.392+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To edit some *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*, a session with the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager***must be created.

All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.

```java

```

It is the programmer responsibility to ensure that the modified element is not read-only by checking element editing permissions.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SessionManager API' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Checking Model Element Permissions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To edit some <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em>, a session with the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager</em><em> </em>must be created. </p><p>All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cb57726-9021-46e3-a746-c9f00cb8d206"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Edit class A");
    if (classA.isEditable())
    {
        classA.setName(newName);
    }
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection1">It is the programmer responsibility to ensure that the modified element is not read-only by checking element editing permissions.</div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f80fb8f-62ab-4a95-b916-c5df66cb9b23"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Checking Model Element Permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573627 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00626: Element Referencing in Texts

- page_id: `249573627`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573627/Element+Referencing+in+Texts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:59:53.316+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API provides the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.ElementReferencingInTexts* class for creating text with element references, setting, and getting text from elements.

#### INFO: Examples

Examples

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling_tool_installation_directory>\openapi\examples\elementreferencingintext.*

##### Creating text that refers an element

To create text that refers an element, use:

- ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String),
- *ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/elementreferenceintext/ElementReferencingInTexts.html#createReferencingText-com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.DisplayMode-)*,
- ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText (BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode, UpdateMode) **

```text

```

##### **Setting text with element references**

Some element properties support text with references, e.g., a comment body, constraint specification, opaque behavior body, etc. In order to set the text with element references, you must convert it using *com.nomagic.text.TextUtils.toLightHtml(java.lang.String)*.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Open API provides the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.ElementReferencingInTexts</em> class for creating text with element references, setting, and getting text from elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8115ff91-29d0-40a0-88a0-8ce2d270a13f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Examples</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling_tool_installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\elementreferencingintext.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating text that refers an element</h3><p>To create text that refers an element, use:</p><ul><li><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String), </em></li><li><em><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/elementreferenceintext/ElementReferencingInTexts.html#createReferencingText-com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.DisplayMode-">)</a></em>, </em></li><li><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText</em><em>(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode, UpdateMode)</em><br /><em><em><br /></em></em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73066c2a-f742-4f9f-9f82-237e888ddd4b"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//creates a text that references an element with default options
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link");

//creates a text that is updated automatically and is displayed using its representation text. 
//This means the elements representation text will be displayed rather than "Link"
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link", DisplayMode.REPRESENTATION_TEXT, 
UpdateMode.AUTOMATIC_UPDATE);

//create a referencing text with a provided display mode and with a default update mode
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link", DisplayMode.REPRESENTATION_TEXT);

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong>Setting text with element references</strong></h3><p>Some element properties support text with references, e.g., a comment body, constraint specification, opaque behavior body, etc. In order to set the text with element references, you must convert it using <em>com.nomagic.text.TextUtils.toLightHtml(java.lang.String)</em>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0b83594-eaad-44c1-b5ff-fc3d8884f6be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Setting text for comment body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//create a text with an element reference
String constructedText = "This text refers an element: " + createReferencingText(elementToRefer);
//convert it to a light html. Light html can be used in a comment body, constraint specification, etc. to show th etext containing references
String textForCommentBody = TextUtils.toLightHtml(constructedText);

Project project = Project.getProject(comment);
SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Set comment text with reference");
comment.setBody(textForCommentBody);
SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125803 space=DEVG version=25 -->
## PAGE 00627: Elements Utility Class

- page_id: `296125803`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/296125803/Elements+Utility+Class
- version_number: 25
- version_date: `2026-04-16T04:35:55.124+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

`[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html)` is a stateless utility class that provides the core operations for working with `[Element](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html)` instances in the KerML / SysML v2 metamodel.

As a developer, you will use this class whenever you need to:

- manage element ownership,
- navigate the containment hierarchy,
- traverse model trees, or
- safely manipulate model structure.

All methods are static — `[Elements](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html)` is never instantiated.

#### **Core Concept: Ownership in KerML**

Every `[Element](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html)` in KerML participates in a containment tree and is connected to its parent through a membership relationship.

Different kinds of elements are owned through different membership types:

Elements under a [Namespace](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Namespace.html) or [Package](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Package.html) use `[OwningMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html)` 
Features (such as attributes or part usages) under a definition use `[FeatureMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureMembership.html)`

This ownership structure forms a strict tree rooted at a top-level namespace, where each element has exactly one logical owner.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Ownership Navigation**

*[findOwner()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#findOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,java.util.function.Predicate))*walks up the ownership chain from a given element until it finds an ancestor that matches a predicate. If no such ancestor exists, it returns null.

```java
e instanceof Package);]]>
```

This is the primary API for contextual queries, such as:

- “Which package contains this element?”
- “What definition owns this feature?”

#### **Establishing Ownership**

Ownership is established through membership relationships using *[setOwningMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))*.

#### **Preferred Mode (Recommended)**

```java

```

This is the default and recommended way to assign ownership.

Resolution follows these rules:

1. If the element already has a valid membership and no preference is given → reuse it
2. If a preferred EClass is provided → use it only if it is compatible and not less specific
3. If no valid membership can be determined → fall back to the default membership type for the namespace

```java

```

This mode allows the framework to select the most appropriate membership while respecting your intent.

#### **Exact Mode**

```java

```

Passing false enforces the exact membership type. No adaptation or fallback occurs.

*[setOwningMembership(member, namespace, membershipEClass, preferredMembershipClass)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass,boolean))* — exact mode

Pass false for the last parameter to enforce an exact membership kind rather than treating it as a preference. The framework will not downgrade or upgrade the membership type.

```java

```

Use this mode when:

- The membership type must be strictly controlled
- You want validation instead of automatic correction

```java

```

This method directly assigns the owning element without resolving or creating a membership relationship.

```java

```

Use this only when:

- You need raw containment control, or
- Membership semantics are handled elsewhere

For most use cases, prefer *[setOwningMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))*.

#### **Tree Traversal**

The traversal APIs enable systematic processing of entire model subtrees, and are foundational for:

- validation
- transformations
- analysis
- export pipelines

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*[consumeRecursively(Element element, Consumer<Element> consumer)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#consumeRecursively(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,java.util.function.Consumer))* and *[consumeRecursively(Collection<? extends Element>, Consumer<Element>)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#consumeRecursively(java.util.Collection,java.util.function.Consumer))*

Performs a depth-first traversal starting from element (or each element in the collection), invoking consumer on every node visited. There is no way to abort early — every element in the subtree is visited unconditionally.

```java
names = new ArrayList<>();
Elements.consumeRecursively(rootPackage, e -> {
    if (e.getDeclaredName() != null) {
        names.add(e.getQualifiedName());
    }
});

// Same call starting from a collection of roots
Elements.consumeRecursively(List.of(packageA, vehiclePartDef), e -> validate(e));]]>
```

*[applyRecursively(Element element, Function<Element, Boolean> function)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#applyRecursively(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,java.util.function.Function))*behaves**like *[consumeRecursively()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#consumeRecursively(java.util.Collection,java.util.function.Consumer))*, but the function returns a *Boolean*. Returning false stops descent into that element's children — the element itself is still visited, but its subtree is skipped. Returning true continues normally.

```java
{
    process(e);
    if (e instanceof PartDefinition pd && pd.isAbstract()) {
        return false; // do not recurse into abstract definitions
    }
    return true;
});]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html" rel="nofollow">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements</a></code> is a stateless utility class that provides the core operations for working with <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html" rel="nofollow">Element</a></code> instances in the KerML / SysML v2 metamodel.</p><p>As a developer, you will use this class whenever you need to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e8a74c3b-2542-42b3-81b4-c4ea221af065">manage element ownership,</li><li data-uuid="26dacd6e-6a17-44e4-b29c-4c0428663e3f">navigate the containment hierarchy,</li><li data-uuid="a54f27ec-996d-4dd9-a407-5c3563d0121c">traverse model trees, or</li><li data-uuid="c7dca3a0-49c7-419e-a9ea-8834bf9ecfed">safely manipulate model structure.</li></ul><p>All methods are static — <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html" rel="nofollow">Elements</a></code> is never instantiated.</p><h2><strong>Core Concept: Ownership in KerML</strong></h2><p>Every <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html" rel="nofollow">Element</a></code> in KerML participates in a containment tree and is connected to its parent through a membership relationship.</p><p>Different kinds of elements are owned through different membership types:</p><p>Elements under a <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Namespace.html">Namespace</a> or <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Package.html">Package</a> use <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html" rel="nofollow">OwningMembership</a></code><br />Features (such as attributes or part usages) under a definition use <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureMembership.html" rel="nofollow">FeatureMembership</a></code></p><p>This ownership structure forms a strict tree rooted at a top-level namespace, where each element has exactly one logical owner.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-3-27_18-21-35.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h2><strong>Ownership Navigation</strong></h2><p><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#findOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,java.util.function.Predicate)">findOwner()</a> </em>walks up the ownership chain from a given element until it finds an ancestor that matches a predicate. If no such ancestor exists, it returns null.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95f2e573-e3d3-423b-a75e-d9f6b7eab424"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;

// Find the nearest enclosing Package ancestor
Element owningPackage = Elements.findOwner(massAttribute, e -> e instanceof Package);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This is the primary API for contextual queries, such as:</p><ul><li data-uuid="80e07cdd-9ad0-48bc-892f-2dc2f3e88fc5">“Which package contains this element?”</li><li data-uuid="c27acae6-2dbe-4960-8fc9-fdbb59bb46bd">“What definition owns this feature?”</li></ul><h2><strong>Establishing Ownership</strong></h2><p>Ownership is established through membership relationships using <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">setOwningMembership</a></em>.</p><h2><strong>Preferred Mode (Recommended)</strong></h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa87205a-414e-4c8b-8b75-f679cb04eb80"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;

Elements.setOwningMembership(member, namespace, preferredMembershipEClass)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This is the default and recommended way to assign ownership.</p><p>Resolution follows these rules:</p><ol><li data-uuid="f26107f8-6df3-4378-8b1e-ae07ec43757a">If the element already has a valid membership and no preference is given → reuse it</li><li data-uuid="6cafc0d3-f3e1-4acf-8e88-913b6ebe6daa">If a preferred EClass is provided → use it only if it is compatible and not less specific</li><li data-uuid="ffdb8505-ac8f-41f3-b23a-c8e6838d9939">If no valid membership can be determined → fall back to the default membership type for the namespace</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8cb71108-af17-4bad-8cf0-7fab07c4dca4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;

// Place an AttributeUsage inside a PartDef (prefer FeatureMembership)
Elements.setOwningMembership(
    massAttribute,
    vehiclePartDef,
    KerMLPackage.Literals.FEATURE_MEMBERSHIP
);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This mode allows the framework to select the most appropriate membership while respecting your intent.</p><h2><strong>Exact Mode</strong></h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5e444aa-0dc2-44f3-ba13-322ca5a77482"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;

Elements.setOwningMembership(member, namespace, membershipEClass, false)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Passing false enforces the exact membership type. No adaptation or fallback occurs.</p><p><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass,boolean)">setOwningMembership(member, namespace, membershipEClass, preferredMembershipClass)</a></em> — exact mode</p><p>Pass false for the last parameter to enforce an exact membership kind rather than treating it as a preference. The framework will not downgrade or upgrade the membership type.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e03f7993-bcae-44f0-9840-0246626642b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.FeatureMembership;

// Enforce FeatureMembership exactly — returns null if not applicable
FeatureMembership fm = Elements.setOwningMembership(
    radiusAttribute,
    wheelDef,
    KerMLPackage.Literals.FEATURE_MEMBERSHIP,
    false // exact mode
);

if (fm == null) {
    // FeatureMembership is not valid for this element/namespace pair
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Use this mode when:</p><ul><li data-uuid="0edb5460-1872-48d4-90d7-eb3da6fabac1">The membership type must be strictly controlled</li><li data-uuid="e45c82f7-5538-4ba2-bc79-9bd846577010">You want validation instead of automatic correction</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09f1be86-fbeb-4931-8b94-0e9a7df8a703"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;

Elements.setOwningElement(Element element, @CheckForNull Element owning)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This method directly assigns the owning element without resolving or creating a membership relationship.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75ce7734-881c-43f9-be17-5e4d37166b6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;

Elements.setOwningElement(derivedFeature, parentDefinition);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Use this only when:</p><ul><li data-uuid="784e32a9-0283-47a8-a713-8f95f53a5f61">You need raw containment control, or</li><li data-uuid="0c67289a-8d32-41a4-9dc2-e54e95d40fd1">Membership semantics are handled elsewhere</li></ul><p>For most use cases, prefer <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">setOwningMembership</a></em>.</p><h2><strong>Tree Traversal</strong></h2><p>The traversal APIs enable systematic processing of entire model subtrees, and are foundational for:</p><ul><li data-uuid="cb9da415-e831-41e4-a5ae-e41d7790d34a">validation</li><li data-uuid="1846ddde-aca2-41ab-8df1-8de98bf1b19e">transformations</li><li data-uuid="fe5f0b77-5113-4958-9709-3228e5217534">analysis</li><li data-uuid="9032aec4-8210-4235-b3b6-c11a98cbca35">export pipelines</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-3-27_18-22-0.png" /></ac:image></p><p><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#consumeRecursively(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,java.util.function.Consumer)">consumeRecursively(Element element, Consumer&lt;Element&gt; consumer)</a></em> and <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#consumeRecursively(java.util.Collection,java.util.function.Consumer)">consumeRecursively(Collection&lt;? extends Element&gt;, Consumer&lt;Element&gt;)</a></em></p><p>Performs a depth-first traversal starting from element (or each element in the collection), invoking consumer on every node visited. There is no way to abort early — every element in the subtree is visited unconditionally.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b837551c-279f-4e5a-8b95-5d0d9019bf09"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;

// Collect all qualified names in a subtree
List<String> names = new ArrayList<>();
Elements.consumeRecursively(rootPackage, e -> {
    if (e.getDeclaredName() != null) {
        names.add(e.getQualifiedName());
    }
});

// Same call starting from a collection of roots
Elements.consumeRecursively(List.of(packageA, vehiclePartDef), e -> validate(e));]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#applyRecursively(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,java.util.function.Function)">applyRecursively(Element element, Function&lt;Element, Boolean&gt; function)</a> </em>behaves<em> </em>like <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#consumeRecursively(java.util.Collection,java.util.function.Consumer)">consumeRecursively()</a></em>, but the function returns a <em>Boolean</em>. Returning false stops descent into that element's children — the element itself is still visited, but its subtree is skipped. Returning true continues normally.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e78558b8-780a-4dce-8eca-9d0c4bb918ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;

// Visit all elements but skip subtrees under PartDefs that are abstract
Elements.applyRecursively(rootPackage, e -> {
    process(e);
    if (e instanceof PartDefinition pd && pd.isAbstract()) {
        return false; // do not recurse into abstract definitions
    }
    return true;
});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573648 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00628: Engine

- page_id: `249573648`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573648/Engine
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-09T16:47:41.571+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1 > Alf Plugin Developer Guide > Cameo Simulation Toolkit > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

730383780

730383782

730383781

ExecutionEngine defines how a Behavior e.g. State Machine is executed, including the full logic how their inner elements (e.g. States) are activated. 
Implementing custom ExecutionEngine and registering it's ExecutionEngineDescriptor enables overriding the default behavior of the Cameo Simulation Toolkit for specific: 
- StateMachine 
- Interaction 
- Activity

**Related pages:**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180514 space=DEVG version=12 -->
## PAGE 00629: Environment Initialization

- page_id: `314180514`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/314180514/Environment+Initialization
- version_number: 12
- version_date: `2026-06-18T20:17:54.265+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Python Integration Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to configure the Python runtime environment required to execute Python scripts.

This setup is a **one-time prerequisite** before running any Python scripts that interact with the modeling tool.

#### Prerequisites

Before setting up the environment, ensure the following are installed:

| Requirement | Notes |
| --- | --- |
| Python 3.10 or later | Must be available on system PATH |

#### Locating the Python example files

After installing the SysML v2 plugin, the Python Open API example is available in the modeling tool installation directory:

```text
/openapi/python/
]]>
```

Locate the Python example archive in the directory above and extract it to a writable location.

#### Setting up the Python environment

Run the following bootstrap script to configure the environment automatically.

```bash

```

#### PANEL: Critical execution rule

solid

Critical execution rule

All scripts must be executed using the Python interpreter inside the .venv environment.

Running scripts outside the virtual environment may cause runtime errors due to missing dependencies or JVM linkage issues.

#### Summary

```text
/openapi/python
2. Extract archive → local directory
3. Run bootstrap_virtual_environment.py]]>
```

#### Troubleshooting Tip

##### Application Crash on macOS

If the application crashes during startup, verify that the virtual environment was created using the Python installation from

```java

```

and not a Homebrew-managed Python installation

```java

```

as Homebrew Python may cause compatibility issues with JPype and native libraries.

On some macOS systems, Python 3.14 may crash even when using the system Python.

If this occurs:

Downgrade to Python 3.10 
Recreate the virtual environment using Python 3.10

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Locate the Python example archive in the directory above and extract it to a writable location.</p><h2>Setting up the Python environment</h2><p>Run the following bootstrap script to configure the environment automatically.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34795bc5-f7cb-4e32-a86d-71ba4669edf0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[python bootstrap_virtual_environment.py
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e2da686-a4cb-491f-9a0b-6e942b6ae218"><ac:parameter ac:name="borderStyle">solid</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Critical execution rule</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>All scripts must be executed using the Python interpreter inside the .venv environment.</p><p>Running scripts outside the virtual environment may cause runtime errors due to missing dependencies or JVM linkage issues.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Summary</h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="935d1f12-9108-49fa-8a54-18c232e8c210"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[1. Locate example → <tool.install.dir>/openapi/python
2. Extract archive → local directory
3. Run bootstrap_virtual_environment.py]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Troubleshooting Tip</h2><h3>Application Crash on macOS</h3><p>If the application crashes during startup, verify that the virtual environment was created using the Python installation from</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="095cd3ca-4117-4cff-b602-90b0d06844f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[/usr/local/bin/python3]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>and not a Homebrew-managed Python installation  </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1056108-3547-4fcc-bc64-61bb251b780d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[/opt/homebrew/bin/python3]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> as Homebrew Python may cause compatibility issues with JPype and native libraries. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f0b6ac8-799d-4d44-8c43-b9957b05a2a5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>On some macOS systems, Python 3.14 may crash even when using the system Python.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If this occurs:</p><p>Downgrade to Python 3.10<br />Recreate the virtual environment using Python 3.10</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573544 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00630: Environment Options

- page_id: `249573544`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573544/Environment+Options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:53:28.018+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Application-related options are referred as environment options. If you are using MagicDraw, they are saved in the *global.opt*file that is located in*<user home directory>\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\<version number>\data.*You can find this file for other modeling tools using the same location pattern.

You can add custom environment options for your modeling tool.

To add your own environment options

1. Extend the com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup class.
2. Add the extending class to application environment options.

###### **Example of adding custom environment options**

```java

```

###### **Example of accessing environment options**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\environmentoptions

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Application-related options are referred as environment options. If you are using MagicDraw, they are saved in the <em>global.opt </em>file that is located in<em> &lt;user home directory&gt;\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\&lt;version number&gt;\data. </em>You can find this file for other modeling tools using the same location pattern. </p><p>You can add custom environment options for your modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add your own environment options</p><hr /><ol><li>Extend the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup</em> class.</li><li>Add the extending class to application environment options.</li></ol><h4><strong>Example of adding custom environment options</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92870dc5-f5fc-4dc4-a0e0-eecab4ec361b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	class MyOptionsGroup extends AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup
    {
        ...
    }
    Application application = Application.getInstance(); 
    EnvironmentOptions options = application.getEnvironmentOptions(); 
    options.addGroup(new MyOptionsGroup());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example of accessing environment options</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3e6aa9b-33ff-47cc-bd74-07c542fc3afb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Application application = Application.getInstance(); 
	EnvironmentOptions options = application.getEnvironmentOptions(); 
	int imageDpi = options.getGeneralOptions().getImageResolutionDpi();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\environmentoptions</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573439 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00631: Event support

- page_id: `249573439`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573439/Event+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:56.822+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to events while changing a model. There is a possibility either to get an event immediately after the property has been changed, or get the event about all the changes in the session, after this session has been closed.

There are four different listener registration types:

- The w hole repository listener. You will get events about the changes in all the elements.
- The specific element listener. You will get events about the changes in any property of this element.
- The e lement’s specific property listener. You will get events about the changes of this property.
- The s pecific element type listener. You will get events about the changes in all the elements of the specific metatype.

The transaction listener is notified, when all the changes within the session are done and the session is closed.

You can find the code examples in *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\events*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to events while changing a model. There is a possibility either to get an event immediately after the property has been changed, or get the event about all the changes in the session, after this session has been closed.</p><p>There are four different listener registration types:</p><ul><li><strong>The w</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">hole repository listener. </strong>You will get events about the changes in all the elements.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The specific element listener. </strong>You will get events about the changes in any property of this element.</li><li><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The e</span></strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">lement’s specific property listener. </strong>You will get events about the changes of this property.</li><li><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The s</span></strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">pecific element type listener. </strong>You will get events about the changes in all the elements of the specific metatype.</li></ul><p>The transaction listener is notified, when all the changes within the session are done and the session is closed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48621266-8f0c-4295-8e01-7b9597d76cbe"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\events</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4c86826-2597-4896-87f5-24fa8306eafa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="44cd2443-8cac-4efc-80ef-0eddee8e5215"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573596 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00632: Example

- page_id: `249573596`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573596/Example
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:11.912+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Oracle DDL generation and customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this sample, we will extend the current Oracle View DROP statement. In the Default template, we have the Oracle view drop function. In this sample, the simple macro is presented, and it generates the following text:

```text

```

See the following sample:

```text

```

We will add a new tag to identify the “CASCADE CONSTRAINTS” clause in the DROP VIEW statement. The following script will be generated:

```text

```

There are three steps to do this:

###### **Step #1. Creating a new stereotype and tags**

We need to create a new stereotype with the Boolean property, or extend the default “View” stereotype with a new property. Let's name a new property “cascade_clause”, apply the stereotype to the View object, and set value to *true*.

We added a new tag to the view stereotype in the default profile.

###### ****

###### **Step #2. Changing the template file**

Add the following lines to the template file:

```text

```

This line retrieves a boolean value from the given tag in the given stereotype “$view_stereotype” and sets it to a newly created $cascadeOption value.

Then add a new checking clause into the drop statement. See the final method:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>In this sample, we will extend the current Oracle View DROP statement. In the Default template, we have the Oracle view drop function. In this sample, the simple macro is presented, and it generates the following text:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff98097b-ec0f-47e5-85e0-4b8dd4de7aab"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    DROP VIEW view_name;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>See the following sample:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd214291-a8a7-4b6a-ac42-7b667c2851ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #macro (dropView $data )
    #writeLine( "DROP VIEW $utils.getQualifiedName( $data )${semicolon}" $nospace)
    #end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>We will add a new tag to identify the “CASCADE CONSTRAINTS” clause in the DROP VIEW statement. The following script will be generated:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a21e59ae-3cde-48a1-9ae4-018ec906ba60"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 	DROP VIEW view_name CASCADE CONSTRAINTS;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>There are three steps to do this:</p><h4><strong>Step #1. Creating a new stereotype and tags</strong></h4><p>We need to create a new stereotype with the Boolean property, or extend the default “View” stereotype with a new property. Let's name a new property “cascade_clause”, apply the stereotype to the View object, and set value to <em>true</em>.</p><p>We added a new tag to the view stereotype in the default profile.</p><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></h4><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Step #2. Changing the template file</strong></h4><p>Add the following lines to the template file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="294b4f13-dfa4-4d76-a9ed-8674cea596bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #set($cascadeOption =false)
    ##this line is required to for setting a new variable to the false state.
    #set($cascadeOption = $oracleHelper.getBooleanValueFromDefaultProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, "cascade_clause") )]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>This line retrieves a boolean value from the given tag in the given stereotype “$view_stereotype” and sets it to a newly created $cascadeOption value.</p><p>Then add a new checking clause into the drop statement. See the final method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="028221c4-31db-4949-ab08-f30891c9f943"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #macro (dropView $data )
    ## sets a value to a new variable
    #set($cascadeOption = $oracleHelper.getBooleanValueFromDefaultProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, "cascade_clause") )
    #writeLine( "DROP VIEW $utils.getQualifiedName( $data )
    #if ($cascadeOption) CASCADE CONSTRAINTS
    #end${semicolon}" $nospace)
    #end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573639 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00633: Execution

- page_id: `249573639`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573639/Execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.317+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1 > Alf Plugin Developer Guide > Cameo Simulation Toolkit > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

189375002

189375005

189375003

A simulation execution represents a model execution of a specific element. It is the top level of the model execution which contains running execution sessions and execution engines. A simulation execution will start when the specific element is executed (through the graphical user interface or Java API). The following APIs are available for handling executions.

- Start Execution
- Stop Execution
- Creating and Registering a New Execution Listener

189375000

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
