````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="310390e7-6a10-43be-b02d-6932fc85126d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">189375002</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62b6012c-ddf2-47cb-acf8-5737e4da53c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">189375005</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aabe498b-ca57-41df-806e-49eb53e67389"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">189375003</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A simulation execution represents a model execution of a specific element. It is the top level of the model execution which contains running execution sessions and execution engines. A simulation execution will start when the specific element is executed (through the graphical user interface or Java API). The following APIs are available for handling executions.</p><ul><li>Start Execution</li><li>Stop Execution</li><li>Creating and Registering a New Execution Listener</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a4d73df-5fe5-450a-9d47-db9a2e2daedb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">189375000</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="64cdf2d4-a520-4580-b651-6ca415e0180c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298090534 space=DEVG version=29 -->
## PAGE 00634: FeatureTypings Utility Class

- page_id: `298090534`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/298090534/FeatureTypings+Utility+Class
- version_number: 29
- version_date: `2026-04-16T05:19:30.744+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In KerML, `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.FeatureTyping](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html)` is a [Relationship](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Relationship.html)that binds a `[Feature](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html)` to a `[Type](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html)`, thereby constraining the set of values that the feature may hold.

`[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureTypings](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html)` is the stateless utility class through which all programmatic feature-typing operations should be performed.

A key concept throughout this API is the distinction between owned and implied `[FeatureTyping](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html)` relationships. Understanding this distinction is essential for using the methods correctly.

**Setting Types**

`[setType(Feature, Type)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#setType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))` replaces all existing owned typings of the given feature with a single new `[FeatureTyping](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html)` that points to the specified type.

Passing *null* removes all owned typings, leaving the feature either untyped or typed only through implied relationships.

```java

```

**Adding Types**

`[addType(Feature, Type)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#addType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))` adds a new [FeatureTyping](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html) to the feature’s existing owned typings without modifying the others.

Use this when the feature must be typed incrementally, for example, during a programmatic model transformation that enriches a partially typed feature.

```java

```

**Reading Types**

`[getOwnedNotImpliedTypes()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#getOwnedNotImpliedTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))` returns the list of `[Type](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html)` instances reachable only through owned, non-implied `[FeatureTyping](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html)` relationships.

```java
explicit = FeatureTypings.getOwnedNotImpliedTypes(massAttr);
// For "mass : Real, MassUnit" → [Real, MassUnit]
// Does not include types inferred through redefinition]]>
```

`[getOwnedNotImpliedTypes()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#getOwnedNotImpliedTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))` returns the list of [Type](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html) instances reachable only through owned, non-implied [FeatureTyping](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html) relationships.

This is the most precise API for answering the question: “What types did the modeler explicitly assign to this feature?” That is also the set of types rendered in textual notation.

```java
all = FeatureTypings.getOwnedTypes(massAttr);
// Includes types from explicit owned FeatureTypings
// and implied owned FeatureTypings (for example, from Redefinition)]]>
```

**Typing a feature at creation time**

A feature is often typed immediately after creation.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">In KerML, <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html" rel="nofollow">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.FeatureTyping</a></code> is a <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Relationship.html">Relationship </a>that binds a <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html" rel="nofollow">Feature</a></code> to a <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html" rel="nofollow">Type</a></code>, thereby constraining the set of values that the feature may hold.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html" rel="nofollow">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureTypings</a></code> is the stateless utility class through which all programmatic feature-typing operations should be performed.</p><p style="text-align: left;">A key concept throughout this API is the distinction between owned and implied <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html" rel="nofollow">FeatureTyping</a></code> relationships. Understanding this distinction is essential for using the methods correctly.</p><p><strong>Setting Types<br /></strong></p><p><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#setType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)" rel="nofollow">setType(Feature, Type)</a></code> replaces all existing owned typings of the given feature with a single new <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html" rel="nofollow">FeatureTyping</a></code> that points to the specified type.</p><p>Passing <em>null</em> removes all owned typings, leaving the feature either untyped or typed only through implied relationships.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43f0e555-2169-423b-a93e-1f17edf97ee4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureTypings;

// Type mass as Real — removes any previously owned typings
FeatureTypings.setType(massAttr, realType);

// Remove all owned typings from a feature
FeatureTypings.setType(massAttr, null);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Adding Types</strong></p><p><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#addType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)" rel="nofollow">addType(Feature, Type)</a></code> adds a new <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html">FeatureTyping</a> to the feature’s existing owned typings without modifying the others.</p><p>Use this when the feature must be typed incrementally, for example, during a programmatic model transformation that enriches a partially typed feature.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b3aab4c-31fb-4220-82a0-7308744574c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureTypings;

// mass is already typed as Real; add MassUnit to form an intersection
FeatureTypings.addType(massAttr, massUnitType);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Reading Types</strong></p><p><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#getOwnedNotImpliedTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)" rel="nofollow">getOwnedNotImpliedTypes()</a></code> returns the list of <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html" rel="nofollow">Type</a></code> instances reachable only through owned, non-implied <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html" rel="nofollow">FeatureTyping</a></code> relationships.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86a4fc65-3a39-48a8-b26d-af7ce6b52ac0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureTypings;

List<Type> explicit = FeatureTypings.getOwnedNotImpliedTypes(massAttr);
// For "mass : Real, MassUnit" → [Real, MassUnit]
// Does not include types inferred through redefinition]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#getOwnedNotImpliedTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)" rel="nofollow">getOwnedNotImpliedTypes()</a></code> returns the list of <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html">Type</a> instances reachable only through owned, non-implied <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureTyping.html">FeatureTyping</a> relationships.</p><p>This is the most precise API for answering the question: “What types did the modeler explicitly assign to this feature?” That is also the set of types rendered in textual notation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b04c4d45-79d6-4706-b7b3-4c0130ecb901"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureTypings;

List<Type> all = FeatureTypings.getOwnedTypes(massAttr);
// Includes types from explicit owned FeatureTypings
// and implied owned FeatureTypings (for example, from Redefinition)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Typing a feature at creation time</strong></p><p>A feature is often typed immediately after creation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="affd57cf-c76e-47f5-9761-425e5a52466f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureTypings;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.KerMLPackage;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.AttributeUsage;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.SysMLFactory;

// Create an attribute usage owned by vehiclePart
AttributeUsage massAttr = SysMLFactory.eINSTANCE.createAttributeUsage();
massAttr.setDeclaredName("mass");
Elements.setOwningMembership(
    massAttr,
    vehiclePart,
    KerMLPackage.Literals.FEATURE_MEMBERSHIP
);

// Assign the type — equivalent to "mass : Real" in SysML v2 notation
FeatureTypings.setType(massAttr, realType);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298095529 space=DEVG version=11 -->
## PAGE 00635: FeatureValues Utility Class

- page_id: `298095529`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/298095529/FeatureValues+Utility+Class
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2026-04-16T05:29:42.759+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A **[FeatureValue](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html)** represents a relationship that binds a **[Feature](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html)** to an **[Expression](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Expression.html)** describing its value.

A feature may have:

- Owned feature values
- Inherited feature values
- Redefined feature values
- Initial or default values

The *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureValues](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html)*utility class provides a unified API for **reading, writing, and resolving feature values** in KerML models.

[Feature](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html) values represent assigned values such as:

- Default values
- Initial values
- Redefined values
- Explicit assignments

This utility should be the primary entry point when working with feature values in the Open API.

#### **Retrieving Feature Values**

##### Get Owned Feature Value

Returns the **[FeatureValue](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html) directly owned** by the feature.

Use this when you only care about explicitly assigned values on the feature itself.

```text

```

##### Check if Feature Has Value

```text

```

##### Check for Binding Feature Value

Returns true only if the feature has a **non-initial and non-default** value.

```text

```

##### Check for Initial Value

```text

```

#### **Resolving Effective Values**

##### Get Effective Value Expression

Returns the **effective value [expression](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Expression.html)**, taking into account:

- The feature itself
- Redefining features
- Inherited values

This is the recommended method when reading values in most cases.

```text

```

##### Get Owned Value Expression

Returns the value [expression](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Expression.html) **directly assigned** to the feature.

```text

```

#### **Setting Feature Values**

##### Set Value via [Redefinition](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html)

Creates or reuses a **redefining feature** inside the given type and assigns a value.

This is the correct approach when specializing or overriding values.

```text

```

##### Set Value with Custom Redefining Feature Type

Allows specifying a custom EClass for the redefining feature.

```text
MyCustomFeatureEClass,
        "Hello"
    );
]]>
```

##### Set Value Directly on Feature

Creates, updates, or removes the owned [FeatureValue](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html).

```text

```

Notes:

- If the value is not an [Expression](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Expression.html) , it will be converted automatically
- Existing expressions are safely replaced
- Passing null removes the FeatureValue

#### **Applicability**

##### Check if Feature Can Have Value

Determines whether a [FeatureValue](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html) relationship is allowed for the feature.

```text

```

#### **Summary**

The *[FeatureValues](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html)*utility class provides a complete API for:

- Retrieving owned and effective values
- Resolving inherited and redefined values
- Creating and updating feature values
- Managing value lifecycle safely

Use:

- [getValueExpression()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html#getValueExpression(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)) for reading effective values
- [setValue(type, feature, value)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html#setValue(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,java.lang.Object)) for specialization scenarios
- [setValue(feature, value)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html#setValue(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,java.lang.Object)) for direct assignments

This ensures correct handling of **inheritance, redefinition, and expression lifecycle** in SysML v2 models.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html">FeatureValue</a></strong> represents a relationship that binds a <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html">Feature</a></strong> to an <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Expression.html">Expression</a></strong> describing its value.</p><p>A feature may have:</p><ul><li>Owned feature values</li><li>Inherited feature values</li><li>Redefined feature values</li><li>Initial or default values</li></ul><p>The <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureValues</a> </em>utility class provides a unified API for <strong>reading, writing, and resolving feature values</strong> in KerML models.</p><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html">Feature</a> values represent assigned values such as:</p><ul><li>Default values</li><li>Initial values</li><li>Redefined values</li><li>Explicit assignments</li></ul><p>This utility should be the primary entry point when working with feature values in the Open API.</p><h2><strong>Retrieving Feature Values</strong></h2><h3>Get Owned Feature Value</h3><p>Returns the <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html">FeatureValue</a> directly owned</strong> by the feature.</p><p>Use this when you only care about explicitly assigned values on the feature itself.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc9f3339-7d01-4187-8fcb-10ee958f3af1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Feature feature = ...;

FeatureValue fv = FeatureValues.getOwnedFeatureValue(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Check if Feature Has Value</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bef84652-16fc-404e-84c2-03c7f69fc641"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean hasValue = FeatureValues.hasOwnedFeatureValue(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Check for Binding Feature Value</h3><p>Returns true only if the feature has a <strong>non-initial and non-default</strong> value.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07a4de57-f237-4d39-944f-8f9fb010b8a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean hasBinding = FeatureValues.hasBindingFeatureValue(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Check for Initial Value</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccee40d9-fc24-4eb1-8e5b-af93185ea9ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean isInitial = FeatureValues.hasInitialFeatureValue(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Resolving Effective Values</strong></h2><h3>Get Effective Value Expression</h3><p>Returns the <strong>effective value <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Expression.html">expression</a></strong>, taking into account:</p><ul><li>The feature itself</li><li>Redefining features</li><li>Inherited values</li></ul><p>This is the recommended method when reading values in most cases.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60987ba6-62cc-422a-b0a5-287a0dfb9973"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Expression expr = FeatureValues.getValueExpression(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Get Owned Value Expression</h3><p>Returns the value <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Expression.html">expression</a> <strong>directly assigned</strong> to the feature.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25a11c1c-6573-4613-8ab7-b233ec297dc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Expression expr = FeatureValues.getOwnedFeatureValueExpression(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Setting Feature Values</strong></h2><h3>Set Value via <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html">Redefinition</a></h3><p>Creates or reuses a <strong>redefining feature</strong> inside the given type and assigns a value.</p><p>This is the correct approach when specializing or overriding values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="600a0aed-5fdf-4567-88eb-94cb339367d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Type myType = ...;
Feature baseFeature = ...;

FeatureValue fv = FeatureValues.setValue(myType, baseFeature, 42);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Set Value with Custom Redefining Feature Type</h3><p>Allows specifying a custom EClass for the redefining feature.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e571af36-b360-4860-989e-cfe5e2513ded"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[FeatureValue fv =
    FeatureValues.setValue(
        myType,
        baseFeature,
        () -> MyCustomFeatureEClass,
        "Hello"
    );
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Set Value Directly on Feature</h3><p>Creates, updates, or removes the owned <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html">FeatureValue</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf5af05a-e21b-4349-a5e0-06555f7cb6c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Feature feature = ...;

// set value
FeatureValue fv = FeatureValues.setValue(feature, 3.14);

// clear value
FeatureValues.setValue(feature, null);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Notes:</p><ul><li>If the value is not an <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Expression.html">Expression</a></em>, it will be converted automatically</li><li>Existing expressions are safely replaced</li><li>Passing <em>null </em>removes the <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html">FeatureValue</a></li></ul><h2><strong>Applicability</strong></h2><h3>Check if Feature Can Have Value</h3><p>Determines whether a <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/FeatureValue.html">FeatureValue</a> relationship is allowed for the feature.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fbb4d721-b869-4413-b8ab-429ea0d9e663"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean applicable = FeatureValues.isApplicable(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Summary</strong></h2><p>The <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html">FeatureValues</a> </em>utility class provides a complete API for:</p><ul><li>Retrieving owned and effective values</li><li>Resolving inherited and redefined values</li><li>Creating and updating feature values</li><li>Managing value lifecycle safely</li></ul><p>Use: </p><ul><li data-uuid="6bc1c34b-0609-4227-8f73-b97892df8a20"><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html#getValueExpression(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)">getValueExpression()</a></em> for reading effective values</li><li data-uuid="d68d93d0-9440-4f54-bee1-06466107ca37"><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html#setValue(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,java.lang.Object)">setValue(type, feature, value)</a> </em>for specialization scenarios</li><li data-uuid="34bc621a-915f-42b0-8a25-4ffc23ceeaef"><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureValues.html#setValue(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,java.lang.Object)">setValue(feature, value)</a></em> for direct assignments</li></ul><p>This ensures correct handling of <strong>inheritance, redefinition, and expression lifecycle</strong> in SysML v2 models.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298095384 space=DEVG version=19 -->
## PAGE 00636: Finding Elements

- page_id: `298095384`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/298095384/Finding+Elements
- version_number: 19
- version_date: `2026-06-30T05:32:43.152+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Finders provide reusable strategies for locating model elements within SysML v2 models.** Each strategy encapsulates a specific lookup mechanism—such as searching by name, qualified name, predicate, or metaclass—so you can choose the most appropriate approach for your use case.

The API supports both **local lookups** (within a given namespace) and **model-wide queries**, making it suitable for everything from simple navigation to more advanced analysis tasks.

Finders are accessed through static factory methods from the *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html)*

```java

```

**Finder Types**

**[byName](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byName())**

Searches for elements by **name** among the directly owned children of a given namespace. This finder is best suited for **local lookups** where names are expected to be unique within the owner.

The **name** represents the *effective (resolved) name* of an element:

- It may be derived or computed
- If declaredName is present → name == declaredName
- If declaredName is absent → the value may be resolved through other mechanisms
- Commonly used for general identification and display purposes

```java

```

```text
byDeclaredName
```

Searches for elements by their **declared name**—the original name explicitly assigned to the element at the point where it is defined.

Unlike `name`, the declared name is **not derived or computed**, making this finder suitable when you need to match the exact, user-defined identifier.

```java

```

**[byQualifiedName](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byQualifiedName())**

Resolves elements using a hierarchical path separated by `::`.

A **qualified name** represents the full path to an element within the model hierarchy, showing how it is nested inside its owning elements.

For example, an `Engine` element inside `Powertrain`, which is inside `Vehicle`, has the qualified name: 
`Vehicle::Powertrain::Engine`

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

```java

```

**[byPredicate](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byPredicate())**

Performs a flexible, condition-based lookup using a lambda predicate.

This finder allows you to define custom matching logic, making it useful for complex queries that cannot be expressed through standard lookup strategies such as name or type.

```java
p.isAbstract(), PartDefinition.class);]]>
```

**[byEClassRecursively](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byEClassRecursively())**

Performs a recursive traversal across model roots or an entire project, returning all elements that match a specified SysML v2 **EClass**.

Supports optional **predicate filtering** for additional constraints, as well as **exact-match semantics** to exclude subtype matches when needed.

```java
allParts = Finder.byEClassRecursively().find(project, SysMLPackage.eINSTANCE.getPartDefinition());]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Finders provide reusable strategies for locating model elements within SysML v2 models.</strong> Each strategy encapsulates a specific lookup mechanism—such as searching by name, qualified name, predicate, or metaclass—so you can choose the most appropriate approach for your use case.</p><p>The API supports both <strong>local lookups</strong> (within a given namespace) and <strong>model-wide queries</strong>, making it suitable for everything from simple navigation to more advanced analysis tasks.</p><p>Finders are accessed through static factory methods from the <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder</a></em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a24cc6fc-f0fa-4f1b-a2a6-1ceccc72cd70"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder;

Finder.byName();
Finder.byDeclaredName();
Finder.byQualifiedName();
Finder.byPredicate();
Finder.byEClassRecursively();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Finder Types</strong></p><p><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byName()">byName</a></strong></p><p>Searches for elements by <strong>name</strong> among the directly owned children of a given namespace. This finder is best suited for <strong>local lookups</strong> where names are expected to be unique within the owner.</p><p>The <strong>name</strong> represents the <em>effective (resolved) name</em> of an element:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d6afe2ec-dc18-4842-a751-784e5bfb8e2d">It may be <strong>derived or computed</strong></li><li data-uuid="92589fed-5afa-48c0-aa11-629495d62013">If <code>declaredName</code> is present → <code>name == declaredName</code></li><li data-uuid="b51c2a52-59bf-4a4f-906b-7f31d28b0b4e">If <code>declaredName</code> is absent → the value may be resolved through other mechanisms</li><li data-uuid="fde640e0-6326-4615-9541-d7f9f718565d">Commonly used for <strong>general identification and display purposes</strong></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51babab9-e7c6-44cb-9018-b45ccc3075f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;

// Find a PartDefinition named "Vehicle" in a package
PartDefinition vehicle = Finder.byName().find(packageElement, "Vehicle", PartDefinition.class);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byDeclaredName()">byDeclaredName</a></strong></pre><p>Searches for elements by their <strong>declared name</strong>—the original name explicitly assigned to the element at the point where it is defined.</p><p>Unlike <code>name</code>, the declared name is <strong>not derived or computed</strong>, making this finder suitable when you need to match the exact, user-defined identifier.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9bcd86c-5e1e-4998-9671-17385da6c5d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.AttributeDefinition;

// Find an AttributeDefinition by declared name
AttributeDefinition attr = Finder.byDeclaredName().find(packageElement, "maxSpeed", AttributeDefinition.class);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byQualifiedName()">byQualifiedName</a></strong></p><p>Resolves elements using a hierarchical path separated by <code>::</code>.</p><p>A <strong>qualified name</strong> represents the full path to an element within the model hierarchy, showing how it is nested inside its owning elements.</p><p>For example, an <code>Engine</code> element inside <code>Powertrain</code>, which is inside <code>Vehicle</code>, has the qualified name:<br /><code>Vehicle::Powertrain::Engine</code></p><p><ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-4-6_16-5-28.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b667cae2-920b-4910-b091-97292a52bc07"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;

// Resolve a nested PartDefinition
PartDefinition engine = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(model, "Vehicle::Powertrain::Engine", PartDefinition.class);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byPredicate()">byPredicate</a></strong></p><p>Performs a flexible, condition-based lookup using a lambda predicate.</p><p>This finder allows you to define custom matching logic, making it useful for complex queries that cannot be expressed through standard lookup strategies such as name or type.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05c040c9-8405-4ce8-b18a-d4f060b2f1ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;

// Find the first abstract PartDefinition
PartDefinition abstractPart = Finder.byPredicate().find(packageElement, p -> p.isAbstract(), PartDefinition.class);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/find/Finder.html#byEClassRecursively()">byEClassRecursively</a></strong></p><p>Performs a recursive traversal across model roots or an entire project, returning all elements that match a specified SysML v2 <strong>EClass</strong>.</p><p>Supports optional <strong>predicate filtering</strong> for additional constraints, as well as <strong>exact-match semantics</strong> to exclude subtype matches when needed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27106553-bafa-4063-a013-ec5642564350"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.find.Finder;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.SysMLPackage;

// Collect every PartDefinition across the project
Stream<PartDefinition> allParts = Finder.byEClassRecursively().find(project, SysMLPackage.eINSTANCE.getPartDefinition());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573426 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00637: Finding elements by name or by meta-type

- page_id: `249573426`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573426/Finding+elements+by+name+or+by+meta-type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:45:46.742+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder* for finding elements by the name or type (meta-type) in a project. This utility class provides several internal finders for executing different types of search operations.

For example:

```java
models = Finder.byNameAllRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");]]>
```

```text

```

**Finding or creating Classifiers**

A special utility class is available for search or creating Classifiers by metatype, simple or qualified name - *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassifierFinder*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Retrieving meta information about element' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder</em> for finding elements by the name or type (meta-type) in a project. This utility class provides several internal finders for executing different types of search operations.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fc8c8b9-aa83-401a-a514-2f31e191100e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   Project project = ...;
   //find a Model element under the project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is not a recursive search
   Model model = Finder.byName().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");
   //find a first Model element under project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is a recursive search.
   Model model = Finder.byNameRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");
   //find all Model elements under project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is a recursive search.
   Collection<Model> models = Finder.byNameAllRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre><br /></pre><p><strong>Finding or creating Classifiers</strong></p><p>A special utility class is available for search or creating Classifiers by metatype, simple or qualified name - <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassifierFinder</em></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f3df531-9a85-4f43-a56f-cbcb8d51f363"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Retrieving meta information about element" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306283255 space=DEVG version=16 -->
## PAGE 00638: Finding Symbols in Diagrams

- page_id: `306283255`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/306283255/Finding+Symbols+in+Diagrams
- version_number: 16
- version_date: `2026-05-13T21:32:28.090+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Managing Presentation Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to find symbols in a loaded diagram by using the [findPresentationElement](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Diagram.html#findPresentationElement(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.model.ModelElement,java.lang.Class))method. A symbol is represented by a **[PresentationElement](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/PresentationElement.html)**.

#### Overview

Use the following method to find the symbol for a model element:

```text

```

Where:

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| modelElement | The SysML v2 model element shown in the diagram. |
| symbolClass | The expected presentation element class or null |

The method returns the symbol if it is found. If the symbol is not shown in the diagram, the method returns **null**.

#### Common symbol classes

| Symbol | Class |
| --- | --- |
| Package Shape | PackageShape.class |
| Shape | FeatureShape.class / ClassifierShape.class |
| Border shape | FeatureOnBorderShape.class |
| Relationship path | ConnectorPath.class |

#### Example diagram structure

Let's use a diagram from the RescueDrone example to find symbols in it:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Finding an element on the diagram

The **context** element is shown as the main shape on the diagram.

```text

```

If the symbol is found, **contextShape** contains the presentation element for the **context** model element.

#### Finding a symbol inside another symbol

The **environment** element is displayed inside the **context** shape.

```text

```

The same method is used even when the symbol is visually inside another shape.

#### Finding a nested element

The **locatePerson_degradeLink** perform action usage is nested deeper in the diagram.

```text

```

Use **[FeatureShape.class](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/FeatureShape.html)** because the expected symbol is a shape.

#### Finding a Feature on the Border shape

Some features can be shown on the border of the other symbols; they can be displayed as **[FeatureShape](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/FeatureShape.html)** or FeatureOnBorderShape in the same diagram. by mentioning the excet class you will get the right symbol

```text

```

In this example,**operatorP1Port**is shown as a port on the**operator**shape.

```text

```

Use **[FeatureOnBorderShape.class](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/FeatureOnBorderShape.html)** when the expected symbol is located on the border of a shape.

#### Finding a connector

Connectors are shown as paths between symbols.

In this example, **environmentToOperator** is shown as a connector path.

```text

```

Use **[ConnectorPath.class](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/ConnectorPath.html)** when the expected symbol is a connector line.

#### Important notes

#### NOTE: Important

Important

Use **findPresentationElement** for symbols that are:

- directly on the diagram
- inside another shape
- nested deeper inside another nested shape
- located on the border of a shape
- shown as a relationship path

#### Summary

The diagram must already be loaded, and the model element must be shown in that diagram. If the symbol is not shown, the method returns **null**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page describes how to find symbols in a loaded diagram by using the <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Diagram.html#findPresentationElement(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.model.ModelElement,java.lang.Class)"> findPresentationElement </a>method. A symbol is represented by a <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/PresentationElement.html">PresentationElement</a></strong>.</p><h2>Overview</h2><p>Use the following method to find the symbol for a model element:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="064f70f6-e722-47dc-af50-6c4baa79926f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[PresentationElement symbol = diagram.findPresentationElement(modelElement, symbolClass);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where:</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p>Parameter</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>modelElement</strong></p></td><td><p>The SysML v2 model element shown in the diagram.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>symbolClass</strong></p></td><td><p>The expected presentation element class or null</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>The method returns the symbol if it is found. If the symbol is not shown in the diagram, the method returns <strong>null</strong>.</p><h2>Common symbol classes</h2><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p>Symbol</p></th><th><p>Class</p></th></tr><tr><td><p>Package Shape</p></td><td><p><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/PackageShape.html">PackageShape.class</a></strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shape</p></td><td><p><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/FeatureShape.html">FeatureShape.class</a> / <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/ClassifierShape.html">ClassifierShape.class</a></strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Border shape</p></td><td><p><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/FeatureOnBorderShape.html">FeatureOnBorderShape.class</a></strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Relationship path</p></td><td><p><strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/ConnectorPath.html">ConnectorPath.class</a></strong></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h2>Example diagram structure</h2><p>Let's use a diagram from the RescueDrone example to find symbols in it:</p><p><ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="PartsView.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h2>Finding an element on the diagram</h2><p>The <strong>context</strong> element is shown as the main shape on the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ce0965d-30f7-4957-9a48-79258daff331"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.diagram.FeatureShape;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

// find element on the diagram
PresentationElement contextShape = contextDiagram.findPresentationElement(context, FeatureShape.class);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If the symbol is found, <strong>contextShape</strong> contains the presentation element for the <strong>context</strong> model element.</p><h2>Finding a symbol inside another symbol</h2><p>The <strong>environment</strong> element is displayed inside the <strong>context</strong> shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd64537d-668a-4c0a-b854-e7e48cc11b54"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.diagram.FeatureShape;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

// find symbol inside the symbol at 2nd level
PresentationElement environmentShape = contextDiagram.findPresentationElement(environment, FeatureShape.class);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The same method is used even when the symbol is visually inside another shape.</p><h2>Finding a nested element</h2><p>The <strong>locatePerson_degradeLink</strong> perform action usage is nested deeper in the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84a1f67d-626f-4d51-a482-17a7d62f1a36"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.diagram.FeatureShape;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

// find symbol inside the symbol 3rd level
PresentationElement locatePersonPerformActionShape = contextDiagram.findPresentationElement(locatePersondegradeLinkPerformActionUsage, FeatureShape.class);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Use <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/FeatureShape.html">FeatureShape.class</a></strong> because the expected symbol is a shape.</p><h2>Finding a Feature on the Border shape</h2><p>Some features can be shown on the border of the other symbols; they can be displayed as <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/FeatureShape.html">FeatureShape</a></strong> or  FeatureOnBorderShape in the same diagram. by mentioning the excet class you will get the right symbol</p><pre><br /></pre><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In this example, </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">operatorP1Port</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> is shown as a port on the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">operator</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> shape.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e8d0551-ef93-4f35-beec-b18718288757"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.diagram.FeatureOnBorderShape;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

// find port on the shape
PresentationElement incidentLocationPortShape = contextDiagram.findPresentationElement(operatorP1Port, FeatureOnBorderShape.class);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Use <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/FeatureOnBorderShape.html">FeatureOnBorderShape.class</a></strong> when the expected symbol is located on the border of a shape.</p><h2>Finding a connector</h2><p>Connectors are shown as paths between symbols.</p><p>In this example, <strong>environmentToOperator</strong> is shown as a connector path.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79d68d5e-2618-4ea2-a8a2-45a090a443de"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.diagram.ConnectorPath;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

// finding the connector
PresentationElement environmentToOperatorPath = contextDiagram.findPresentationElement(environmentToOperator, ConnectorPath.class);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Use <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/diagram/ConnectorPath.html">ConnectorPath.class</a></strong> when the expected symbol is a connector line.</p><h2>Important notes</h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3dec5e90-b3cf-4ee5-94e4-e460f4a1dd28"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use <strong>findPresentationElement</strong> for symbols that are:</p><ul><li>directly on the diagram</li><li>inside another shape</li><li>nested deeper inside another nested shape</li><li>located on the border of a shape</li><li>shown as a relationship path</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Summary</h2><p>The diagram must already be loaded, and the model element must be shown in that diagram. If the symbol is not shown, the method returns <strong>null</strong>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573672 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00641: General convention

- page_id: `249573672`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573672/General+convention
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-01-31T10:11:22.245+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Unless specified otherwise, this document assumes that the following specific operations call HTTP methods.

| Operation | HTTP method | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Get or read | GET | Get data. |
| Create | POST | Create new data. |
| Replace attributes | PUT | Replace all existing attributes with the new ones. Attributes with unspecified new values will be reset to null values. |
| Update a (sub)set of attributes | PATCH | Replace some attributes only with the new ones. Unspecified attributes remain unchanged. |
| Delete | DELETE | Delete data. |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication' space='DEVG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model manipulation' space='DEVG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw-specific extensions' space='DEVG']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Unless specified otherwise, this document assumes that the following specific operations call HTTP methods.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 99.5857%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 20.1384%;" /><col class="" style="width: 12.343%;" /><col class="" style="width: 67.5523%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Operation</th><th>HTTP method</th><th>Meaning</th></tr><tr class=""><td>Get or read</td><td>GET</td><td>Get data.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Create</td><td>POST</td><td>Create new data.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Replace attributes</td><td>PUT</td><td>Replace all existing attributes with the new ones. Attributes with unspecified new values will be reset to null values.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Update a (sub)set of attributes</td><td>PATCH</td><td>Replace some attributes only with the new ones. Unspecified attributes remain unchanged.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Delete</td><td>DELETE</td><td>Delete data.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Authentication" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Model manipulation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="MagicDraw-specific extensions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573322 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00642: How plugins work

- page_id: `249573322`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573322/How+plugins+work
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:51:35.971+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins
- labels: ['plugin', 'init']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

On every startup, a modeling tool scans the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugins directories' space=''], and searches for subdirectories there:

- If a subdirectory contains the plugin descriptor file, the plugin's manager reads the descriptor file.
- If requirements specified in a descriptor file are fulfilled, the plugin's manager loads a specified class (the specified plugin class must be derived from t he *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin* class). Then a met hod *init()* of th e loaded class is called. Th e *init()* meth od can add GUI components using the actions architecture or do other activities and return from the method. T he *init()* met hod is called only if isSupported() returns true .

The following figure illustrates how do plugins work.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugins directories' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Th<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">e <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><em>init()</em></span> meth</span>od can add GUI components using the actions architecture or do other activities and return from the method. T<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">he <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><em>init()</em></span> met</span>hod is called only if <em>isSupported()</em> returns <em>true</em>. </li></ul><p>The following figure illustrates how do plugins <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="820d05a4-ab69-4d48-84c7-ec08aad275c6">work</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="How plugins work" ac:alt="How plugins work" ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="how_plugins_work.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be6237d0-46ae-44d8-ae47-134ddfc9ad1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Plugins directories" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573360 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00643: How to distribute resources

- page_id: `249573360`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573360/How+to+distribute+resources
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:34.550+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Distributing Resources
- labels: ['resource-builder', 'resource-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can distribute custom resources in the following two ways:

- Using Custom Resource Building Wizard :

1. Install the Development Tools plugin. Click Help > Resource/Plugin Manager , download the plugin and restart the program.
2. Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of the resource, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='']. The structure must not contain the "data/resourcemanager" folders yet.
3. Start Resource Builder (**Tools** > **Development Tools** >******Build Custom Resource...**), select a *MagicDraw* folder structure you have constructed in the step #2 step.
4. Fill the properties in the provided form.
5. Select the desired resource (a ZIP archive) output file, and click **Finish**. Resource Builder will create the "data/resourcemanager" folder containing an XML file with the properties you provided and assemble everything inside the *MagicDraw* folder into the ZIP file you specified as the output file. The resource (ZIP) file you have created can be distributed to your team members and installed using the Resource/Plugin Manager.

- Distribute manually. The manual distribution consists of the following steps:

1. Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of resource, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space=''] .
2. Create a Resource descriptor file. For more information about the Resource descriptor file, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space=''] .
3. Archive created files to a zip file. The zip file should include the required files, as well as folders that have a structure matching the structure of a modeling tool. Information about the folders structure for each type of resource and the general view of the file structure is depicted in the following figure: [ATTACHMENT filename='Structure_of_directories_and_files_that_could_be_distributed_through_ResourceManager.png']
4. Import your prepared data to your modeling tool through the Resource Manager. For more information about the MagicDraw Resource Manager, see*MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*.

Resource Manager supports zip archives only!

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can distribute custom resources in the following two ways:</p><ul><li>Using <strong>Custom Resource Building Wizard</strong>:</li></ul><ol><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Install the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin. Click <strong>Help </strong>&gt; <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>, download the plugin and restart the program.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of the resource, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60754bd1-08fe-4b22-8e1c-f3706d2cc6a8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The structure must not contain the &quot;data/resourcemanager&quot; folders yet.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Start Resource Builder (<strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Development Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> </strong><strong>Build Custom Resource...</strong>), select a <em>MagicDraw</em> folder structure you have constructed in the step #2 step.</p></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Fill the properties in the provided form.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Select the desired resource (a ZIP archive) output file, and click <strong>Finish</strong>. Resource Builder will create the &quot;data/resourcemanager&quot; folder containing an XML file with the properties you provided and assemble everything inside the <em>MagicDraw</em> folder into the ZIP file you specified as the output file. The resource (ZIP) file you have created can be distributed to your team members and installed using the Resource/Plugin Manager.</p></li></ol><ul><li>Distribute manually. The manual distribution consists of the following steps:</li></ul><ol><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of resource, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link>.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Create a Resource descriptor file. For more information about the Resource descriptor file, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource manager descriptor file" /></ac:link>.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Archive created files to a zip file. The zip file should include the required files, as well as folders that have a structure matching the structure of a modeling tool. Information about the folders structure for each type of resource and the general view of the file structure is depicted in the following figure:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structure_of_directories_and_files_that_could_be_distributed_through_ResourceManager.png" /></ac:image><br /><em><br /></em></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Import your prepared data to your modeling tool through the Resource Manager.<p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fe2b8d4-4f2f-4d32-8a11-ff2b796c6db8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>For more information about the MagicDraw Resource Manager, see </span><em>MagicDraw UserManual.pdf</em><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cf381e0-b667-4158-ada3-e128fab3c32d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Resource Manager supports zip archives only!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc9101d3-2965-4f05-a251-d4bbaa295297"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource manager descriptor file" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573566 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00644: How to provide a solution for a problem found during validation?

- page_id: `249573566`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573566/How+to+provide+a+solution+for+a+problem+found+during+validation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:08:07.600+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The validation rule returns annotations as validation results to the validation engine. Each annotation can contain a list of action objects that implements how a particular problem found by the validation rule can be solved. In order to create an action for solving, the validation rule’s provider must create a Java class that extends the*com.nomagic.actions.NMAction* class and implement the *actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)* method. A user will be able to invoke the action from the validation results table or from the browser. In order to enable performing the action on multiple targets, the action class must implement the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationAction* interface.

See *MyAction.java*, *FixJavaConstantNamesAction.java*, *MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java* and *JavaConstantNameValidationRuleImpl.java* examples in <program installation directory>\openapi\examples\validation.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation rules' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Annotating the elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The validation rule returns annotations as validation results to the validation engine. Each annotation can contain a list of action objects that implements how a particular problem found by the validation rule can be solved. In order to create an action for solving, the validation rule’s provider must create a Java class that extends the<em> com.nomagic.actions.NMAction</em> class and implement the <em>actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)</em> method. A user will be able to invoke the action from the validation results table or from the browser. In order to enable performing the action on multiple targets, the action class must implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationAction</em> interface.</p><p>See <em>MyAction.java</em>, <em>FixJavaConstantNamesAction.java</em>, <em>MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java</em> and <em>JavaConstantNameValidationRuleImpl.java</em> examples in &lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\validation.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e957a260-479d-4d40-9eea-ef16acd7c6f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Annotating the elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180524 space=DEVG version=10 -->
## PAGE 00645: How to Run a Custom User Python Script

- page_id: `314180524`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/314180524/How+to+Run+a+Custom+User+Python+Script
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2026-06-19T20:30:09.141+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Python Integration Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to create and execute custom Python scripts using the Python Open API Integration framework.

#### Overview

A custom Python script follows the same execution pattern as the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running the Python Open API Example' space=''].

The framework components provided in the package should be reused whenever possible to ensure consistent startup, shutdown, and environment configuration behavior.

#### Creating a New Script

Create a new Python file in your project directory, for example:

```text

```

A custom script typically consists of the following steps:

- initialize the application environment
- start the modeling tool
- create or open the project
- execute model modifications
- save the project
- shut down the application

#### Basic Script Structure

The following example shows the recommended structure for a custom script. The skeleton file can be found in the `custom_script` directory.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page describes how to create and execute custom Python scripts using the Python Open API Integration framework.</p><h2>Overview</h2><p>A custom Python script follows the same execution pattern as the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Running the Python Open API Example" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The framework components provided in the package should be reused whenever possible to ensure consistent startup, shutdown, and environment configuration behavior.</p><h2>Creating a New Script</h2><p>Create a new Python file in your project directory, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9a31b61-4a59-4adf-a41f-eaaaf5d828c5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[my_custom_script.py
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A custom script typically consists of the following steps:</p><ul><li>initialize the application environment</li><li>start the modeling tool</li><li>create or open the project</li><li>execute model modifications</li><li>save the project</li><li>shut down the application</li></ul><h2>Basic Script Structure</h2><p>The following example shows the recommended structure for a custom script. The skeleton file can be found in the <code class="KDcb0c" style="text-decoration: none;">custom_script</code> directory.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7779d41a-f1dd-4466-a599-ee146de68684"><ac:parameter ac:name="">python</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[from libraries.core.application_manager import ApplicationManager
from libraries.core.sysml_project_operations import create_or_open_project

from pathlib import Path
import platform
import jpype._gui as jgui

IS_MAC = platform.system() == "Darwin"

CAMEO_INSTALL_PATH = (
    Path("/Applications/Cameo_Systems_Modeler_2026x_Refresh2")
    if IS_MAC
    else Path(r"C:\Users\MZN14\Downloads\Cameo_Systems_Modeler_2026x_Refresh2")
)

app_manager = ApplicationManager(CAMEO_INSTALL_PATH)

def execute_workflow():

    app_manager.start_application(show_gui=False)

    try:
        create_or_open_project("MyProject")

        #
        # Add custom Open API logic here
        #

        app_manager.save_project_changes()

    finally:
        app_manager.teardown_application_session()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app_manager.start_jvm()
    if IS_MAC:
        jgui.setupGuiEnvironment(execute_workflow)
    else:
        execute_workflow()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573425 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00646: Identifying elements

- page_id: `249573425`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573425/Identifying+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:52:18.977+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Two methods provide an identifier of an *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*. Either method can be used depending on the situation. This section explains the difference between the two:

1. com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.base.ModelObject.getLocalID()
2. com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation.MDObject.getID()

The meaning of the returned identifiers depends on the project type.

###### Local (.mdzip) projects

Both methods return the same identifier, which is permanent and unique in the scope of the project. In most cases, this ID is unique among different projects, unless you create a copy of the project by copying the file or using the **Save As** command.

#### TIP: Examples

Examples

1. If a project A is used in projects B and C , then the elements of the project A have the same identifiers in both projects B and C .
2. If a project A is copied as a project A1 , and a project B uses the project A , using the project A1 additionally will not be allowed since IDs must remain unique in the scope of the project B .

###### Teamwork Cloud projects

If a local project is added to the Teamwork Cloud (or vice versa), *getLocalID()* consistently returns the same identifier value. Note that several elements with the same local ID may exist in the scope of a single Teamwork Cloud project.

*getID()* returns UUID, which is unique both in the project and in the scope of the whole Teamwork Cloud server repository.

The Teamwork Cloud imports (clones) the contents of used projects. This means copies of the elements are created inside the project. While **getLocalID()** returns the same value for different copies, **getID()** will return a unique value for each copy (project usage).

#### TIP: Example

Example

If a project *A* is copied as a project *A1*, and a Teamwork Cloud project *B* uses both *A* and *A1*, **getLocalID()** returns the same value for corresponding elements of *A* and *A1*, but **getID()** returns different identifiers for the same elements.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

Both identifiers can be used to retrieve the element using the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getElementByID(java.lang.String)*. Be aware that if several elements exist in the project by the provided local ID, only a single one of them is returned.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with UML model' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573342 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00647: Identifying plugin dependencies

- page_id: `249573342`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573342/Identifying+plugin+dependencies
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:20.758+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some Open API classes may come from plugins (like the SysML plugin, for example). In this case, make sure that *jar*files from a plugin directory are added to the compiled classpath. Also, your *plugin.xml* must define the required plugin too.

To find dependencies

- In the Main toolbar, click Tools > Development Tools > Find Plugin Dependency by Class Name...

The Development Tools menu item is available once the Development Tools Plugin is installed. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL'] page.

Also, your <em>plugin.xml</em> must define the required plugin too.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To find dependencies</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Main toolbar, click <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Tools &gt; Development Tools &gt; Find Plugin Dependency by Class Name...<br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="715abddf-cc51-4d32-8a21-36295559c0a5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Development Tools menu item is available once the Development Tools Plugin is installed. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Installing plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
