<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573443 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00670: Listening to property change events

- page_id: `249573443`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573443/Listening+to+property+change+events
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-10T12:32:01.420+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Event support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *PropertyChangeEvents* support provides the ability to listen to the event by different scopes. The scope depends on how the listener is registered:

- If the listener is registered at the whole repository, it receives events about the changes in all the elements.
- If the listener is registered at the specific element, it receives events about the changes in any property of this element.
- If the listener registered with the specific property at the element, it receives events about the changes in this property.
- If the listener is registered with the specific property for the element type, it receives events about the changes in all the elements of the specific type.

The *java.beans.PropertyChangeListener* should be registered to receive these events. There are several different ways to register a listener:

- To listen for the whole repository, use:

```java

```

This listener will get all the property change events from any element.

- To listen for any delete operation in the repository and get notified before the delete action is performed, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified, when any model element is set to be deleted.

- To listen for any property changes of the specific element, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified when any element property is changed.

- To listen for property changes defined by the specified config of the specific element type, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified, when any property of any element in the project with this type is changed. If “configs” is NULL, the listener will get all property change events.

*EventSupport* could be disabled from the event firing:

- To check if it is enabled, use:

```java

```

- To stop/ start event firing, use:

```java

```

These examples show, how to create the property change listeners to listen to the different kind of properties.

###### **Listener for listening to the specific element’s any property changes:**

```java

```

###### **Listener for listening to the specific property (NAME) of the specific element:**

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The <em>PropertyChangeEvents</em> support provides the ability to listen to the event by different scopes. The scope depends on how the listener is registered:</p><ul><li>If the listener is registered at the whole repository, it receives events about the changes in all the elements.</li><li>If the listener is registered at the specific element, it receives events about the changes in any property of this element.</li><li>If the listener registered with the specific property at the element, it receives events about the changes in this property.</li><li>If the listener is registered with the specific property for the element type, it receives events about the changes in all the elements of the specific type.</li></ul><p>The <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeListener</em> should be registered to receive these events. There are several different ways to register a listener:</p><ul><li>To listen for the whole repository, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffb93836-0efa-4a84-81ac-387a95a341c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[        project.getRepositoryListenerRegistry().addPropertyChangeListener(listener, (RefObject)null);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will get all the property change events from any element.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><ul><li>To listen for any delete operation in the repository and get notified before the delete action is performed, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf20a9ef-fe34-40a0-9f46-3d0b55acfcc1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getRepositoryListenerRegistry().addPropertyChangeListener(listener, UML2MetamodelConstants.BEFORE_DELETE);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified, when any model element is set to be deleted.</p><p><br /></p><ul><li>To listen for any property changes of the specific element, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c70a72de-22c3-4b7a-bae0-1256621e191d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		element.addPropertyChangeListener(listener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified when any element property is changed.</p><p><br /></p><ul><li><p>To listen for property changes defined by the specified config of the specific element type, use:</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="caa780fd-eb62-494e-adcb-9e57cf81aba5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getSmartEventSupport().register(new Registration(aClass, config, listener));]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified, when any property of any element in the project with this type is changed. If “configs” is NULL, the listener will get all property change events.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dce80e5-9c6e-41b1-b514-60eaa771f2dc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><em>EventSupport</em> could be disabled from the event firing:</p><ul><li>To check if it is enabled, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="971277da-d508-4924-a757-3d984f918130"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 		project.getRepository().getEventSupport().isEnableEventFiring()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>To stop/ start event firing, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3628d536-4402-46cf-a926-c4a752530943"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getRepository().getEventSupport().setEnableEventFiring(...)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><p>These examples show, how to create the property change listeners to listen to the different kind of properties.</p><h4><strong>Listener for listening to the specific element’s any property changes:</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c9d224a-fac7-4b80-b6f2-e788242ff04e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    element.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            // evt.getPropertyName() is changed
        }

    });]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection3"><h4><strong>Listener for listening to the specific property (NAME) of the specific element:</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0763d8c8-9067-4161-97d8-11a5c302c053"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    element.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            if (PropertyNames.NAME.equals(evt.getPropertyName()))
            {
                // name is Changed
            }
        }
   });]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573444 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00671: Listening to related elements in hierarchy events

- page_id: `249573444`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573444/Listening+to+related+elements+in+hierarchy+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:40:07.025+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Event support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is a possibility to listen for changes of the elements, which are somehow related with the given element. For example, we want to be notified, when the element’s owner name is changed. The *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartPropertyChangeListener* class is dedicated for the situations like this. The main idea of the solution is to provide the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfig*, which will provide the chain of the property names to listen to.

The***SmartListenerConfig*class provides static methods for the default configuration of property chains. This is useful, when listening for common property change events.

Use the *SmartPropertyChangeListener*class*createSmartPropertyListener(...)* method to create and register such listener for the particular element, which will be notified with events provided by the *SmartListenerConfig*.

****If the listener is not needed anymore, unregister it using the *removePropertyChangeListener(...**)***method.

****

**Example #1. Listen to the element owner's name**

```java

```

**Example #2. Listen recursively to the owner's name and the *"Is Abstract"* property**

```java

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>There is a possibility to listen for changes of the elements, which are somehow related with the given element. For example, we want to be notified, when the element’s owner name is changed. The <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartPropertyChangeListener</em> class is dedicated for the situations like this. The main idea of the solution is to provide the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfig</em>, which will provide the chain of the property names to listen to.</p><p>The<em> </em><em>SmartListenerConfig </em>class provides static methods for the default configuration of property chains. This is useful, when listening for common property change events.</p><p>Use the <em>SmartPropertyChangeListener </em>class<em> createSmartPropertyListener(...)</em> method to create and register such listener for the particular element, which will be notified with events provided by the <em>SmartListenerConfig</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f47013c6-52be-40ae-ae38-90aad40b75da"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>If the listener is not needed anymore, unregister it using the <em>removePropertyChangeListener(...<strong>) </strong></em>method.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Example #1. Listen to the element owner's name</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e455947-8c4a-48c6-b2c2-35fe80d95f6d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   		Element element = ...; // some element
 
        SmartListenerConfig config = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); // listen to the element's name 
        config.listenToNested(PropertyNames.OWNER).listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); //listen to the element owner's name
 
        // create the listener
        SmartPropertyChangeListener.createSmartPropertyListener(element, new PropertyChangeListener()
        {
            public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
            {
               // the change event comes here
            }
        }, config);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Example #2. Listen recursively to the owner's name and the <em>&quot;Is Abstract&quot;</em> property</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90ac467b-5be6-4826-9d81-0decdf8ca156"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    	Element element = ...; // some element
 
        SmartListenerConfig config = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.IS_ABSTRACT); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.OWNER, config);
 
        SmartPropertyChangeListener.createSmartPropertyListener(element, new PropertyChangeListener()
        {
            public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
            {
                // the change event comes here
            }
        }, config);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573445 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00672: Listening to transaction commit events on session closing

- page_id: `249573445`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573445/Listening+to+transaction+commit+events+on+session+closing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:23:09.399+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Event support
- labels: ['transaction-commit-listener']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every session creating and closing starts and commits the model editing transaction. The *com.nomagic.uml2.transaction.TransactionCommitListener* is a special listener, which is notified when the all changes inside a transaction are done and the transaction is closed.

The *TransactionCommitListener* contains *transactionCommited(java.util.Collection<java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent>)***method, which provides a collection of all *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent(s)* that were executed in a transaction.

****

**Create a custom transaction commit listener:**

```java
events)
        {
            return new Runnable()
            {
                public void run()
                {
                    for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
                    {
                        if (UML2MetamodelConstants.INSTANCE_CREATED.equals(event.getPropertyName()))
                        {
                            Object source = event.getSource();
                            if (source instanceof Property)
                            {
                                Property property = (Property) source; Element owner = property.getOwner();
                                if (owner instanceof Classifier)
                                {
                                    Classifier propertyOwner = (Classifier) owner;
                                    propertyOwner.setName("Contains (" + propertyOwner.getAttribute().size() + ") attributes");
                                }

 
                                // additionally for this Property we register listener to listen for any property changes in this Element properties.
                                property.addPropertyChangeListener(new DerivedValuePropertyChangeListener());
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            };
       }
    }]]>
```

This “transaction commit listener” checks, if a new property is created in a classifier and updates the classifier’s (a property owner) name. All changes are done in the same session.

###### **Register the custom “transaction commit listener” into the project**

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every session creating and closing starts and commits the model editing transaction. The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.transaction.TransactionCommitListener</em> is a special listener, which is notified when the all changes inside a transaction are done and the transaction is closed.</p><p>The <em>TransactionCommitListener</em> contains <em>transactionCommited(java.util.Collection&lt;java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent&gt;)</em><em> </em>method, which provides a collection of all <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent(s)</em> that were executed in a transaction.</p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">Create a custom transaction commit listener:</strong></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30d64b3d-3441-4403-a926-9b85f42af2ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public class MyTransactionListener implements TransactionCommitListener
    {
        public Runnable transactionCommited(final Collection<PropertyChangeEvent> events)
        {
            return new Runnable()
            {
                public void run()
                {
                    for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
                    {
                        if (UML2MetamodelConstants.INSTANCE_CREATED.equals(event.getPropertyName()))
                        {
                            Object source = event.getSource();
                            if (source instanceof Property)
                            {
                                Property property = (Property) source; Element owner = property.getOwner();
                                if (owner instanceof Classifier)
                                {
                                    Classifier propertyOwner = (Classifier) owner;
                                    propertyOwner.setName("Contains (" + propertyOwner.getAttribute().size() + ") attributes");
                                }

 
                                // additionally for this Property we register listener to listen for any property changes in this Element properties.
                                property.addPropertyChangeListener(new DerivedValuePropertyChangeListener());
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            };
       }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>This “transaction commit listener” checks, if a new property is created in a classifier and updates the classifier’s (a property owner) name. All changes are done in the same session.</p><h4><strong>Register the custom “transaction commit listener” into the project</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e23f7d2b-8475-417a-8dbc-c98449059aa6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    MyTransactionListener transactionListener = new MyTransactionListener(); 
    TransactionManager transactionManager = project.getRepository().getTransactionManager();
    transactionManager.addTransactionCommitListener(transactionListener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="491fee21-8713-4bf9-a6c9-2219abb1169b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00673: Local Repository

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html)* class provides utility methods for creating and opening projects stored in Local UPS.

#### Project Creation

Create a new SysML v2 project

```java

```

##### Notes

- The project name must be unique within the local repository
- Category is optional:

```text
Creating with Category
```

```java

```

#### Project Opening

Opens an existing project from the local repository by name.

```text

```

##### Notes

- Project must exist in the local repository (UPS)
- Project name must match exactly
- Throws an exception if the project cannot be found or opened

#### Summary

| Operation | Method |
| --- | --- |
| Create project | createProject(name) |
| Create with category | createProject(name,category) |
| Open project | openProject(name) |

````html
Project project = SysMLProjectHelper.createUPSProject("MyLocalProject"); ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Notes</h3><ul><li data-uuid="49a45de7-d55e-4ac2-aac5-ddcbd801c8e1">The project name must be unique within the local repository</li><li data-uuid="f3fff837-d7a6-4ecd-9693-e547b67b0a65">Category is optional:</li></ul><pre><span style="font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;letter-spacing: -0.006em;white-space: normal;">Creating with Category</span></pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56251698-9681-422a-96b1-5ceec90f10e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper;

Project project = SysMLProjectHelper.createUPSProject("MyLocalProject", "MyCategory");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Project Opening</h2><p>Opens an existing project from the local repository by name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce77dfcb-9f20-4225-9233-8498c4055049"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper;

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Notes</h3><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project</a> must exist in the local repository (UPS)</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project</a> name must match exactly</li><li>Throws an exception if the project cannot be found or opened</li></ul><h2>Summary</h2><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p>Operation</p></th><th><p>Method</p></th></tr><tr><td><p>Create project</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html#createProject(java.lang.String)">createProject(name)</a></code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create with category</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html#createProject(java.lang.String,java.lang.String)">createProject(name,category)</a></code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open project</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html#openProject(java.lang.String)">openProject(name)</a></code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573601 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00674: MagicDraw file format

- page_id: `249573601`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573601/MagicDraw+file+format
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:12.903+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: ['zip-based-file-format', 'plain-text-file-format', 'xmi']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### File format in MagicDraw 3.6-17.0

MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) file was a plain xmi file with extensions used to save diagrams information and project structure information (a mount table). The mdzip file was a compressed xmi file. The code engineering information was saved in the different file with extension "mdr".

##### File format in MagicDraw 17.0.1 and up

The inner project structure as well as the file format has been changed in version 17.0.1. An XMI version is upgraded to 2.4 with corresponding changes required by this standard.

A project is a collection of xmi, text, and binary resources. A native file format contains all resources saved as separate files. All these files are collected to one zip file.

###### Zip Based File Format (mdzip, xml.zip)

A zip file (xml.zip, mdzip) contains at least the following entries:

- Meta information about entries records.properties.
- Project identifier starts with PROJECT-, for example, PROJECT-f41072fa-d1aa-4e90-b7d9- fdd8862ed8af.
- Project structure information:

*com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project*.

- Shared and not shared uml model information:

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.shared_model,*

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model*

- Proxies information backup of other external resources. Starts with proxy.
- Project options ends with

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.personalprojectoptions and*

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.commonprojectoptions.*

- Diagrams and other “binary” resources, entry names do not contain any meaningful information, just resource id, like 80a7058a-91b1-41fb-9a3a-cd7a8c6be52d.

###### Plain Text File Format (xml, mdxml)

Export to a non zip file formats like .xml or .mdxml is a combination of all zip file entries into the one plain xmi file. In this file, a uml model is saved as it was in earlier versions, while other information is saved as xmi:Extension.

****

###### Changes Related to Diagrams

Until version 17.0.1, diagrams were saved as an xmi extension. All diagrams were saved under one extension <mdOwnedDiagrams>. Both a diagram model and diagram symbols were saved in this extension. The <mdOwnedDiagrams> extension was saved outside a uml:Model element.

Version 17.0.1 saves the diagram element as a separate element extension named <modelExtension>. This extension is stored in a place where the diagram element is serialized (inside a diagram owner). If one owner has more than one diagram, they may be written in the one extension. In this extension, a diagram element is saved as a normal model element, and all its attributes and references are saved according xmi rules. A diagram element contains DiagramRepresentationObject, which contains information about a diagram type, required features, and so on. DiagramRepresentationObject contains DiagramContentsDescriptor, which describes used elements in a diagram and contains BinaryObject. BinaryObject has the attribute “streamContentID” which identifies a zip entry for diagram symbols. All information is stored in <mdOwnedViews>. The following schema presents finding diagram symbols entry id:

**Diagram > DiagramRepresentationObject > DiagramContentsDescriptor > BinaryObject > BinaryObject. streamContentID**

****

###### Genmodel for DiagramRepresentationObject and DiagramContentsDescriptor

```xml
diagram.ecore
	CDO=org.eclipse.emf.cdo
	
		
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
		
	
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><h3>File format in MagicDraw 3.6-17.0</h3><p>MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) file was a plain xmi file with extensions used to save diagrams information and project structure information (a mount table). The mdzip file was a compressed xmi file. The code engineering information was saved in the different file with extension &quot;mdr&quot;.</p><h3>File format in MagicDraw 17.0.1 and up</h3><p>The inner project structure as well as the file format has been changed in version 17.0.1. An XMI version is upgraded to 2.4 with corresponding changes required by this standard. </p><p>A project is a collection of xmi, text, and binary resources. A native file format contains all resources saved as separate files. All these files are collected to one zip file.</p><h4>Zip Based File Format (mdzip, xml.zip)</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">A zip file (xml.zip, mdzip) contains at least the following entries:</span></p><ul><li>Meta information about entries records.properties.</li><li>Project identifier starts with PROJECT-, for example, PROJECT-f41072fa-d1aa-4e90-b7d9- fdd8862ed8af.</li><li>Project structure information: </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project</em>.</p><ul><li>Shared and not shared uml model information: </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.shared_model, </em></p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model</em></p><ul><li>Proxies information backup of other external resources. Starts with proxy.</li><li>Project options ends with </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.personalprojectoptions and </em></p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.commonprojectoptions.</em></p><ul><li>Diagrams and other “binary” resources, entry names do not contain any meaningful information, just resource id, like 80a7058a-91b1-41fb-9a3a-cd7a8c6be52d.</li></ul><p> </p><h4>Plain Text File Format (xml, mdxml)</h4><p>Export to a non zip file formats like .xml or .mdxml is a combination of all zip file entries into the one plain xmi file. In this file, a uml model is saved as it was in earlier versions, while other information is saved as xmi:Extension.</p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><h4>Changes Related to Diagrams</h4><p>Until version 17.0.1, diagrams were saved as an xmi extension. All diagrams were saved under one extension &lt;mdOwnedDiagrams&gt;. Both a diagram model and diagram symbols were saved in this extension. The &lt;mdOwnedDiagrams&gt; extension was saved outside a uml:Model element.</p><p>Version 17.0.1 saves the diagram element as a separate element extension named &lt;modelExtension&gt;. This extension is stored in a place where the diagram element is serialized (inside a diagram owner). If one owner has more than one diagram, they may be written in the one extension. In this extension, a diagram element is saved as a normal model element, and all its attributes and references are saved according xmi rules. A diagram element contains DiagramRepresentationObject, which contains information about a diagram type, required features, and so on. DiagramRepresentationObject contains DiagramContentsDescriptor, which describes used elements in a diagram and contains BinaryObject. BinaryObject has the attribute “streamContentID” which identifies a zip entry for diagram symbols. All information is stored in &lt;mdOwnedViews&gt;. The following schema presents finding diagram symbols entry id:<span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Diagram &gt; DiagramRepresentationObject &gt; DiagramContentsDescriptor &gt; BinaryObject &gt; BinaryObject. streamContentID</strong></p></div><div class="WordSection2"><p><strong style="color: rgb(61,61,61);line-height: 1.6666666;"><br /></strong></p><h4>Genmodel for DiagramRepresentationObject and DiagramContentsDescriptor</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da45d653-b7b0-4f71-aaa4-06f4252b8180"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<genmodel:GenModel xmi:version="2.0"
xmlns:xmi="http://www.omg.org/XMI" xmlns:ecore="http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/Ecore"
xmlns:genmodel="http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/GenModel" modelDirectory="/com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation/src"
modelPluginID="com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation" modelName="Diagram
updateClasspath="false"
rootExtendsInterface="org.eclipse.emf.cdo.CDOObject" 
rootExtendsClass="org.eclipse.emf.internal.cdo.CDOObjectImpl"
reflectiveDelegation="true" importerID="org.eclipse.emf.importer.ecore"
featureDelegation="Reflective"
containmentProxies="true" complianceLevel="5.0" copyrightFields="false"
usedGenPackages="../../com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project/model/binary.genmodel#//binary"
classNamePattern="">
	<foreignModel>diagram.ecore</foreignModel>
	<modelPluginVariables>CDO=org.eclipse.emf.cdo</modelPluginVariables>
	<genPackages prefix="Diagram" basePackage="com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation"
	disposableProviderFactory="true"
	ecorePackage="diagram.ecore#/">
		<genClasses image="false" ecoreClass="diagram.ecore#//
		AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject">
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/ID"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/type"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/umlType"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramProperties"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/initialFrameSizeSet"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/requiredFeature"/>
			<genFeatures property="None" children="true" createChild="true"
			ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//
			AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramContents"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramStyleID"/></genClasses>
			<genClassesecoreClass="diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor">
			<genFeatures property="None" notify="false" createChild="false"
			ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/representation"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/exporterName"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/exporterVersion"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/usedElements"/>
			<genFeatures property="None" children="true" createChild="true" ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/ binaryObject"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/contentHash"/>
		</genClasses>
	</genPackages>
</genmodel:GenModel>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573684 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00675: MagicDraw-specific extensions

- page_id: `249573684`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573684/MagicDraw-specific+extensions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:27:56.770+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1263120387

INLINE

1263120388

INLINE

1263120386

INLINE

**On this page**

1263120388

INLINE

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model manipulation' space='DEVG'] describes how REST API can be used to manipulate data in Teamwork Cloud at the Eclipse modeling framework (EMF) level. However, usingthe API at the EMF level may become difficult when working with larger projects and more complex compositions.

Therefore, to facilitate the most common model manipulation tasks, Teamwork Cloud REST API has MagicDraw-specific extensions that help minimize the number of calls necessary to find the primary MagicDraw UML model within project decomposition.

##### Inspecting model decomposition

Normally, MagicDraw has different kinds of models within Teamwork Cloud resources. The term “models” here refers to the data structure required to store MagicDraw-specific project information that the tool needs when loading up projects on the client-side. Currently, Teamwork Cloud REST API exposes two kinds of such models – **decomposition** and **UML**, as shown in the code below. You can retrieve a list of available models within the master branch and chosen resource revision by issuing GET to */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models*.

```text

```

You can further inspect the content of each of the models by issuing GET to */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/{modelId}*, where *modelId* is **decomposition** or **uml** respectively.

##### Decomposition model

This model lists all of the Used Project hierarchy within the chosen resource. The excerpt of the results after calling */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/decomposition* is shown below.

```text

```

The main root project which the call is executed against has a type of **models:PrimaryProject**. You can find all of its directly used projects within the **models:uses** array. Since the decomposition model covers the full hierarchy, it includes both locally used projects (usually concerning Standard/System profiles) and server projects.

Each of the directly used projects has a type of **models:UsedProject** that has its own decomposition, hence enabling the full breakdown of usage structure from the primary project perspective covering both, directly and indirectly, used projects.

The **id** attribute refers to the project ID of **esiproject:EsiProject** type, not the UML model root element.

##### UML model

Different from **decomposition** model previously described, the **UML** model provides a flat view of the structure of the target model. Note that the term **project** should not be confused with the term **UML** model here. The purpose of the **UML** model is to expose direct links to root elements of UML models in primary projects, and both of its, directly and indirectly, used projects.

The excerpt of the results after calling */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/uml*is shown below.

```text

```

The **models:PrimaryModel** type object provides a direct link to the main project’s UML model root element via the **models:root** attribute. Compared to the EMF-based approach described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model manipulation' space='DEVG'], using the above REST API extension is a matter of a single call and requires only the resource ID and revision number to be known in advance.

1288072285

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide' space='DEVG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='General convention' space='DEVG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication' space='DEVG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model manipulation' space='DEVG']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="085e67a4-d978-4b07-90dd-ee51c8be799e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1263120387</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cef85e87-6835-4de7-bd33-7816b846f62c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1263120388</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4eb33940-92c5-40c8-8d90-2f7737a248bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1263120386</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7db233a9-d0dd-4835-af3a-2811e1988e41" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bbea89b-6453-4768-b045-aee8a46ddc25"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1263120388</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:link ac:anchor="MagicDrawprimaryproject"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Model manipulation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model manipulation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> describes how REST API can be used to manipulate data in Teamwork Cloud at the Eclipse modeling framework (EMF) level. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8f06d718-efd3-4cd1-81c4-b9130e1386d2">However, using </ac:inline-comment-marker>the API at the EMF level may become difficult when working with larger projects and more complex compositions.</p><p>Therefore, to facilitate the most common model manipulation tasks, Teamwork Cloud REST API has MagicDraw-specific extensions that help minimize the number of calls necessary to find the primary MagicDraw UML model within project decomposition.</p><h3>Inspecting model decomposition</h3><p>Normally, MagicDraw has different kinds of models within Teamwork Cloud resources. The term “models” here refers to the data structure required to store MagicDraw-specific project information that the tool needs when loading up projects on the client-side. Currently, Teamwork Cloud REST API exposes two kinds of such models – <strong>decomposition</strong> and <strong>UML</strong>, as shown in the code below. You can retrieve a list of available models within the master branch and chosen resource revision by issuing GET to <em>/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d224ef-7c44-4b16-b8b7-a206337296b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "@type": [
    "ldp:Container",
    "ldp:BasicContainer"
  ],
  "ldp:contains": [
    {
      "@id": "decomposition"
    },
    {
      "@id": "uml"
    }
  ],
  "@id": "",
  "@context": {
    "ldp": "http://www.w3.org/ns/ldp#"
  }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can further inspect the content of each of the models by issuing GET to <em>/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/{modelId}</em>, where <em>modelId</em> is <strong>decomposition</strong> or <strong>uml</strong> respectively.</p><h3>Decomposition model</h3><p>This model lists all of the Used Project hierarchy within the chosen resource. The excerpt of the results after calling <em>/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/decomposition</em> is shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f7f660a-656e-452d-8fb0-dd04a8a40714"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "@type": "models:Decomposition",
  "models:usages": [
    {
      "models:project": {
        "models:name": "HSUV",
        "@type": "models:PrimaryProject",
        "@id": "../../elements/8a91f0a9-cb83-4c64-865e-e4c797556807"
      },
      "models:uses": [      
        {
          "models:requiredVersion": "33",
          "models:uri": "twcloud:/45d11019-c077-4d4e-84d3-75eaf21e1230/37b7511d-bb55-4280-99b9-2da1e1cad9d6",
          "models:name": "Electronic components library",
          "@id": "../../elements/70abbe99-a551-4e46-9eda-d718c027a300"
        },
        {
          "models:requiredVersion": "19.0 v9",
          "models:uri": "file:/C:/Users/tomvil/Desktop/Cameo_Systems_Modeler_190_sp1_no_install/profiles/SysML%20Profile.mdzip",
          "models:name": "SysML Profile",
          "@id": "../../elements/7e6d87a8-790e-4415-b1d1-493a5a2c82ac"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "models:project": {
        "models:name": "Time & Performance_Profile",
        "@type": "models:UsedProject",
        "@id": "../../elements/80f3fefb-77f9-49f1-afa8-6fe25951659c"
      },
      "models:uses": [
        {
          "models:requiredVersion": "19.0 v9",
          "models:uri": "file:/C:/Users/tomvil/Desktop/Cameo_Systems_Modeler_190_sp1_no_install/profiles/UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip",
          "models:name": "UML_Standard_Profile",
          "@id": "../../elements/280a000d-ec4a-4278-aeda-15f270c99a19"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "models:project": {
        "models:name": "Electronic components library",
        "@type": "models:UsedProject",
        "@id": "../../elements/70abbe99-a551-4e46-9eda-d718c027a300"
      },
      "models:uses": [
        {
          "models:requiredVersion": "19.0 v9",
          "models:uri": "file:/C:/Users/tomvil/Desktop/Cameo_Systems_Modeler_190_sp1_no_install/profiles/SysML%20Profile.mdzip",
          "models:name": "SysML Profile",
          "@id": "../../elements/7e6d87a8-790e-4415-b1d1-493a5a2c82ac"
        },
        {
          "models:requiredVersion": "19.0 v9",
          "models:uri": "file:/C:/Users/tomvil/Desktop/Cameo_Systems_Modeler_190_sp1_no_install/profiles/UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip",
          "models:name": "UML_Standard_Profile",
          "@id": "../../elements/280a000d-ec4a-4278-aeda-15f270c99a19"
        },
        {
          "models:requiredVersion": "19.0 v9",
          "models:uri": "file:/C:/Users/tomvil/Desktop/Cameo_Systems_Modeler_190_sp1_no_install/SysML%20constraints.mdzip",
          "models:name": "SysML constraints",
          "@id": "../../elements/fcffda41-a4a4-45d1-85bf-ee033f449701"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "@id": "",
  "@context": {
    "models": "https://localhost:8111/osmc/schema/kerml/234567/models#",
    "ldp": "http://www.w3.org/ns/ldp#"
  }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The main root project which the call is executed against has a type of <strong>models:PrimaryProject</strong>. You can find all of its directly used projects within the <strong>models:uses</strong> array. Since the decomposition model covers the full hierarchy, it includes both locally used projects (usually concerning Standard/System profiles) and server projects.</p><p>Each of the directly used projects has a type of <strong>models:UsedProject</strong> that has its own decomposition, hence enabling the full breakdown of usage structure from the primary project perspective covering both, directly and indirectly, used projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4db9d783-2aa8-4b90-9192-8ef42d3ae783"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>id</strong> attribute refers to the project ID of <strong>esiproject:EsiProject</strong> type, not the UML model root element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>UML model</h3><p>Different from <strong>decomposition</strong> model previously described, the <strong>UML</strong> model provides a flat view of the structure of the target model. Note that the term <strong>project</strong> should not be confused with the term <strong>UML</strong> model here. The purpose of the <strong>UML</strong> model is to expose direct links to root elements of UML models in primary <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b6ac24b3-e444-4fb0-baf6-ad5679a1e22e">projects, and both</ac:inline-comment-marker> of its, directly and indirectly, used projects.</p><p>The excerpt of the results after calling <em>/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/uml </em>is shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16295c56-3819-4323-918a-a66a8b44a8e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "@type": "models:Uml",
  "@id": "",
  "@context": {
    "models": "https://localhost:8111/osmc/schema/kerml/234567/models#",
    "ldp": "http://www.w3.org/ns/ldp#"
  },
  "models:roots": [
    {
      "models:name": "HSUV",
      "@type": "models:PrimaryModel",
      "models:root": "../../elements/2b36804f-b904-4b29-87a5-5d438e10b696"
    },
    {
      "models:name": "Electronic components library",
      "@type": "models:UsedModel",
      "models:root": "../../elements/42dda2bc-a1cf-4ad8-bb73-88b20caa3a74"
    },   
    {
      "models:name": "SysML Profile",
      "@type": "models:UsedModel",
      "models:root": "../../elements/ee2da8c9-63dc-42ec-a68a-d96dddba72df"
    },
    {
      "models:name": "UML_Standard_Profile",
      "@type": "models:UsedModel",
      "models:root": "../../elements/f325ec2b-b17c-4e59-9ddf-2e7d74e578e1"
    },    
    {
      "models:name": "Fuel Supply",
      "@type": "models:UsedModel",
      "models:root": "../../elements/c4adaad7-9884-4501-a1a1-b2f515b7e6bc"
    },
    {
      "models:name": "Steering system",
      "@type": "models:UsedModel",
      "models:root": "../../elements/f1af8807-676c-4087-86ac-6e962b5bae1f"
    },
    {
      "models:name": "Time & Performance_Profile",
      "@type": "models:UsedModel",
      "models:root": "../../elements/895a1228-b850-4d8b-93b8-b33917f19952"
    }
  ]
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="confluence-link">The <strong>models:PrimaryModel</strong> type object provides a direct link to the main project’s UML model root element via the <strong>models:root</strong> attribute. Compared to the EMF-based approach described in <ac:link ac:anchor="MagicDrawprimaryproject"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Model manipulation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model manipulation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, using the above REST API extension is a matter of a single call and requires only the resource ID and revision number to be known in advance.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="523578f0-1dd8-40ae-8eab-3a39a0f3a43f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1288072285</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><span class="plugin_pagetree_children_span"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span class="plugin_pagetree_children_span"><span class="plugin_pagetree_children_span"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="General convention" /></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span class="plugin_pagetree_children_span"><span class="plugin_pagetree_children_span"><span class="plugin_pagetree_children_span"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Authentication" /></ac:link></span><br /></span></span></li><li><span class="plugin_pagetree_children_span"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Model manipulation" /></ac:link></span><span class="plugin_pagetree_children_span"><br /></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573588 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00676: Managing code engineering sets

- page_id: `249573588`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573588/Managing+code+engineering+sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:21:17.300+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Code engineering using API
- labels: ['code-engineering-manager', 'java-code-engineering-manager', 'java-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringManager* class provides static API methods for creating, removing, reversing, generating, and adjusting code-engineering settings. The *CodeEngineeringManager* class is used for managing code engineering sets.

When creating the code engineering set, the following fields are required:

- Language and dialect IDs. All languages and dialects IDs are stored in the com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringConstants class.
- The name of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code engineering set' space=''] .
- The project, where code engineering set will be added.
- The working package.
- The working directory.

New *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringSet* can be created directly via *CodeEngineeringManager*using the *CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String,**Project, Package, java.lang.String)***method.

The language and dialect can be selected from the *CodeEngineeringConstants* class. The dialect is required for DDL and C++ languages.

**•**null *workingPackage*means, that the code engineering working package is *Model* by default

**•**null *workingDirectory*means, that the code engineering working directory is the modeling tool install root

To remove the instance of *CodeEngineeringSet* use the *CodeEngineeringManager.removeCodeEngineeringSet(**CodeEngineeringSet)***method.

All Code Engineering Sets can be retrieved by the *CodeEngineeringManager.getAllCodeEngineeringSets(**Project)***method.

To perform generation of the CodeEngineeringSet object use the *CodeEngineeringManager.generate(**Project**, boolean)***method.

To perform the reverse engineering use the *CodeEngineeringManager.reverse(**Project**, boolean)***method.

###### ****

###### **Languag****e Specific Options**

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.JavaCodeEngineeringManager* provides methods to set Java specific options.These options applie to all code engineering sets in the project.

To add a classpaths for the java code engineering sets, use *JavaCodeEngineeringManager.setJavaClasspath(**Project, java.lang.String[])*

Setting a new classpath will override the old one.

To get applied classpaths for java code engineering sets, use *JavaCodeEngineeringManager.getJavaClasspath(**Project)*.

To resolve collection generics when reversing the java code, *CodeEngineeringManager.setResolveCollectionGenerics(**Project**, boolean)*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <em>CodeEngineeringManager</em> class is used for managing code engineering sets.</p><p>When creating the code engineering set, the following fields are required:</p><ul><li>Language and dialect IDs. All languages and dialects IDs are stored in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringConstants</em> class.</li><li>The name of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Code engineering set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[code engineering set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>The project, where code engineering set will be added.</li><li>The working package.</li><li>The working directory.</li></ul><div class="WordSection1"><p>New <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringSet</em> can be created directly via <em>CodeEngineeringManager </em>using the <em>CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, </em><em>Project, Package, java.lang.String)</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>method.</p><p>The language and dialect can be selected from the <em>CodeEngineeringConstants</em> class. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573488 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00677: Managing compartments

- page_id: `249573488`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573488/Managing+compartments
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T17:35:34.510+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some elements display information in their symbol compartments. For example, a UML Class symbol can contain compartments for attributes, operations, and ports.

Compartments do not have to display all available information. Instead, they can show a subset, while the remaining entries remain hidden. For example, you may choose to display only a few specific operations on a Class symbol in a diagram.

To manage the visibility of compartment entries, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.CompartmentManager*API.

Additional details are available in the JavaDoc.

##### CompartmentManager

```java

```

Utility methods for showing and hiding elements in compartments.

This class provides helper APIs for showing and hiding:

- Model elements within diagram compartments (for example, attributes or slots)
- Element properties rendered on diagram symbols

##### Examples

All diagram modifications must be executed inside a modeling session using *SessionManager*.

###### Example 1: Display a Property in a Compartment

```java
CompartmentManager.showCompartmentElement(
        classView,
        CompartmentID.ATTRIBUTES,
        property
    )
);]]>
```

###### Example 2: Hide a Slot on a Class Shape

```java
CompartmentManager.hideCompartmentElement(
        classView,
        CompartmentID.TAGGED_VALUES_ON_SHAPE,
        slot
    )
);

]]>
```

###### Example 3: Show Element Properties on a Class Shape

```java
CompartmentManager.showCompartmentElementProperties(
        classView,
        Arrays.asList(
            PropertyNames.OWNER,
            PropertyNames.VISIBILITY,
            PropertyNames.APPLIED_STEREOTYPE
        )
    )
);

]]>
```

###### Example 4: Hide an Element Property from a Class Shape

```java
CompartmentManager.hideCompartmentElementProperty(
        classView,
        PropertyNames.IS_ABSTRACT
    )
);]]>
```

##### Code Examples Location

Additional examples demonstrating compartment manipulation can be found in the modeling tool's OpenAPI distribution:

*<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\compartmentedit*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Some elements display information in their symbol compartments. For example, a UML Class symbol can contain compartments for attributes, operations, and ports.</p><p>Compartments do not have to display all available information. Instead, they can show a subset, while the remaining entries remain hidden. For example, you may choose to display only a few specific operations on a Class symbol in a diagram.</p><p>To manage the visibility of compartment entries, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.CompartmentManager </em>API.</p><p>Additional details are available in the JavaDoc.</p><h3>CompartmentManager</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5a1e86b-5f20-431b-834f-25fae79968bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols;

@OpenApiAll
public class CompartmentManager]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Utility methods for showing and hiding elements in compartments.</p><p>This class provides helper APIs for showing and hiding:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f4d43450-0c2b-4836-a389-ef5b85bb9f2e">Model elements within diagram compartments (for example, attributes or slots)</li><li data-uuid="ac2dec4b-7f05-442d-b653-ed2959f42474">Element properties rendered on diagram symbols</li></ul><h3>Examples</h3><p>All diagram modifications must be executed inside a modeling session using <em>SessionManager</em>.</p><h4>Example 1: Display a Property in a Compartment</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc97722c-61b4-4639-b7ed-f7ff6df748f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Property property = ...;   // Property model element
ClassView classView = ...; // Class symbol

SessionManager.getInstance().executeInsideSession(
    Project.getProject(property),
    "Show Property",
    () -> CompartmentManager.showCompartmentElement(
        classView,
        CompartmentID.ATTRIBUTES,
        property
    )
);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example 2: Hide a Slot on a Class Shape</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bb9324a-1e2f-462a-ace0-aba98ac598a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot slot = ...;           // Slot model element
ClassView classView = ...; // Class symbol

SessionManager.getInstance().executeInsideSession(
    Project.getProject(slot),
    "Hide Slot",
    () -> CompartmentManager.hideCompartmentElement(
        classView,
        CompartmentID.TAGGED_VALUES_ON_SHAPE,
        slot
    )
);

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example 3: Show Element Properties on a Class Shape</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb8acac2-a18b-42c5-90b2-de68f05c3f56"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ClassView classView = ...; // Class symbol

SessionManager.getInstance().executeInsideSession(
    Project.getProject(classView),
    "Show Properties",
    () -> CompartmentManager.showCompartmentElementProperties(
        classView,
        Arrays.asList(
            PropertyNames.OWNER,
            PropertyNames.VISIBILITY,
            PropertyNames.APPLIED_STEREOTYPE
        )
    )
);

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example 4: Hide an Element Property from a Class Shape</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7e92fea-5e8b-4d17-9c9f-35fa4b1e515d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ClassView classView = ...; // Class symbol

SessionManager.getInstance().executeInsideSession(
    Project.getProject(classView),
    "Hide Property",
    () -> CompartmentManager.hideCompartmentElementProperty(
        classView,
        PropertyNames.IS_ABSTRACT
    )
);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Code Examples Location</h3><p>Additional examples demonstrating compartment manipulation can be found in the modeling tool's OpenAPI distribution:</p><p><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\compartmentedit</em></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988882 space=DEVG version=19 -->
## PAGE 00678: Managing Diagrams

- page_id: `303988882`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988882/Managing+Diagrams
- version_number: 19
- version_date: `2026-05-01T00:05:40.787+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Overview

In SysML v2, diagrams are used to visualize selected parts of a model for analysis and design.

The diagram content is not defined directly by the diagram itself. Instead, it is driven by underlying modeling concepts:

- ViewDefinition → defines the structure and type of diagram (template)
- ViewUsage → applies the definition to a specific context and determines what content appears
- Diagram → provides the graphical representation of that content

A **diagram is a view**

This separation ensures that:

- Model content is consistent and reusable
- Diagrams remain synchronized with the model
- Visualization is independent from data definition

#### ViewUsage

A [**ViewUsage**](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewUsage.html)is a configured representation of your model.

It controls:

- What elements are shown
- How elements are filtered
- How the content is prepared for display

A **[ViewUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewUsage.html)** is the **source of truth** for diagram content.

#### Diagram Types

[Diagram types](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/dsl/ViewTypes.html) define how the diagram looks.

- General View → General-purpose modeling
- Action Flow View → Flow of actions and processes
- Interconnection View → Connections between parts
- State Transition View → States and transitions

#### Diagram and ViewUsage Relationship

- **ViewUsage** → defines content
- **Diagram** → displays it visually

A diagram:

- Is a visual representation of a ViewUsage
- Always depends on a ViewUsage
- Does not define content itself

#### Diagrams OpenAPI

The `*[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Diagrams](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Diagrams.html)*` class provides operations to:

- Create diagrams
- Retrieve diagrams

##### Create a Diagram

```text

```

##### Get a Diagram

```text

```

#### Key Notes

- **ViewUsage** is the semantic container behind every diagram
- A diagram only visualizes the ViewUsage
- Diagrams are created and retrieved via ViewUsage

#### Summary

- ViewDefinition → template
- ViewUsage → defines content
- Diagram → visual representation

visual representation of a ViewUsage</li><li>Always depends on a ViewUsage</li><li>Does not define content itself</li></ul><h2>Diagrams OpenAPI</h2><p>The <code class="hljs language-markdown"><em><a to:</p><ul><li>Create diagrams</li><li>Retrieve diagrams</li></ul><h3>Create a Diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6b7a394-c397-45e1-aa41-aea875f4485e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Diagram;

````html
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.Diagrams;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.ViewUsage;

ViewUsage viewUsage = Finder.byName().find(rootElement, diagramName, ViewUsage.class);
Diagram diagram = Diagrams.getDiagram(viewUsage);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Key Notes</h2><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewUsage.html"><strong>ViewUsage</strong> </a>is the semantic container behind every diagram</li><li>A <strong>diagram only visualizes</strong> the ViewUsage</li><li>Diagrams are created and retrieved via ViewUsage</li></ul><h2>Summary</h2><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewDefinition.html">ViewDefinition </a>→ template</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ViewUsage.html">ViewUsage </a>→ defines content</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/Diagram.html">Diagram </a>→ visual representation</li></ul><hr />
````

## PAGE 00679: Managing hyperlinks

- page_id: `249573530`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573530/Managing+hyperlinks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-31T16:09:18.603+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Managing navigation in model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML models may have a different kind of hyperlinks assigned to model elements. There is API for managing these hyperlinks - you can assign new hyperlinks, remove existing.

It is also possible to register your own hyperlink handler for some custom hyperlink type.

See the following pages for more information:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>UML models may have a different kind of hyperlinks assigned to model elements. There is API for managing these hyperlinks - you can assign new hyperlinks, remove existing. </p><p>It is also possible to register your own hyperlink handler for some custom hyperlink type.</p><p>See the following pages for more information:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8a88dff7-44c7-44b9-af31-4dfaeca31809" /></p>
````

## PAGE 00680: Managing navigation in model

- page_id: `249573528`
- space_key: `DEVG`

- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API allows to change how navigation in a model is handled. Using that API you can create and manage hyperlinks, change the mouse double-click behavior on elements.

See these pages for more details:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing double-click behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing hyperlinks' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Open API allows to change how navigation in a model is handled. Using that API you can create and manage hyperlinks, change the mouse double-click behavior on elements. </p><p>See these pages for more details:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing double-click behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing hyperlinks" /></ac:link> </li></ul>
````

## PAGE 00681: Managing Presentation Elements

- page_id: `304015206`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/304015206/Managing+Presentation+Elements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-29T18:59:52.350+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **[PresentationElementsManager](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/PresentationElementsManager.html)** provides a centralized Open API for creating, modifying, moving, and deleting diagram presentation elements.

It encapsulates all diagram editing operations behind a command-based mechanism, ensuring that changes are executed within an active editing session and properly tracked by the editing framework.

#### Capabilities

Using this API, you can:

- Create shapes, paths, and annotations
- Update text and geometry of symbols
- Move elements within or across diagram containers
- Remove presentation elements from diagrams

#### Requirements

All operations require:

- A valid parent presentation element associated with a diagram
- An active editing session

#### Accessing the Manager

The manager is exposed as a singleton and should be accessed via:

```text

```

For API details, see: 
[PresentationElementsManager](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/PresentationElementsManager.html)

This API is the primary entry point for programmatic diagram manipulation in SysML v2.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/PresentationElementsManager.html">PresentationElementsManager</a></strong> provides a centralized Open API for creating, modifying, moving, and deleting diagram presentation elements.</p><p>It encapsulates all diagram editing operations behind a command-based mechanism, ensuring that changes are executed within an active editing session and properly tracked by the editing framework.</p><h2>Capabilities</h2><p>Using this API, you can:</p><ul><li>Create shapes, paths, and annotations</li><li>Update text and geometry of symbols</li><li>Move elements within or across diagram containers</li><li>Remove presentation elements from diagrams</li></ul><h2>Requirements</h2><p>All operations require:</p><ul><li>A valid parent presentation element associated with a diagram</li><li>An active editing session</li></ul><h2>Accessing the Manager</h2><p>The manager is exposed as a singleton and should be accessed via:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33e523d4-ac18-40a8-bd94-1a597703a23c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[PresentationElementsManager.getInstance();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For API details, see:<br /><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/PresentationElementsManager.html">PresentationElementsManager</a></p><p>This API is the primary entry point for programmatic diagram manipulation in SysML v2.</p>
````
