data-uuid="78b9ebd0-2208-4725-bd5e-d8084b7f9f4c">Support for undo/redo operations</li></ul><p>The platform provides two approaches for performing model modifications:</p><ul><li data-uuid="555f0f75-7ede-4448-b5c9-293353161ff8">The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing API" /></ac:link></strong> (recommended)</li><li data-uuid="8a6faa8f-8170-41f3-a288-eff55e5fcfd7">The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></strong> (lower-level control)</li></ul><h2><strong>Model Modification Concepts</strong></h2><p>Before modifying a model, it is important to understand the core concepts:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6e52e3ef-58a9-4c4a-8acf-ddbb556d04c9"><strong>Commands</strong><br />Each modification is recorded as a command for undo/redo</li><li data-uuid="f8a6fe49-bb66-4abe-8686-40f2bfb09d2e"><strong>Command History</strong><br />Tracks executed changes and enables rollback</li><li data-uuid="aa6f542a-fc4a-44d3-80a8-a51b427a1272"><strong>Sessions</strong><br />Define the scope in which model changes are applied</li></ul><p>These mechanisms ensure that all changes are safe, traceable, and reversible.</p><h2><strong>Choosing an API</strong></h2><h3>Use the Editing API (Recommended)</h3><ul><li data-uuid="a3809323-e0af-43dc-bcc0-3921e79ce177">Automatically manages commands and command history</li><li data-uuid="87ae7100-1125-437c-b308-9033bea55216">Simpler and safer to use</li><li data-uuid="d637413b-5c77-449f-a66d-93d601d6e759">Best for most plugin and automation scenarios</li></ul><p>See: <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing API" /></ac:link></strong></p><h3>Use SessionManager (Advanced)</h3><ul><li data-uuid="30b5f33d-d8ed-4d95-8773-761edab7bcc2">Provides manual control over sessions</li><li data-uuid="3124b350-1d7b-4727-94c6-1be7503a42a4">Requires explicit session lifecycle management</li><li data-uuid="967ab4d7-c272-4bef-83de-ab7bdfa68e49">Useful for legacy integrations or specialized workflows</li></ul><p>See: <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></strong></p><h2><strong>Example Comparison</strong></h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb723b17-8711-4e6f-955d-d4733302f21a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing;
````html
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager;

Editing editing = Editing.getInstance(project);

// Recommended approach (Editing API)
editing.execute("Create element", () -> {
    // model changes here
});
 
// Manual approach (SessionManager)
SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Edit");
try {
    // model changes here
} finally {
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Key Takeaways</strong></h2><ul><li data-uuid="c665fedb-401c-4c70-9371-0ca2697abe01">All model changes must occur within a controlled execution context</li><li data-uuid="ec42f09d-49c7-421b-9d10-546ef63a3862">Prefer the <strong>Editing API</strong> for most use cases</li><li data-uuid="996c2c82-75f9-46fb-831a-965763a01d11">Use <strong>SessionManager</strong> only when you need explicit control</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180527 space=DEVG version=8 -->
## PAGE 00690: Model Report Example

- page_id: `314180527`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/314180527/Model+Report+Example
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-06-19T20:20:23.613+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Python Integration Guide > Running the Python Open API Example
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to export a SysML v2 model from Cameo Systems Modeler into an Excel file using the Python Open API.

The exporter traverses the model hierarchy and writes a structured representation of elements into a formatted Excel spreadsheet.

#### Overview

*export_model_to_excel.py*script does the following:

- Traverses the model hierarchy starting from the root namespace
- Extracts key element properties (ID, type, name)
- Preserves hierarchical structure (nested elements)
- Writes results into a formatted Excel file

The export maintains the logical structure of the model, including deep package nesting levels.

#### Running the export

Run the main script from the project root:

```bash

```

The export is triggered from the main script:

```java

```

##### Output

The script generates or overwrites:

```text

```

If the file already exists, it will be overwritten automatically.

Ensure the Excel file is closed before running the export.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page describes how to export a SysML v2 model from Cameo Systems Modeler into an Excel file using the Python Open API.</p><p>The exporter traverses the model hierarchy and writes a structured representation of elements into a formatted Excel spreadsheet.</p><h2>Overview</h2><p><em>export_model_to_excel.py </em>script does the following:</p><ul><li>Traverses the model hierarchy starting from the root namespace</li><li>Extracts key element properties (ID, type, name)</li><li>Preserves hierarchical structure (nested elements)</li><li>Writes results into a formatted Excel file</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bef7d866-0047-43d5-be3f-1029469fce26"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The export maintains the logical structure of the model, including deep package nesting levels.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Running the export</h2><p>Run the main script from the project root:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2baf1c44-e494-4155-a404-147e41e6f9da"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[python run_cameo_drone_example.py
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The export is triggered from the main script:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b0d215b-3387-4a0a-90b1-25661ad56d9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[def execute_excel_export():
    logger.info("Executing model export...")
    exporter = ExcelModelExporter(project=active_project)
    exporter.export_to_file("SysML_Model_Export.xlsx")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Output</h3><p>The script generates or overwrites:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afbf41b9-78d1-49c1-a6ac-574180266c1f"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[src/examples/SysML_Model_Export.xlsx
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05a7d6ff-9dde-4cb8-a0ee-b265bbb09b29"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the file already exists, it will be overwritten automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5664a21-a08f-4d53-bf22-89f85ad7605b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Ensure the Excel file is closed before running the export.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015245 space=DEVG version=9 -->
## PAGE 00691: Model Traversal

- page_id: `304015245`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/304015245/Model+Traversal
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-05-01T03:55:48.771+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model traversal refers to navigating through model elements and relationships to inspect, collect, modify, or analyze model data.

Traversal can be implemented in multiple ways depending on the structure of the model and the type of relationships being explored.

#### Common Traversal Approaches

Model traversal is not limited to a single strategy.

Common traversal approaches include:

- Ownership traversal
- Relationship traversal

#### Ownership-Based Traversal

Ownership traversal is one of the most common methods. Ownership traversal allows navigation through hierarchical model structures.

Key APIs :[element.getOwnedElement()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#getOwnedElement())

```java

```

#### Traversing Owned Relationships

Ownership relationships represent containment hierarchy. Relationships themselves can be traversed directly.

Key APIs : [element.getOwnedRelationship()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#getOwnedRelationship())

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Model traversal refers to navigating through model elements and relationships to inspect, collect, modify, or analyze model data.</p><p>Traversal can be implemented in multiple ways depending on the structure of the model and the type of relationships being explored.</p><h2><span>Common Traversal Approaches</span></h2><p><span>Model traversal is not limited to a single strategy.</span></p><p><span>Common traversal approaches include:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="746efae0-6f6b-4575-b50a-46d31a3231e0"><span>Ownership traversal</span></li><li data-uuid="347812da-f43d-4f41-96c2-e8a945c995d0"><span>Relationship traversal</span></li></ul><h2><span>Ownership-Based Traversal</span></h2><p><span>Ownership traversal is one of the most common methods. Ownership traversal allows navigation through hierarchical model structures.</span></p><p><span>Key APIs : </span><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#getOwnedElement()">element.getOwnedElement()</a></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="751785f8-e23b-41cf-80ad-86e7e5c1e4b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;

public void traverseOwnedElements(Element element)
{
	System.out.println(element.getName());

	for (Element child : element.getOwnedElement())
	{
		traverseOwnedElements(child);
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><span>Traversing Owned Relationships</span></h2><p><span>Ownership relationships represent containment hierarchy. Relationships themselves can be traversed directly.</span></p><p><span>Key APIs : <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#getOwnedRelationship()">element.getOwnedRelationship()</a></span></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41a8d9ae-0057-4b05-af4c-3b678b1de430"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship;

public void traverseRelationships(Element element)
{
	for (Relationship relationship : element.getOwnedRelationship())
	{
		System.out.println(relationship.getName());
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573428 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00692: Model traversing, Visitor pattern

- page_id: `249573428`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573428/Model+traversing+Visitor+pattern
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:59:03.676+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Here is an example of how to collect all children from the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* and avoid recursion using a simple *for*cycle:

```java
children = new ArrayList(); 
    Element current = ... 
    children.add(current);
    // if the current element has children, list will be increased. 
    for (int i = 0; i < children.size(); i++)
    {
        current = children.get(i);
        // add all children into end of this list, so it emulates a recursion. 
        children.addAll(current.getOwnedElement());
    }]]>
```

It is highly recommended to use more advanced collecting methods from *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder* utility:

- byScope()
- byType()
- byTypeRecursively()

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Here is an example of how to collect all children from the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> and avoid recursion using a simple <em>for </em>cycle:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1480f664-32bf-4755-aa6c-63eb86e14427"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List<Element> children = new ArrayList<Element>(); 
    Element current = ... 
    children.add(current);
    // if the current element has children, list will be increased. 
    for (int i = 0; i < children.size(); i++)
    {
        current = children.get(i);
        // add all children into end of this list, so it emulates a recursion. 
        children.addAll(current.getOwnedElement());
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><p>It is highly recommended to use more advanced collecting methods from <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder</em> utility:</p><ul><li><em>byScope()</em></li><li><em>byType()</em></li><li><em>byTypeRecursively()</em></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d87af5a4-da9b-49c0-aab8-6991bc3cdde8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="674518bd-42f1-4fec-be9d-51cc04f7a18e"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=309365577 space=DEVG version=6 -->
## PAGE 00693: Model Validation

- page_id: `309365577`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/309365577/Model+Validation
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-06-23T10:49:22.868+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways to create custom validation rules and validation suites in SysML v2:

- Model-based validation – validation rules and suites are defined directly in the model.
- Plugin-based validation – validation rules are implemented in Java by using the SysML v2 Open API and registered through a plugin.

If your validation requirements can be expressed in the model, the model-based approach is typically the simplest solution because it does not require plugin development or deployment.

For information about creating model-based validation suites and rules, see:

- Custom validation suites and rules

For information about creating plugin-based validation suites and rules by using the Open API, see:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Custom Validation Rules and Suites' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running Validation and Inspecting Results' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are two ways to create custom validation rules and validation suites in SysML v2:</p><ul><li><strong>Model-based validation</strong> – validation rules and suites are defined directly in the model.</li><li><strong>Plugin-based validation</strong> – validation rules are implemented in Java by using the SysML v2 Open API and registered through a plugin.</li></ul><p>If your validation requirements can be expressed in the model, the model-based approach is typically the simplest solution because it does not require plugin development or deployment.</p><p>For information about creating model-based validation suites and rules, see:</p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Custom-validation-suites-and-rules&amp;version=latest">Custom validation suites and rules</a></li></ul><p>For information about creating plugin-based validation suites and rules by using the Open API, see:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5ddb94b0-f814-4b3b-bf35-b20dcea8c974"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Custom Validation Rules and Suites" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d739540f-8d0f-4ca2-bdc2-8b727084bf2b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Running Validation and Inspecting Results" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573433 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00694: ModelHierarchyVisitor

- page_id: `249573433`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573433/ModelHierarchyVisitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:15:41.228+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with UML model > Model traversing, Visitor pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor* is an enhanced implementation of the *Visitor*pattern and a subclass of *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelVisitor*, used for visiting model elements.

Every *visit<element_metatype>*method calls a *visit<element_metatype>*method for a super type.

Model elements *visit…*methods have an additional context parameter of the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.VisitorContext* type. The visitor context is used to track what super type *visit<element_metatype> m*ethods were already visited (to avoid multiple visits because some model elements have a multiple inheritance). Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.

For example, you can put some code into *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor.visitClassifier(**Classifier)***and it will be executed for all subclasses of a *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier*.

```java
it = root.getOwnedElement().iterator();
    while (it.hasNext())
    {
        it.next().accept(myVisitor);
    }]]>
```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\statistics*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor</em> is an enhanced implementation of the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Visitor </em>pattern and a subclass of <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelVisitor</em>, used for visiting model elements. </p><p>Every <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; </em>method calls a <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; </em>method for a super type.</p><p>Model elements <em>visit… </em>methods have an additional context parameter of the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.VisitorContext</em> type. The visitor context is used to track what super type <em>visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; m</em>ethods were already visited (to avoid multiple visits because some model elements have a multiple inheritance). Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.</p><p><br /></p><p>For example, you can put some code into <em>com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor.visitClassifier(</em><em>Classifier)</em><em> </em>and it will be executed for all subclasses of a <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7085da27-f3ae-415f-9197-30427949c8f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ModelVisitor myVisitor = new ModelHierarchyVisitor()
    {
        public void visitClassifier(Classifier element, VisitorContext context)
        {
          	//this is my classifier
        }
    };
    Project project = ...;
    Package root = project.getPrimaryModel(); 
    Iterator<Element> it = root.getOwnedElement().iterator();
    while (it.hasNext())
    {
        it.next().accept(myVisitor);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\statistics</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573320 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00695: Modeling Tools Developer Guide

- page_id: `249573320`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573320/Modeling+Tools+Developer+Guide
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-28T15:10:33.458+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the generated javadoc ([https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/)), you will find a detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. JavaDocs are located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\docs*.

This guide describes the Open Java API of MagicDraw or a Cameo Suite product, such as Cameo Systems Modeler, and provides instructions on how to implement custom plugins, add actions to the menus or toolbars, change UML model elements as well as create new patterns. Though MagicDraw is used in examples mainly, other modeling tools, since they are based on the MagicDraw modeling platform, perform the same.

The program core code and used libraries are packaged in jars files that are located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\lib* (and its subdirectories).

Some plugins also exposes Open API. Plugins libraries can be located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\plugins\<plugin_name>*

The code can be divided into the following three API scope groups:

- OpenAPI – a code for public usage, stable through builds and versions.
- InternalAPI – a code for the internal NoMagic usage only, may change through builds and versions without any restrictions.
- NotAPI – not an API code, it may change in each build.

Java annotations are used to distinguish which API scope in the provided code belongs to. Annotations of the type (class, interface, enum) declaration:

| Annotation | API Scope | Scope applies to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @OpenApiAll | OpenAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |
| @OpenApi | OpenAPI | The annotated type declaration only. Each declared member, constructor is annotated explicitly with the @OpenApi or @InternalApi annotation (see the following table). |
| @InternalApi | InternalAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |
| @NotApi | NotAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |

Annotations of the member, constructor declaration of the @OpenApi type:

| Annotation | API Scope | Scope applies to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @OpenApi | OpenAPI | The annotated declared member or constructor. |
| @InternalApi | InternalAPI | The annotated declared member or constructor. |

Only code from the OpenAPI scope should be used to extend the modeling tool. All the InternalAPI and NotAPI scope code are additionally annotated with the *@Deprecated* annotation. This allows seeing the illegal API usage in all modern IDEs without an additional configuration.

We provide a set of plugin samples in *<*modeling tool installation directory*>\openapi\examples*. By using these examples, you can find out on how to use an OpenAPI.

In the generated javadoc, you will find the detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. JavaDocs are located in *<*modeling tool installation directory*>\openapi\docs*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

JavaDocs are located in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\docs</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This guide describes the Open Java API of MagicDraw or a Cameo Suite product, such as Cameo Systems Modeler, and provides instructions on how to implement custom plugins, add actions to the menus or toolbars, change UML model elements as well as create new patterns. Plugins libraries can be located in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\plugins\&lt;plugin_name&gt;</em></p><p> The code can be divided into the following three API scope groups:</p><ul><li><strong>OpenAPI </strong>– a code for public usage, stable through builds and versions.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">InternalAPI </strong>– a code for the internal NoMagic usage only, may change through builds and versions without any restrictions.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">NotAPI </strong>– not an API code, it may change in each build.</li></ul><p>Java annotations are used to distinguish which API scope in the provided code belongs to. This allows seeing the illegal API usage in all modern IDEs without an additional configuration.</p><p>We provide a set of plugin samples in <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="eeeaea8f-b523-4d12-a6f1-a68f5661d9d1">&lt;<em>modeling tool installation directory</em>&gt;\openapi\examples</ac:inline-comment-marker></em>. By using these examples, you can find out on how to use an OpenAPI.</p><p>In the generated javadoc, you will find the detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015230 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00696: Moving Shape Elements

- page_id: `304015230`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/304015230/Moving+Shape+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T20:58:32.703+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Managing Presentation Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The [`PresentationElementsManager`](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/PresentationElementsManager.html) class provides Open API methods for moving presentation elements within diagrams.

Elements can be moved in two ways:

- Changing their location within the same parent
- Moving them to a different parent (which may also change their model ownership)

#### Move an Element to a New Location

Use this method to reposition a shape without changing its parent.

```text

```

#### Move an Element to a Different Parent

Use this method to move an element into another parent at a specific location.

```text

```

#### Move Multiple Elements

You can also move multiple elements at once.

```text
elements = ...;
PresentationElement newParent = ...;
Point newLocation = new Point(200, 150);

PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().movePresentationElements(elements, newParent, newLocation);
]]>
```

#### Notes

- The target location must be specified.
- Elements and parent must belong to a diagram.
- The parent must be editable.
- Moving to a different parent may also change the underlying model element ownership.
- If the model is read-only, only the visual position is updated.
- All move operations must be performed inside an active editing session.
- Movement operations are executed through commands managed by the editing framework.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/diagram/PresentationElementsManager.html"><code>PresentationElementsManager</code></a> class provides Open API methods for moving presentation elements within diagrams.</p><p>Elements can be moved in two ways:</p><ul><li>Changing their <strong>location</strong> within the same parent</li><li>Moving them to a <strong>different parent</strong> (which may also change their model ownership)</li></ul><h2>Move an Element to a New Location</h2><p>Use this method to reposition a shape without changing its parent.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d976492b-b476-4659-a4f6-7c2acdc07768"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PresentationElementsManager;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

import java.awt.Point;

PresentationElement element = ...;
Point newLocation = new Point(200, 150);

PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(element, newLocation);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Move an Element to a Different Parent</h2><p>Use this method to move an element into another parent at a specific location.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c52de31-a433-44bd-9500-7bca58bf3f2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PresentationElementsManager;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

import java.awt.Point;

PresentationElement element = ...;
PresentationElement newParent = ...;
Point newLocation = new Point(200, 150);

PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(element, newParent, newLocation);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Move Multiple Elements</h2><p>You can also move multiple elements at once.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2685a936-4e49-4b87-adbc-04a7969e54b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.diagram.PresentationElementsManager;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement;

import java.awt.Point;
import java.util.List;

List<PresentationElement> elements = ...;
PresentationElement newParent = ...;
Point newLocation = new Point(200, 150);

PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().movePresentationElements(elements, newParent, newLocation);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Notes</h2><ul><li>The target location must be specified.</li><li>Elements and parent must belong to a diagram.</li><li>The parent must be editable.</li><li>Moving to a different parent may also change the underlying model element ownership.</li><li>If the model is read-only, only the visual position is updated.</li><li>All move operations must be performed inside an active editing session.</li><li>Movement operations are executed through commands managed by the editing framework.</li></ul>
````

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To move a symbol on the diagram to another location, you can use the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56154ac-0627-4845-9653-ee18d51a0f3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElementManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(PresentationElement view, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17822cd1-f81b-4e4a-953a-f7e105178d09"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElement view = ...;

    SessionsManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Similarly, a group of symbols can be moved by using the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab01b1f8-334a-42da-9009-3d61172a088e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    movePresentationElements(List<PresentationElement> views, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolmove</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b483f3d-c1a6-45f7-9612-a04919e10de5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
