<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573321 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00715: Plugins

- page_id: `249573321`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573321/Plugins
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:53.913+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General
- labels: ['plugin', 'plug-in']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Plugins are the only one way to change the functionality of a modeling tool. The main purpose of the plugin architecture is to add a new functionality to your modeling tool. Although there is a limited ability to remove an existing functionality using plugins. 
 A plugin must contain the following resources:

- A directory

- Compiled java files, packaged into a jar file

- A plugin descriptor file

- Optional files used by the plugin

Typically a plugin creates some GUI components allowing a user to use the plugin functionality. Generally, this is not necessary because the plugin can listen for some changes in a project and activate itself on the desired changes.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Plugins are the only one way to change the functionality of a modeling tool. The main purpose of the plugin architecture is to add a new functionality to your modeling tool. Although there is a limited ability to remove an existing functionality using plugins. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> A plugin must contain the following resources:</p><ul><li>A directory</li></ul><ul><li>Compiled java files, packaged into a jar file</li></ul><ul><li>A plugin descriptor file</li></ul><ul><li>Optional files used by the plugin</li></ul><p>Typically a plugin creates some GUI components allowing a user to use the plugin functionality. Generally, this is not necessary because the plugin can listen for some changes in a project and activate itself on the desired changes.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cafabac-b175-4310-8c9b-9d91fca265fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="aeea49da-d2c5-448f-972b-cc3702986582"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573338 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00716: Plugins directories

- page_id: `249573338`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573338/Plugins+directories
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:20.343+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins > Detail information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Plugins are loaded from two locations on the program startup.

##### **Installation directory**

Plugins are loaded from the program installation directory (a global plugin directory) *<modeling tool installation directory>\plugins*.

This directory can be changed using a *md.plugins.dir* java system property and specifying paths separated with a semicolon (;) symbol.

To change the location of the plugins directory

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dmd.plugins.dir="absolute path to plugins directory1;absolute path to plugins directory2" Sample property value-Dmd.plugins.dir="C:\Program Files\MagicDraw\plugins;D:\MyPlugins" If you are loading plugins from a custom location, make sure the location contains extracted plugin files. Compressed plugin files (*.zip*) cannot be loaded.Besides the plugin itself, any other files (profiles, modelLibraries, samples, etc.) contained in the plugin *.zip* file are not added to the installation directory like they are when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL'].

##### **Configuration files location**

Additionally, plugins are loaded from a configuration files location (*<Configuration files location>\plugins*). This allows to have global and user plugins.

See “MagicDraw Configuration Files Location” in the MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.

Another issue on Unix systems is related to user permissions to write. If a modeling tool is installed in a root, user is not allowed to write in a global plugin directory if a user has not such permissions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Plugins are loaded from two locations on the program startup.</p><h3><strong>Installation directory</strong></h3><p>Plugins are loaded from the program installation directory (a global plugin directory) <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\plugins</em>.</p><p>This directory can be changed using a <em>md.plugins.dir</em> java system property and specifying paths separated with a semicolon (;) symbol.</p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To change the location of the plugins directory</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em> file, which is located in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property:</p><pre>-Dmd.plugins.dir=&quot;absolute path to plugins directory1;absolute path to plugins directory2&quot;</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce50ee8e-88d6-4372-a529-306178a57c78"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample property value</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>-Dmd.plugins.dir=&quot;C:\Program Files\MagicDraw\plugins;D:\MyPlugins&quot;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4fd1934-8587-47f7-8473-59f432781e76"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you are loading plugins from a custom location, make sure the location contains extracted plugin files. Compressed plugin files (<em>.zip</em>) cannot be loaded.</li><li>Besides the plugin itself, any other files (profiles, modelLibraries, samples, etc.) contained in the plugin <em>.zip</em> file are not added to the installation directory like they are when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installing a resource]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3><strong>Configuration files location<br /></strong></h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Additionally, plugins are loaded from a configuration files location (<em>&lt;Configuration files location&gt;\plugins</em>). This allows to have global and user plugins. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2852841e-5fcc-44bf-93d4-82d06a41d199"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> See “MagicDraw Configuration Files Location” in the MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Another issue on Unix systems is related to user permissions to write. If a modeling tool is installed in a root, user is not allowed to write in a global plugin directory if a user has not such permissions.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573607 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00717: Plugins migration

- page_id: `249573607`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573607/Plugins+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:13.646+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

We are keeping Open API stable as much as possible, but in some cases API changes in a newer application version. For example this happens if support for a new UML version is added or some very serious refactoring is done in components.

Sometimes it is enough to recompile plugin code with new libraries and adjust some minor API changes. Sometimes more serious migration is needed.

We recommend to recompile a plugin with new libraries to see the incompatibilities if any.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For example this happens if support for a new UML version is added or some very serious refactoring is done in components.</p><p>Sometimes it is enough to recompile plugin code with new libraries and adjust some minor API changes. Sometimes more serious migration is needed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2de9e02f-14fe-487a-90bf-e89620726db5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We recommend to recompile a plugin with new libraries to see the incompatibilities if any.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6effda46-6001-4f01-b36f-045a9ad9dab4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ac344481-bbee-4176-81b9-ee4c059195f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573620 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00718: Plugins migration to MagicDraw 15.0 and later Open API

- page_id: `249573620`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573620/Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+15.0+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:39.525+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API in version 15.0 and later versions has changed together with changes in the UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification. You should read this chapter if you want to migrate your plugin created for earlier MagicDraw version.

Thereby we made a cleaner UML metamodel implementation API after the UML specification has been updated. There are no big changes in Open API, so migration will not be a long and complicate task for you.

The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML metamodel changes' space=''] .
- Some deprecated methods have been removed:

| Removed deprecated method | Substitution |
| --- | --- |
| BaseElement.getProject() | Project.getProject(BaseElement) |
| MainFrame.getDialogParent() | MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent() |
| MainFrame.setDialogParent(Frame) | MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().setDialogParent(Frame) |
| ElementListProperty.setStereotype(Stereotype) | ElementListProperty.setSelectableRestrictedElements(Collection) |

- Changes in libraries jars have been made. Open API classes now are packaged into md_api.jar and md_common_api.jar (was md.jar and md_common.jar ). If your plugin was using API classes from some program build-in plugin, a plugin jar is also renamed with a “ ProgramInitials_api.jar ” pattern.
- Changes in package names for build-in plugins have been made. In earlier program versions, some build-in plugins exposed their Open API, for example, patterns, transformations, emf, and etc. We have changed a package name pattern. Earlier API versions used a com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl. **** pattern, now these plugins are moved to com.nomagic.magicdraw.*.*, for example com.nomagic.magicdraw.patterns.* . A simple import statement change in your java source will solve this migration issue.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Open API in version 15.0 and later versions has changed together with changes in the UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification. You should read this chapter if you want to migrate your plugin created for earlier MagicDraw version. </p><p>Thereby we made a cleaner UML metamodel implementation API after the UML specification has been updated. There are no big changes in Open API, so migration will not be a long and complicate task for you.</p><p>The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML metamodel changes" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Some<strong> deprecated methods</strong> have been removed:</li></ul><table><thead><tr><th><div class="tablesorter-header-inner"><p><strong>Removed deprecated method</strong></p></div></th><th><div class="tablesorter-header-inner"><p><strong>Substitution</strong></p></div></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><pre>BaseElement.getProject()</pre></td><td><pre>Project.getProject(BaseElement)</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>MainFrame.getDialogParent()</pre></td><td><pre>MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent()</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>MainFrame.setDialogParent(Frame)</pre></td><td><pre>MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().setDialogParent(Frame)</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>ElementListProperty.setStereotype(Stereotype)</pre></td><td><pre>ElementListProperty.setSelectableRestrictedElements(Collection)</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><ul><li>Changes in<strong> libraries jars</strong> have been made. Open API classes now are packaged into <em>md_api.jar </em>and <em>md_common_api.jar </em>(was <em>md.jar </em>and <em>md_common.jar</em>). If your plugin was using API classes from some program build-in plugin, a plugin jar is also renamed with a “<em>ProgramInitials_api.jar</em>” pattern.</li><li>Changes in<strong> package names for build-in plugins</strong> have been made. In earlier program versions, some build-in plugins exposed their Open API, for example, patterns, transformations, emf, and etc. We have changed a package name pattern. Earlier API versions used a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.</em><strong><em>**</em> </strong>pattern, now these plugins are moved to<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.*.*,</em> for example <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.patterns.*</em>. A simple import statement change in your java source will solve this migration issue.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93c2742d-0888-468c-8234-5dfbb95f3a8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="90dfd71b-5ead-4567-bfcc-bb3acf2ac4f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573612 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00719: Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API

- page_id: `249573612`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573612/Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+17.0.1+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:56:12.640+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you are migrating from earlier than MagicDraw 15.0 version, please read the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugins migration to MagicDraw 15.0 and later Open API' space=''] chapter first.

The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:

- UML metamodel changes . UML 2.4.1 specification introduced few changes in the UML metamodel itself. Some metamodel classes were added, some of them were removed, some metaclasses properties were changed. Most of these changes are not in a core UML, so they will affect you if your plugin is oriented to complex things in UML. For the details of changes, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project (decomposition) structure API changes' space=''].
- Changes in methods:
  - ***com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.ProjectOptionsConfigurator*.**The method *afterLoad(**ProjectOptions)* has been added. An empty implementation of this method must be added to a custom ProjectOptionsConfigurator implementation.
  - ***com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener*.**The following methods have been added:

*projectActivatedFromGUI(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectActivatedFromGUI-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectCreated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectCreated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectOpenedFromGUI(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectOpenedFromGUI-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreActivated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreActivated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreClosed(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreClosed-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreDeActivated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreDeActivated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreReplaced(Project[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreReplaced-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreReplaced-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreSaved(Project, boolean)*

*projectPreClosedFinal(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreClosedFinal-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

An empty implementation of these methods must be added to the custom *ProjectEventListener*implementation. The code change is not required, if *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListenerAdapter* is extended.

- Changes in libraries jars have been made. New jar files have been introduced in the lib folder. Do not forget to update your [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compilation classpath' space=''] .

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

The code change is not required, if <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListenerAdapter</em> is extended.</p><ul><li>Changes in<strong> libraries jars</strong> have been made. New jar files have been introduced in the <em>lib</em> folder. Do not forget to update your <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Compilation classpath" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[classpath]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5df8faa5-3def-442a-8382-aa7a0747971a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9adf068f-208c-4ef8-a1d4-873e19f5d28d"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573610 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00720: Plugins migration to MagicDraw 18.0 and later Open API

- page_id: `249573610`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573610/Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+18.0+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:15.101+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Open API Changes

**UML 2.5 support related changes**

- UML metamodel interfaces have been adjusted to UML 2.5 support related changes.
- c om.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL2 and com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL3 classes have been merged into com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile.

**UI refactoring related changes**

UI of diagrams' windows management, symbols' compartments management, and elements creation in compartments have been refactored. As a consequence, some actions have been removed from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID* interface and new actions have been added.

IDs of the removed actions are as follows:

- ActionsGroups.NEXT_DIAGRAM_RELATED
- ActionsGroups.PREVIOUS_DIAGRAM_RELATED
- ActionsID.DIAGRAM_NAVIGATION_GROUP
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_ATTRIBUTE
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_EXTENSION
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_LITERAL
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERAND
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERATION
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_PORT
- ActionsID.LINK_EDITING
- ActionsID.METRICS
- ActionsID.OPEN_IN_NEW_TAB_ACTION
- ActionsID.OPEN_LAST_ActionsID.RECENT_DIAGRAMS
- ActionsID.REDO_LIST
- ActionsID.UNDO_LIST

**Project Merge related changes**

Types of parameters have been changed from Set to Collection, because of performance related improvements in methods of the following interfaces:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Change
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.MacroChange
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.RelatedChange
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.RelatedMacroChange

**Structured expressions related changes**

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.expressions.ExpressionHelper*class has been added. It provides utility methods to use in queries defined with the StructuredExpression language (used by smart packages, derived properties in DSL, and so forth).

**UML Interactions related changes**

The *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper* class has been added. It provides utility methods to access Interactions part of UML metamodel.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3 class="heading">Open API Changes</h3><p><strong>UML 2.5 support related changes</strong></p><ul><li>UML metamodel interfaces have been adjusted to UML 2.5 support related changes. </li><li>c<em>om.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL2</em> and <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL3</em> classes have been merged into <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile.</em></li></ul><p><strong>UI refactoring related changes</strong></p><p>UI of diagrams' windows management, symbols' compartments management, and elements creation in compartments have been refactored. As a consequence, some actions have been removed from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID</em> interface and new actions have been added.</p><p>IDs of the removed actions are as follows:</p><ul><li>ActionsGroups.NEXT_DIAGRAM_RELATED</li><li>ActionsGroups.PREVIOUS_DIAGRAM_RELATED</li><li>ActionsID.DIAGRAM_NAVIGATION_GROUP</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_ATTRIBUTE</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_EXTENSION</li><li> ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_LITERAL</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERAND</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERATION</li><li> ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_PORT</li><li>ActionsID.LINK_EDITING</li><li>ActionsID.METRICS</li><li> ActionsID.OPEN_IN_NEW_TAB_ACTION</li><li>ActionsID.OPEN_LAST_ActionsID.RECENT_DIAGRAMS</li><li> ActionsID.REDO_LIST</li><li>ActionsID.UNDO_LIST</li></ul><p><strong>Project Merge related changes</strong></p><p>Types of parameters have been changed from Set to Collection, because of performance related improvements in methods of the following interfaces:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Change</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.MacroChange</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.RelatedChange</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.RelatedMacroChange</em></li></ul><p><strong>Structured expressions related changes</strong></p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.expressions.ExpressionHelper </em>class has been added. It provides utility methods to use in queries defined with the StructuredExpression language (used by smart packages, derived properties in DSL, and so forth).</p><p><strong>UML Interactions related changes</strong></p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper</em> class has been added. It provides utility methods to access Interactions part of UML metamodel.</p>
````

## PAGE 00722: Plugins migration to MagicDraw 18.2 and later Open API

- page_id: `249573608`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573608/Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+18.2+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:36.740+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins migration

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

## PAGE 00723: Presentation element selection events

- page_id: `249573506`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573506/Presentation+element+selection+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:01.492+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Diagram events
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to register a *java.beans.PropertyChangeListener* on a project. Selection changes fire *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent*.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to register a <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeListener</em> on a project. Selection changes fire <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82b2b534-023d-437c-bc7f-b110a6e43dcc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Project project = ;

Listener listener = new Listener();
SelectionProvider selectionProvider = SelectionProvider.getInstance(project);
selectionProvider.addSelectionChangedListener(listener);

private static class Listener implements SelectionProvider.SelectionChangedListener
{
	@Override
	public void selectionChanged(SelectionProvider.SelectionChangedEvent event)
	{
		// was selected
		SelectionProvider.Selection oldSelection = event.getOldSelection();
		
		// now selected
		SelectionProvider.Selection newSelection = event.getNewSelection();
		
		// do something
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573462 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00724: Presentation elements (symbols)

- page_id: `249573462`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573462/Presentation+elements+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:50:47.707+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams
- labels: ['path-connector', 'setter', 'set', 'sset']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The UML semantic defines only the UML metamodel. The modeling tool has its own structure of classes for UML elements representation in the diagram. The base class of this structure is *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement*. A presentation element is a textual or graphical presentation of one or more model elements.

Presentation elements are sometimes called symbols also.

In the metamodel, a *PresentationElement* is the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement* that presents a set of model elements to a user. It is the base for all metaclasses used for the presentation. All other metaclasses with this purpose are indirect subclasses of *PresentationElement*.

A current version of Open API provides just a basic structure of presentation elements:

Every presentation element can have children. For example, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement* has a collection of inner presentation elements. *PresentationElement* of some *Package* can have a collection of presentation elements for inner *Package* elements.

Current version *PresentationElement* API allows you to:

- Access element bounds PresentationElement.getBounds()
- Access a model com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element of a presentation element PresentationElement.getElement() . A presentation element can have no Element , for example, a com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.TextBoxView .
- Access children PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()
- Access properties of the element PresentationElement.getProperty(java.lang.String) , PresentationElement.getPropertyManager(). The sample of properties would be the Suppress Operations property for a class presentation element, the Autosize property for any com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement .
- Select/unselect or access a selection state of the presentation element ( PresentationElement.isSelected() , PresentationElement.setSelected(boolean) ) .

A subclass of presentation elements *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathConnector* provides information about connected paths to the presentation element. To get a collection of connected paths to the presentation element, use the method **PathConnector*.getConnectedPathElements()*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

All other metaclasses with this purpose are indirect subclasses of <em>PresentationElement</em>.</p><p>A current version of Open API provides just a basic structure of presentation elements:</p><div class="WordSection1"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PresentationElements.png" /></ac:image><br /><p><br /></p><p>Every presentation element can have children. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573505 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00725: Presentation elements modification events

- page_id: `249573505`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573505/Presentation+elements+modification+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:29:08.577+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Diagram events
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to all diagram changes in a single adapter:

1. The listener receives events from all opened diagrams.
2. The adapter delegates these events to your own listener.

The adapter works in a project scope.

****

To listen to diagram change events, you need to create the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramListenerAdapter* object, that is, to pass the property change listener as a delegator, which receives all events.

To create the *DiagramListenerAdapter* object, call:

```java

```

The *DiagramListenerAdapter* object is registered for a project on its creation.

To start using the adapter, install it. Uninstall it when it is no longer necessary, that is, you do not need to receive any events about diagram changes anymore.

Property names related to presentation elements modification are defined in *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ExtendedPropertyNames***class*.*

**Example: Listening to symbol creation / removal**

```java

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to all diagram changes in a single adapter:</p><ol><li>The listener receives events from all <em>opened </em>diagrams.</li><li>The adapter delegates these events to your own listener.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04930027-ff28-4880-92a6-0dd1eb6571da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The adapter works in a project scope.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>            </strong></p><p>To listen to diagram change events, you need to create the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object, that is, to pass the property change listener as a delegator, which receives all events.</p><p>To create the <em>DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object, call:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e521b8d-0412-4726-a1e6-ae12e9ae463f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    new DiagramListenerAdapter(propertyChangeListener)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <em>DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object is registered for a project on its creation.</p><p>To start using the adapter, install it. Uninstall it when it is no longer necessary, that is, you do not need to receive any events about diagram changes anymore.</p><p><br /></p><p>Property names related to presentation elements modification are defined in <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ExtendedPropertyNames</em><em> </em>class<em>.</em></p><p><strong>Example: Listening to symbol creation / removal</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ed5ff09-ede8-4f7d-bc3f-956244a75f95"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    DiagramListenerAdapter adapter = new DiagramListenerAdapter(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            String propertyName = evt.getPropertyName();
            if (ExtendedPropertyNames.VIEW_ADDED.equals(propertyName) || ExtendedPropertyNames.VIEW_REMOVED.equals(propertyName))
            {
                // an added / removed symbol
                PresentationElement presentationElement = (PresentationElement) evt.getNewValue();
            }
        }
    }
    
    adapter.install(project);
 
    // When the adapter is no longer needed, uninstall it. 
    adapter.uninstall(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre class="auto-cursor-target"> </pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573656 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00726: Printing messages in Simulation Console

- page_id: `249573656`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573656/Printing+messages+in+Simulation+Console
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.548+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1 > Alf Plugin Developer Guide > Cameo Simulation Toolkit > Java APIs > Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print different types of messages on the **Simulation Console** pane by using the following codes

- SimulationManager.logConsoleDebug ( Project project , String message)

Displays debugging information.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleInfo ( Project project , String message)

Displays information in general.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleWarn ( Project project , String message)

Displays warning messages.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleError ( Project project , String message)

Displays error messages.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573613 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00727: Project (decomposition) structure API changes

- page_id: `249573613`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573613/Project+decomposition+structure+API+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:04:40.634+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins migration > Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following figure shows the domain model of the project decomposition.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The core change is an introduction of *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject* - *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject* and *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject*. *IPrimaryProject* is a single instance in the scope of a project. *IPrimaryProject* can have attached (used) any number of *IAttachedProject* (used projects), while *IAttachedProject* itself can attach other used projects (*IAttachedProject*).

The earlier than 17.0.1 project structure API made deprecated in version 17.0.1. The deprecated classes from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules*package are:

*Committable*

*IProject*

*ModuleDescriptor*

*ModulesManager*

*MountHelper*

*MountInfo*

*MountTable*

*MountTableListener*

*ShareInfo*

*ShareTable*

There is no straight one to one mapping, but the set of deprecated classes are now generally replaced by the set of the following classes:

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.mounting.IMountPoint*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.sharing.ISharePoint*

*ProjectUtilities* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService* utility classes are introduced to help working with a new project decomposition structure and should replace *MountHelper*.

*ModuleDescriptor* is replaced with a combination of *IAttachedProject* and *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration*. There is separate *ModuleDescriptor* for every (same) project usage in MagicDraw 17.0 or earlier versions, while there is a single *IAttachedProject* for the used project, but a separate *ProjectAttachmentConfiguration* for every project usage in the current version.

The example of the MagicDraw 17.0 and earlier version code:

```java
projectModules = project.getModulesManager().getMountTable().getModules()
	for (ModuleDescriptor moduleDescriptor : projectModules)
	{
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = moduleDescriptor.getAutoLoadType();
		final boolean loadIndex = moduleDescriptor.isLoadIndex();
		final boolean editable = moduleDescriptor.isEditable();
	}]]>
```

The example of a new MagicDraw 17.0.2 code:

```java
projectAttachedProjects = ProjectUtilities.getAttachedProjects(primaryProject);
	for (IAttachedProject attachedProject : projectAttachedProjects)
	{
		final ProjectAttachmentConfiguration attachmentConfiguration = ProjectUtilities.getAttachment(primaryProject, attachedProject);
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = ProjectUtilities.getAutoLoadKind(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean loadIndex = ProjectUtilities.isLoadIndex(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean editable = !attachmentConfiguration.isReadOnly();
	}]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The following figure shows the domain model of the project decomposition.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Domain model of project decomposition" ac:alt="Domain model of project decomposition"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Domain_model_of_project_decomposition.png" /></ac:image></p><div class="WordSection1"><p><br /></p><p>The core change is an introduction of <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject</em> - <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject</em> and <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject</em>. <em>IPrimaryProject</em> is a single instance in the scope of a project. <em>IPrimaryProject</em> can have attached (used) any number of <em>IAttachedProject</em> (used projects), while <em>IAttachedProject</em> itself can attach other used projects (<em>IAttachedProject</em>).</p><p>The earlier than 17.0.1 project structure API made deprecated in version 17.0.1. The deprecated classes from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules </em>package are:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Committable</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em> IProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em> ModuleDescriptor</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ModulesManager </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountHelper </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountInfo </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountTable </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountTableListener </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ShareInfo </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ShareTable</em></p></div><div class="WordSection2"><p><br /></p><p>There is no straight one to one mapping, but  the set of deprecated classes are now generally replaced by the set of the following classes:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.mounting.IMountPoint</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.sharing.ISharePoint</em></p><p><em>ProjectUtilities</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService</em> utility classes are introduced to help working with a new project decomposition structure and should replace <em>MountHelper</em>.</p><p><em>ModuleDescriptor</em> is replaced with a combination of <em>IAttachedProject</em> and <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em>. There is separate <em>ModuleDescriptor</em> for every (same) project usage in MagicDraw 17.0 or earlier versions, while there is a single <em>IAttachedProject</em> for the used project, but a separate <em>ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em> for every project usage in the current version.</p><p><br /></p><p>The example of the MagicDraw 17.0 and earlier version code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ea0081f-b05b-4ada-a258-f78dd22d3f07"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	final Collection<ModuleDescriptor> projectModules = project.getModulesManager().getMountTable().getModules()
	for (ModuleDescriptor moduleDescriptor : projectModules)
	{
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = moduleDescriptor.getAutoLoadType();
		final boolean loadIndex = moduleDescriptor.isLoadIndex();
		final boolean editable = moduleDescriptor.isEditable();
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The example of a new MagicDraw 17.0.2 code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c472fef9-e92a-462c-b715-593ad911b154"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	final IPrimaryProject primaryProject = project.getPrimaryProject();
	final Collection<IAttachedProject> projectAttachedProjects = ProjectUtilities.getAttachedProjects(primaryProject);
	for (IAttachedProject attachedProject : projectAttachedProjects)
	{
		final ProjectAttachmentConfiguration attachmentConfiguration = ProjectUtilities.getAttachment(primaryProject, attachedProject);
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = ProjectUtilities.getAutoLoadKind(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean loadIndex = ProjectUtilities.isLoadIndex(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean editable = !attachmentConfiguration.isReadOnly();
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573388 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00728: Project concept

- page_id: `249573388`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573388/Project+concept
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:38.077+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Working with project
- labels: ['application', 'project', 'model', 'root-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project* represents the storage of all project specific data like the UML model, all diagrams, project specific options and etc.

Multiple projects can be opened at the same time, but just one Project can be active in UI. Data from one project can not reference data in other opened project. The active (current) project can be accessible in the following way:

```java

```

Project

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement (UML Element or Presentation Element)

```java

```

The project keeps references to a root *Model(s)*, also has references to all diagrams.

Project always have one primary root Model and may have zero or more attached Models from used projects. Attached models are available only in projects loaded from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud' space='MT']. Locally stored projects or projects have only a primary root model.

The primary root model can be accessed in the following way:

```java

```

All root models can be accessed in the following way:

```java
models = project.getModels();]]>
```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project structure decomposition' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML-based' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project</em> represents the storage of all project specific data like the UML model, all diagrams, project specific options and etc.</p><p>Multiple projects can be opened at the same time, but just one Project can be active in UI. Data from one project can not reference data in other opened project. The active (current) project can be accessible in the following way:</p><div class="WordSection1"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64d0d6b5-8f4e-4580-85c5-7f67f8f84fac"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection1">Also, the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Project </em>is accessible for any other <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement (UML Element or Presentation Element)</em></div><div class="WordSection1"><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7032c3b8-1f0c-4778-8c65-d8b5e1366e57"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Project.getProject(element);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>The project keeps references to a root <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Model(s)</em>, also has references to all diagrams.</p><p>Project always have one primary root Model and may have zero or more attached Models from used projects. Attached models are available only in projects loaded from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Using Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Enterprise Dataware server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Locally stored projects or projects have only a primary root model.</p><p>The primary root model can be accessed in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fc25cc4-311a-4752-9d10-d328721fd47a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Package model = project.getPrimaryModel();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span>All root models can be accessed in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48570a3b-95d2-4b8a-8056-d004f14ce5fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List<Package> models = project.getModels();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="579ea26b-cf13-4db3-98f0-b9e6944d72e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project structure decomposition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML-based" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UML Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573391 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00729: Project descriptor

- page_id: `249573391`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573391/Project+descriptor
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:38.241+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Working with project
- labels: ['projectdescriptorsfactory', 'teamworkutils', 'project-descriptors-factory', 'teamwork-utils']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor* represents a project (or a used project) as a resource for storing and loading. The same project can have multiple *ProjectDescriptors*.

There are two types of *ProjectDescriptors*:

- A local project descriptor. It represents a local ordinary project. A descriptor can be created for a project or file object.
- A r emote project descriptor. It represents a project stored in the server.

A server project has both descriptors (local and remote) because it can be saved locally.

Every *ProjectDescriptor* provides the following properties:

- URI. The location is a specific java.lang.String value that holds all information that is needed to access a project.
- Representation String. A string used for a project identification in the user interface (GUI).
- Remote flag. The remote flag indicates if the project descriptor represents a server project.

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptorsFactory*can create an appropriate *ProjectDescriptor* object,

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils***helps to find an existing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud' space='MT'] (remote) project descriptors.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor</em> represents a project (or a used project) as a resource for storing and loading. The same project can have multiple <em>ProjectDescriptors</em>.</p><p>There are two types of <em>ProjectDescriptors</em>:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">A local project descriptor. </strong>It represents a local ordinary project. A descriptor can be created for a project or file object.</li><li><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">A r</span></strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">emote project descriptor.</strong> It represents a project stored in the server.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00f130cd-2a48-40e4-9687-d59f7800d6f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A server project has both descriptors (local and remote) because it can be saved locally.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Every <em>ProjectDescriptor</em> provides the following properties:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">URI.</strong> The location is a specific <em>java.lang.String</em> value that holds all information that is needed to access a project.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Representation String.</strong> A string used for a project identification in the user interface (GUI).</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Remote flag. </strong>The remote flag indicates if the project descriptor represents a server project.</li></ul><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptorsFactory </em>can create an appropriate <em>ProjectDescriptor</em> object,</p><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils</em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em>helps to find an existing <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Using Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (remote) project descriptors.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573404 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00730: Project life-cycle events

- page_id: `249573404`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573404/Project+life-cycle+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:36:16.519+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager.addProjectListener(ProjectEventListener)* to register *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener*. The listener will be invoked on various project life-cycle related actions.

A recommendation is to register a listener in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.init()* method.

##### Save participant

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant* to plugin into a save/commit operation.

Register it using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(SaveParticipant[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Application.html#addSaveParticipant-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant-)*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager.addProjectListener(ProjectEventListener)</em> to register <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener</em>. The listener will be invoked on various project life-cycle related actions. </p><p>A recommendation is to register a listener in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.init()</em> method.</p><h3>Save participant</h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant</em> to plugin into a save/commit operation. </p><p>Register it using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(SaveParticipant<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Application.html#addSaveParticipant-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant-">)</a></em>. </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e3f64e1-1fb4-457e-b9d9-bffd65c50979"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Projects management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573398 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00731: Project loading and saving

- page_id: `249573398`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573398/Project+loading+and+saving
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:09:51.759+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Working with project > Projects management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Projects are saved and loaded by using two methods in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* class.

- saveProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)
- loadProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)

“Save” means “Commit” for the server project.

A project cannot be saved using a descriptor, if the project isn’t specified, and a project cannot be loaded, if the file isn’t specified. In such cases *java.lang.IllegalStateException* is thrown.

The silent mode means that during the saving or load process no GUI interruptions for a user input is used, for example, there is no the **Commit Project**dialog box while committing a server project or there is no the **Save**dialog box while saving a new project (a project is saved into the last used directory).

##### **Save participant**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant* to plugin into a save/commit operation.

Register it using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(**SaveParticipant)*.

Implementing *SaveParticipant.isReadyForSave(**Project, ProjectDescriptor)* you can "disable" the save/commit operation until some conditions are met.

###### Example #1. Saving an active project

```java

```

###### Example #2. Loading a project from the file

The project can be loaded, if the project’s file name is known:

```java

```

###### Example #3. Importing another project file

The project can be imported, if the project's file name is known:

```java

```

###### Example #4. Loading a server project

The project can be loaded, if the project’s qualified name is known:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project descriptor' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Projects are saved and loaded by using two methods in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager</em> class.</p><ul><li><em>saveProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)</em></li><li><em>loadProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)</em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42fdc785-9a78-4dce-ab21-2f094ef0cbd2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>“Save” means “Commit” for the server project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A project cannot be saved using a descriptor, if the project isn’t specified, and a project cannot be loaded, if the file isn’t specified. In such cases <em>java.lang.IllegalStateException</em> is thrown.</p><p>The silent mode means that during the saving or load process no GUI interruptions for a user input is used, for example, there is no the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Commit Project </strong>dialog box while committing a server project or there is no the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Save </strong>dialog box while saving a new project (a project is saved into the last used directory).</p><h3><strong>Save participant</strong></h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant</em> to plugin into a save/commit operation. </p><p>Register it using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(</em><em>SaveParticipant)</em>. </p><p>Implementing <em>SaveParticipant.isReadyForSave(</em><em>Project, ProjectDescriptor)</em> you can &quot;disable&quot; the save/commit operation until some conditions are met.</p><h4>Example #1. Saving an active project</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="402ef67b-d6b8-4112-8555-156fa0eff7dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();
    // An active project
    Project project = projectsManager.getActiveProject();
 
    // Get a project descriptor
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.getDescriptorForProject(project);
    
    // Save a project 
    projectsManager.saveProject(projectDescriptor, true);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4>Example #2. Loading a project from the file</h4><p>The project can be loaded, if the project’s file name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83f7a0b8-25be-4db9-92aa-df95ec7afe16"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(projectFilePath);
    // Create a project descriptor 
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI()); 
    projectsManager.loadProject(projectDescriptor, true);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example #3. Importing another project file</h4><p>The project can be imported, if the project's file name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5347718-0fe2-45bb-ba94-786d7bb53ffb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(projectFilePath);
    // Create a project descriptor 
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI()); 
    projectsManager.importProject(projectDescriptor);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example #4. Loading a server project</h4><p>The project can be loaded, if the project’s qualified name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="070a54dc-b403-4943-bb90-ef37f4e987e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();
    // Create a project descriptor
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = TeamworkUtils.getRemoteProjectDescriptorByQualifiedName(remoteProjectQualifiedName);
    if (projectDescriptor != null)
    {
        projectsManager.loadProject(projectDescriptor, true);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d623b2ab-c3d7-4b26-b554-42442b6e0377"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project descriptor" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573403 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00732: Project options

- page_id: `249573403`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573403/Project+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:22:37.350+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every project has project related options. These options are stored together with the project data. Options are organized using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager* classes.

Project options belong to three categories:

1. Visible in the Project Options UI window
2. Invisible in the Project Options UI window
3. Personal invisible in the Project Options UI window

Personal options are specific to every user in a server project. It means, every user may have different values of the same option if it is marked as "personal". The personal option is treated as a regular option in a local project.

##### **Retrieving Project Options**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getOptions()**.*

##### **Retrieving Project Option value**

The following example shows how to access project option’s value:

```java

```

**Adding custom project options**

Configurators are used for defining a new project options.

The following example shows, how to add the project option’s configurator at the plugin’s *init()* method in order to have the additional project option ”Test Property” for every project.

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every project has project related options. These options are stored together with the project data. Options are organized using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager</em> classes. </p><p>Project options belong to three categories:</p><ol><li>Visible in the Project Options UI window</li><li>Invisible in the Project Options UI window</li><li>Personal invisible in the Project Options UI window</li></ol><p>Personal options are specific to every user in a server project. It means, every user may have different values of the same option if it is marked as &quot;personal&quot;. The personal option is treated as a regular option in a local project.</p><h3><strong>Retrieving Project Options</strong></h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getOptions()</em><em>.</em></p><h3><strong>Retrieving Project Option value</strong></h3><p>The following example shows how to access project option’s value:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bab1580e-fad4-4325-9c3c-dfc0bac2e96a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //retrieving a visible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    //retrieving an invisible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_INVISIBLE_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    //retrieving a personal invisible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PERSONAL_INVISIBLE_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    
    //retrieving an option value
    if (property != null)
    {
        Object value = property.getValue();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Adding custom project options</span></strong></p><p class="WordSection1">Configurators are used for defining a new project options. </p><p class="WordSection1">The following example shows, how to add the project option’s configurator at the plugin’s <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">init()</em> method in order to have the additional project option ”Test Property” for every project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="240636e0-34c2-464e-9969-6154f03d6f38"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectOptions.addConfigurator(new ProjectOptionsConfigurator()
    {
        public void configure(ProjectOptions projectOptions)
        {
            com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property property = projectOptions.getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
            if (property == null)
            {
                // Create a property, if it does not exist
                property = new StringProperty("TEST_PROPERTY_ID", "description");
                // Group
                property.setGroup("MY_GROUP");
                // The custom resource provider
                property.setResourceProvider(new PropertyResourceProvider()
                {
                    public String getString(String string, Property property)
                    {
                        if ("TEST_PROPERTY_ID".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate ID
                            return "Test Property";
                         }
                         if ("TEST_PROPERTY_ID_DESCRIPTION".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate a description
                            return "Test Property in My Group";
                        }
                        if ("MY_GROUP".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate a group
                            return "My Group";
                        }
						return string; 
					}
                });
          
                // Add a property
                projectOptions.addProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, property);
            }
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08f1f2bc-1687-469f-99c0-4be4352cdb03"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573401 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00733: Project structure decomposition

- page_id: `249573401`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573401/Project+structure+decomposition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:39:25.076+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Project structure is decomposed into several parts - a primary project and attached projects. The primary project part belong to a project itself. Attached project parts comes from "used projects" (modules). Used projects can use other projects, so there is a deep structure of attached projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities* utility class for retrieving more project decomposition related information.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService* or*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* classes for managing attached projects.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects management' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Used project management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Project structure is decomposed into several parts - a primary project and attached projects. The primary project part belong to a project itself. Attached project parts comes from &quot;used projects&quot; (modules). Used projects can use other projects, so there is a deep structure of attached projects.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Project structure decomposition" ac:alt="Project structure decomposition"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project structure decomposition.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities</em> utility class for retrieving more project decomposition related information.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService</em> or<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager</em> classes for managing attached projects.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ebbfd8f-20ed-42c8-b119-db7efb958c35"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Projects management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Used project management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573393 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00734: Projects management

- page_id: `249573393`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573393/Projects+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:26:16.766+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* class is responsible for containment and management of projects.

Use the code below to access *ProjectsManager*

```java

```

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
*ProjectsManager* provides the API for *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project* creating, closing, saving, loading, and activating. A program can have multiple opened projects, but only one project can be active.

```java
projects = projectsManager.getProjects();
 
    //Gets an active project
    Project activeProject = projectsManager.getActiveProject();]]>
```

An active project can also be accessed directly from *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application*:

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\projects*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573534 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00735: Properties

- page_id: `249573534`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573534/Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:32:10.163+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General
- labels: ['property-editor', 'property-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools include the Open API that provides a set of classes used as properties for diagrams’ symbols, project or environment options and etc.

Every property has two major attributes – the ID and value. A property has a value of the type *java.lang.Object*. Every specific property has a value of the specific type. For example, the value of *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.BooleanProperty* is*java.lang.Boolean*, the value of a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.StringProperty* is *java.lang.String*.

The property ID identifies the specific property in the properties set.

You must set some *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyResourceProvider* to your property instance in order to display a normal name, not the ID of the property in the UI of a modeling tool. *PropertyResourceProvider* has just one method *getString(java.lang.String , Property[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/properties/PropertyResourceProvider.html#getString-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property-), where a key is an ID of your property.*

The collection of properties is grouped by *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager*. Every *PropertyManager* has a name and a list of properties. It can return the property by the ID, properties with the same value, properties whose values are different.

For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing properties of presentation elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Every <em>PropertyManager</em> has a name and a list of properties. It can return the property by the ID, properties with the same value, properties whose values are different.</p><p>For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fee02083-faa4-4e60-b526-a587d03e9b37"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing properties of presentation elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00736: Property change event concept

- page_id: `249573440`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573440/Property+change+event+concept
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:36.777+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Event support
- labels: ['new-element-creation-event', 'elements-name-change-event']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML model uses standard *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent* events for notifying listeners about changes.

The main properties of the *PropertyChangeEvent*are as follows:

- Property name. The changed property name.
- New value. The new (current) changed property value.
- Old value. The old property value, which was before the property change.

Properties names are described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML model properties names' space=''] page

###### **Example of the element’s name change event**

```java

```

###### **Example of the new element creation event**

```java

```
