<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573434 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00751: Retrieving meta information about element

- page_id: `249573434`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573434/Retrieving+meta+information+about+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:22:53.550+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every Element is identified by it's "metaclass". Element metaclass is mapped into java interface (for example *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class*).

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.getClassType()* to retrieve the element metaclass.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassTypes* for managing the metaclasses - collecting all available, collecting derived and etc.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every Element is identified by it's &quot;metaclass&quot;. Element metaclass is mapped into java interface (for example <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class</em>). </p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.getClassType()</em> to retrieve the element metaclass.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassTypes</em> for managing the metaclasses - collecting all available, collecting derived and etc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573681 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00752: Reusing Teamwork Cloud session

- page_id: `249573681`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573681/Reusing+Teamwork+Cloud+session
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T14:58:11.970+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When using cURL, user credentials (user name/password or a token) can be provided in a command-line argument. A new session is created during authentication. It is **very** important to note that a Teamwork Cloud session remains open after a REST API returns. This implies that a Teamwork Cloud session is not closed automatically. If the user’s credentials are still in use (without sending a valid cookie), the license count will be used up, and new sessions cannot be created. The user needs to wait until the session is timed out. The default timeout is 15 minutes.

The correct way to use REST API is to log in only once, reuse the session for all subsequent calls, and log out once finished.

The following example is a Bash script used for creating a comma-separated list of email addresses for all users in Teamwork Cloud, to facilitate notification sending.

```text
users.json
sed 's/\"\,\"/\n/g' users.json > users.txt
sed -i 's/\[\"//g' users.txt
sed -i 's/\"\]//g' users.txt
while read username; do
		curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL/$username" -H "accept: application/json" >> email.txt
done < users.txt
curl -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt $LOGOUTURL
grep -E -o "\b[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\b" email.txt > email.lst
sed -i 's/\n/;/g' email.lst
sort email.lst | uniq > email_unique.lst
tr '\n' ',' < email_unique.lst > email_list.csv
truncate -s-1 email_list.csv

]]>
```

The first execution of cURL logs in to the server. The server returns a session ID in a cookie. The cookie is saved into the **cookie.txt** file. The remaining cURL calls send the cookie back to the server to reuse the open session.

The example below shows how to use PowerShell to create internal users from a CSV file.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When using cURL, user credentials (user name/password or a token) can be provided in a command-line argument. A new session is created during authentication. It is <strong>very</strong> important to note that a Teamwork Cloud session remains open after a REST API returns. This implies that a Teamwork Cloud session is not closed automatically. If the user’s credentials are still in use (without sending a valid cookie), the license count will be used up, and new sessions cannot be created. The user needs to wait until the session is timed out. The default timeout is 15 minutes.</p><p>The correct way to use REST API is to log in only once, reuse the session for all subsequent calls, and log out once finished.</p><p>The following example is a Bash script used for creating a comma-separated list of email addresses for all users in Teamwork Cloud, to facilitate notification sending.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="154a6874-c10f-4833-bd14-899a0068625c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
# Script to export a unique list of email address for all users in Teamwork Cloud into a singe CSV list (email_list.csv)
# Author:  Benjamin Krajmalnik
#
# 
SERVER=127.0.0.1
RESTBASEURL=https://$SERVER:8111/osmc
USERURL=${RESTBASEURL}/admin/users
LOGINURL=${RESTBASEURL}/login
LOGOUTURL=${RESTBASEURL}/logout
username=Administrator
password=Administrator
rm -f email*.*
curl -k -s -c cookie.txt --insecure -u $username:$password $LOGINURL
curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL" -H "accept: application/json" > users.json
sed 's/\"\,\"/\n/g' users.json > users.txt
sed -i 's/\[\"//g' users.txt
sed -i 's/\"\]//g' users.txt
while read username; do
		curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL/$username" -H "accept: application/json" >> email.txt
done < users.txt
curl -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt $LOGOUTURL
grep -E -o "\b[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\b" email.txt > email.lst
sed -i 's/\n/;/g' email.lst
sort email.lst | uniq > email_unique.lst
tr '\n' ',' < email_unique.lst > email_list.csv
truncate -s-1 email_list.csv

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The first execution of cURL logs in to the server. The server returns a session ID in a cookie. The cookie is saved into the <strong>cookie.txt</strong> file. The remaining cURL calls send the cookie back to the server to reuse the open session.</p><p>The example below shows how to use PowerShell to create internal users from a CSV file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcf44a99-0ec2-4a02-8871-c1632d46fd92"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# Script to import users from CSV file into Teamwork Cloud
# Author:  Benjamin Krajmalnik
# Format: non-quoted CSV, no headers
#  userLogin,userPassword,fullUserName,departmentName,emailAddress
#
# Usage:
# PowerShell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ImportUsersFromCSV.ps1
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
Add-Type @"
  using System.Net;
  using System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates;
  public class TrustAllCertificatesPolicy : ICertificatePolicy 
  {
     public bool CheckValidationResult
     (
       ServicePoint srvPoint, X509Certificate certificate
       ,
       WebRequest request, int certificateProblem
     ) 
     {
       return true;
     }
  }
"@
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CheckCertificateRevocationList = $false;
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::ServerCertificateValidationCallback += { $true; };
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CertificatePolicy = New-Object TrustAllCertificatesPolicy
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [System.Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12
$Server = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Server Name or IP address'
$Admin = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Administrator User'
$Passwd = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Server Administrator Password'
$Userfile = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the full path name of the file containing the users'
$RESTBASEURL = "https://"+$Server+":8111/osmc/"
$ADDUSERURL = $RESTBASEURL + "admin/users"
$LOGINURL = $RESTBASEURL   + "login"
$LOGOUTURL = $RESTBASEURL + "logout"
$pair="$($Admin):$($Passwd)"
$bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes($pair)
$base64 = [System.Convert]::ToBase64String($bytes)
$basicAuthValue = "Basic $base64"
$headers = @{ Authorization = $basicAuthValue }
$RestError = $null
Try {
$response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "$LOGINURL" -Method Post -Headers $headers -SessionVariable 'Session'
} Catch {
	$RestError = $_
}
foreach($line in [System.IO.File]::ReadLines($Userfile))
{
                  $login,$password,$username,$department,$email = $line.split(',').trim()
                  
                  $JSON=@"
                  {
                                             "userName": "$login",
                                             "password": "$password",
                                             "otherAttributes": {
                                                            "mobile": "",
                                                            "name": "$username",
                                                            "department": "$department",
                                                            "email": "$email"
                                             },
                                             "enabled": true
                              }
"@
                              $response = Invoke-WebRequest  -Uri "$ADDUSERURL" -Method Post -Body $JSON -ContentType "application/json"  -WebSession $Session
}
 
$response = Invoke-WebRequest  -Uri "$LOGOUTURL" –Method Get -WebSession $Session]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573386 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00753: Running action with progress

- page_id: `249573386`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573386/Running+action+with+progress
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:42:52.686+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Adding new functionality
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For long lasting actions, there is a need to show a progress and run the asynchronous code.

There are two options to execute these actions:

- a foreground task - use com.nomagic.task.RunnableWithProgress or com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.MagicDrawProgressStatusRunner
- a background task - use com.nomagic.magicdraw.task.BackgroundTaskRunner

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool**installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\progressstatus*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Multi-threading' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>For long lasting actions, there is a need to show a progress and run the asynchronous code.</p><p>There are two options to execute these actions:</p><ul><li>a foreground task - use <em>com.nomagic.task.RunnableWithProgress</em> or <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.MagicDrawProgressStatusRunner</em></li><li>a background task - use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.task.BackgroundTaskRunner</em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;<em>modeling tool</em> </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">installation direc</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">tory&gt;\openapi\examples\progressstatus</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74b88a6d-75d0-4f9c-9077-d7b780313a90"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Multi-threading" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573597 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00754: Running programs in batch mode

- page_id: `249573597`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573597/Running+programs+in+batch+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:45:32.669+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are cases when custom task must be executed in the batch (command line) mode. For example, if you want to run MagicDraw or other modeling tool application, open some project, execute code generation and exit application.

API provides two ways to run the tool in the batch mode depending on the required functionality.

1. Functionality is provided by an application core. You need to extend the com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine class.
2. Functionality is provided by a plugin. Plugin loading is managed by the application core so it is undesirable to add core and plugin classes into the same class path. The different approach accessing plugin functionality is used. You need to implement the com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction interface.

****You can find the code examples in

1. <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\*imagegenerator* . It takes a project file and destination directory as arguments and generates images for all diagrams.
2. <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin . Just prints the program arguments to the application log file.

The application cannot be run on the headless device even in a batch mode. The graphical environment is required.

For more information on the launching batch mode programs, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space=''].

For more information on creation of batch mode programs, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementing command line launchers' space=''].

More information is available in javadoc.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You need to extend the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine</em> class.</li><li>Functionality is provided by a plugin. Plugin loading is managed by the application core so it is undesirable to add core and plugin classes into the same class path. The different approach accessing plugin functionality is used. You need to implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction</em> interface.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ol><li><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\<em>imagegenerator</em></em>. It takes a project file and destination directory as arguments and generates images for all diagrams.</li><li><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin</em>. Just prints the program arguments to the application log file.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e86ef95-eade-424e-a877-f424d503c8b6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The application cannot be run on the headless device even in a batch mode. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180520 space=DEVG version=9 -->
## PAGE 00755: Running the Python Open API Example

- page_id: `314180520`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/314180520/Running+the+Python+Open+API+Example
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-07-13T17:33:07.435+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Python Integration Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page demonstrates how to launch the modeling tool from Python, create or open a SysML v2 project, modify model using the Open API, and save the resulting project. The example is based on the Rescue Drone Model and illustrates a typical end-to-end workflow.

#### Overview

The script demonstrates a complete end-to-end Open API workflow:

- starts the modeling tool from Python
- opens or creates a local SysML v2 project
- accesses the SysML v2 model through the Open API
- creates model using the RescueDroneModelCreator
- saves the project
- closes the project and shuts down the application

After successful execution, a project named `PythonExampleOpenAPI` is created or updated.

#### Setting installation path

Before running the example, configure the modeling tool installation path in run_cameo_drone_example.py

```java

```

#### Running the Example

Execute the example using:

```text

```

If execution completes successfully, the example project is created, modified, and saved automatically.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page demonstrates how to launch the modeling tool from Python, create or open a SysML v2 project, modify model using the Open API, and save the resulting project. The example is based on the Rescue Drone Model and illustrates a typical end-to-end workflow.</p><h2>Overview</h2><p>The script demonstrates a complete end-to-end Open API workflow:</p><ul><li>starts the modeling tool from Python</li><li>opens or creates a local SysML v2 project</li><li>accesses the SysML v2 model through the Open API</li><li>creates model using the <code>RescueDroneModelCreator</code></li><li>saves the project</li><li>closes the project and shuts down the application</li></ul><p>After successful execution, a project named <code>PythonExampleOpenAPI</code> is created or updated.</p><h2>Setting installation path</h2><p>Before running the example, configure the modeling tool installation path in run_cameo_drone_example.py</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b878fff-588f-4b1c-af99-755ae777e9e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[CAMEO_INSTALL_PATH = (
    Path("/Applications/Cameo_Systems_Modeler")
    if IS_MAC
    else Path(r"C:\Program Files\Cameo Systems Modeler")
)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Running the Example</h2><p>Execute the example using:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1a9a32e-5c6f-4b86-b158-d30e51354411"><ac:parameter ac:name="">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[python run_cameo_drone_example.py
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If execution completes successfully, the example project is created, modified, and saved automatically.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=309365580 space=DEVG version=5 -->
## PAGE 00756: Running Validation and Inspecting Results

- page_id: `309365580`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/309365580/Running+Validation+and+Inspecting+Results
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-05-29T04:53:41.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Model Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The validation Open API can be used to:

- Run validation for selected model elements.
- Configure validation suites and validation parameters.
- Retrieve validation failures.
- Inspect validation failure details.
- Display validation results in the Validation Results table.

#### Main Classes

| Class | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ValidationService | Provides services for running validation and accessing validation suites. |
| ValidationService.Params | Configures a validation request. |
| ValidationSuite | Represents a validation suite used during validation. |
| ValidationFailure | Represents a validation rule violation. |
| ValidationResultsTables | Displays validation failures in the Validation Results window. |

#### Getting the Validation Service

Use [ValidationService#getInstanceByProject(ModelElementProject)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getInstanceByProject(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.project.ModelElementProject)) to get the validation service for a project.

```text

```

#### Creating a Validation Suite

Use [ValidationService#createValidationSuite(Element)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#createValidationSuite(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element)) to create a validation suite for a model element.

```text

```

#### Using Built-in Validation Suites

[ValidationService](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html) also provides access to built-in validation suites.

##### SysML Constraints Suite

Use [ValidationService#getSysMLConstraintsSuite()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getSysMLConstraintsSuite()) to get the SysML constraints validation suite.

```text

```

##### Dassault Systèmes Validation Suite

Use [ValidationService#getDassaultSystemesValidationSuite()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getDassaultSystemesValidationSuite()) to get the Dassault Systèmes validation suite.

```text

```

##### Dassault Systèmes Composition Integrity Validation Suite

Use [ValidationService#getDassaultSystemesCompositionIntegrityValidationSuite()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getDassaultSystemesCompositionIntegrityValidationSuite()) to get the Dassault Systèmes Composition Integrity validation suite.

```text

```

#### Creating Validation Parameters

Use [ValidationService#createParams(Collection)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#createParams(java.util.Collection)) to create validation parameters for the elements you want to validate.

```text

```

#### Running Validation

Run validation using [ValidationService#validate(ValidationService.Params)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#validate(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationService.Params)).

```text
failures = validationService.validate(params);
]]>
```

#### Full Example

The following example:

1. Gets the validation service.
2. Gets a validation suite.
3. Creates validation parameters.
4. Runs validation.

```text
failures = validationService.validate(params);
]]>
```

#### Inspecting Validation Failures

Validation results are returned as a collection of [ValidationFailure](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html) objects.

Use [ValidationFailure#getMessage()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getMessage()), [ValidationFailure#getValidationRuleName()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getValidationRuleName()), and [ValidationFailure#getSeverityLevel()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getSeverityLevel()) to inspect validation results.

```text

```

##### Getting the Validated Target and Failed Element

Use [ValidationFailure#getTarget()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getTarget()) to get the validated target element.

Use [ValidationFailure#getElement()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getElement()) to get the element that caused the failure.

```text

```

#### Displaying Validation Results

Use [ValidationResultsTables#getInstance(Project)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/validation/ui/table/ValidationResultsTables.html#getInstance(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project)) to get the validation results table service.

Then use [ValidationResultsTables#openValidationResults(Element, Collection)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/validation/ui/table/ValidationResultsTables.html#openValidationResults(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,java.util.Collection)) to display validation failures in the Validation Results table.

```text

```

#### Selecting Validation Suites

Validation suites can be added individually or in groups.

##### Add a Single Validation Suite

Use [ValidationService.Params#addSuite(ValidationSuite)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addSuite(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite)).

```text

```

##### Add Multiple Validation Suites

Use [ValidationService.Params#addSuites(Collection)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addSuites(java.util.Collection)).

```text

```

##### Add Active Validation Suites

Use [ValidationService.Params#addActiveSuites()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addActiveSuites()) to add all active validation suites.

```text

```

##### Add Passive Validation Suites

Use [ValidationService.Params#addPassiveSuites()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addPassiveSuites()) to add all passive validation suites.

```text

```

#### Getting Active and Passive Suites

Use [ValidationService#getActiveSuites()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getActiveSuites()) and [ValidationService#getPassiveSuites()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getPassiveSuites()) to retrieve validation suites available in the project.

```text
activeSuites = validationService.getActiveSuites();

List passiveSuites = validationService.getPassiveSuites();
]]>
```

#### Filtering by Constraint Names

Validation can be restricted to selected constraint names using [ValidationService.Params#addConstraintNames(Collection)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addConstraintNames(java.util.Collection)).

```text

```

#### Using Progress Status

A custom progress status can be supplied using [ValidationService.Params#setProgressStatus(ProgressStatus)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#setProgressStatus(com.nomagic.task.ProgressStatus)).

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The validation Open API can be used to:</p><ul><li>Run validation for selected model elements.</li><li>Configure validation suites and validation parameters.</li><li>Retrieve validation failures.</li><li>Inspect validation failure details.</li><li>Display validation results in the Validation Results table.</li></ul><h2>Main Classes</h2><table><tbody><tr><th><p>Class</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><a request.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationSuite.html">ValidationSuite</a></p></td><td><p>Represents a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/validation/ui/table/ValidationResultsTables.html">ValidationResultsTables</a></p></td><td><p>Displays validation failures in the Validation Results window.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h2>Getting the Validation Service</h2><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getInstanceByProject(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.project.ModelElementProject)">ValidationService#getInstanceByProject(ModelElementProject)</a> to get the validation service for a project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="187bf6b7-99f5-408e-8e9b-ad9d2d298d86"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationService;

ValidationService validationService = ValidationService.getInstanceByProject(project);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Creating a Validation Suite</h2><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#createValidationSuite(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element)">ValidationService#createValidationSuite(Element)</a> to create a validation suite for a model element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a1608b5-d7ee-4533-9b58-ecb27cd72837"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationService;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite;

ValidationSuite validationSuite = ValidationService.createValidationSuite(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using Built-in Validation Suites</h2><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html">ValidationService</a> also provides access to built-in validation suites.</p><h3>SysML Constraints Suite</h3><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getSysMLConstraintsSuite()">ValidationService#getSysMLConstraintsSuite()</a> to get the SysML constraints validation suite.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9092e845-bda9-4ad0-8f3a-492ee1a01695"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite;

ValidationSuite sysMLConstraintsSuite = validationService.getSysMLConstraintsSuite();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Dassault Systèmes Validation Suite</h3><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getDassaultSystemesValidationSuite()">ValidationService#getDassaultSystemesValidationSuite()</a> to get the Dassault Systèmes validation suite.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58398290-ba46-48a2-b137-3a396fd471e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite;

ValidationSuite dassaultSystemesValidationSuite = validationService.getDassaultSystemesValidationSuite();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Dassault Systèmes Composition Integrity Validation Suite</h3><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getDassaultSystemesCompositionIntegrityValidationSuite()">ValidationService#getDassaultSystemesCompositionIntegrityValidationSuite()</a> to get the Dassault Systèmes Composition Integrity validation suite.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efff4262-e27e-4ca0-8758-a8cd7846b0f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite;

ValidationSuite compositionIntegritySuite = validationService.getDassaultSystemesCompositionIntegrityValidationSuite();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Creating Validation Parameters</h2><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#createParams(java.util.Collection)">ValidationService#createParams(Collection)</a> to create validation parameters for the elements you want to validate.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64a0fbad-6050-408d-ad4c-d3967ae135d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationService;

import java.util.Collection;

ValidationService.Params params = validationService.createParams(elementsToValidate).addSuite(validationSuite);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Running Validation</h2><p>Run validation using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#validate(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationService.Params)">ValidationService#validate(ValidationService.Params)</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96910590-71ef-43fb-b078-a28e73a65959"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationFailure;

import java.util.Collection;

Collection<ValidationFailure> failures = validationService.validate(params);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Full Example</h2><p>The following example:</p><ol><li>Gets the validation service.</li><li>Gets a validation suite.</li><li>Creates validation parameters.</li><li>Runs validation.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a1ca755-cee6-4b09-8540-26377a0aa5cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationFailure;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationService;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.validation.ui.table.ValidationResultsTables;

import java.util.Collection;

ValidationService validationService = ValidationService.getInstanceByProject(project);

ValidationSuite validationSuite = validationService.getDassaultSystemesValidationSuite();

ValidationService.Params params = validationService.createParams(elementsToValidate).addSuite(validationSuite);

Collection<ValidationFailure> failures = validationService.validate(params);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Inspecting Validation Failures</h2><p>Validation results are returned as a collection of <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html">ValidationFailure</a> objects.</p><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getMessage()">ValidationFailure#getMessage()</a>, <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getValidationRuleName()">ValidationFailure#getValidationRuleName()</a>, and <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getSeverityLevel()">ValidationFailure#getSeverityLevel()</a> to inspect validation results.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13b8aa65-a4d5-4224-917e-acc91f083c7a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.SeverityLevel;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationFailure;

for (ValidationFailure failure : failures)
{
	String message = failure.getMessage();
	String ruleName = failure.getValidationRuleName();
	SeverityLevel severity = failure.getSeverityLevel();
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting the Validated Target and Failed Element</h3><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getTarget()">ValidationFailure#getTarget()</a> to get the validated target element.</p><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationFailure.html#getElement()">ValidationFailure#getElement()</a> to get the element that caused the failure.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ced3baa0-e41d-46de-b2da-e80951752360"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement;

for (ValidationFailure failure : failures)
{
	BaseElement target = failure.getTarget();
	BaseElement failedElement = failure.getElement();
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Displaying Validation Results</h2><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/validation/ui/table/ValidationResultsTables.html#getInstance(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project)">ValidationResultsTables#getInstance(Project)</a> to get the validation results table service.</p><p>Then use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/validation/ui/table/ValidationResultsTables.html#openValidationResults(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,java.util.Collection)">ValidationResultsTables#openValidationResults(Element, Collection)</a> to display validation failures in the Validation Results table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56ad059-9acd-47a3-b359-d8055e71a4af"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.validation.ui.table.ValidationResultsTables;

ValidationResultsTables.getInstance(project).openValidationResults(validationOwner, failures);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Selecting Validation Suites</h2><p>Validation suites can be added individually or in groups.</p><h3>Add a Single Validation Suite</h3><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addSuite(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite)">ValidationService.Params#addSuite(ValidationSuite)</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18d65b89-c2c7-467b-a476-325a25be6b9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[params.addSuite(validationSuite);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Add Multiple Validation Suites</h3><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addSuites(java.util.Collection)">ValidationService.Params#addSuites(Collection)</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0d68d76-294c-43fa-a533-b28d49a7a55e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[params.addSuites(validationSuites);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Add Active Validation Suites</h3><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addActiveSuites()">ValidationService.Params#addActiveSuites()</a> to add all active validation suites.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3d652c7-1247-4a0d-992f-1deb49b3fe54"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[params.addActiveSuites();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Add Passive Validation Suites</h3><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addPassiveSuites()">ValidationService.Params#addPassiveSuites()</a> to add all passive validation suites.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="185d2c8b-423d-45fe-b380-c8b114135508"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[params.addPassiveSuites();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Getting Active and Passive Suites</h2><p>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getActiveSuites()">ValidationService#getActiveSuites()</a> and <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.html#getPassiveSuites()">ValidationService#getPassiveSuites()</a> to retrieve validation suites available in the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7592b6c-8c9e-4b30-875d-dc05c842968c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.validation.ValidationSuite;

import java.util.List;

List<ValidationSuite> activeSuites = validationService.getActiveSuites();

List<ValidationSuite> passiveSuites = validationService.getPassiveSuites();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Filtering by Constraint Names</h2><p>Validation can be restricted to selected constraint names using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#addConstraintNames(java.util.Collection)">ValidationService.Params#addConstraintNames(Collection)</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09502587-1dfe-4fd0-a2de-9b7e9a4818fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import java.util.List;

params.addConstraintNames(List.of(
		"Constraint1",
		"Constraint2"
));
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using Progress Status</h2><p>A custom progress status can be supplied using <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/validation/ValidationService.Params.html#setProgressStatus(com.nomagic.task.ProgressStatus)">ValidationService.Params#setProgressStatus(ProgressStatus)</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="137c6de6-3616-4352-ad6b-9ce7f5c33db0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.task.ProgressStatus;

params.setProgressStatus(progressStatus);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5478fe15-51af-4122-aaa4-1a0111f53781" /></h3><h3>Performing the forward engineering</h3><p>The example shows how to perform a simple java code generation.</p><p><strong>Step #1. Creating <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c8fe96a-5d37-4773-afd7-e09335dc20e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject(); 
    String name = "sample CE project";
    String workingDir = OPENAPI_DATA_DIRECTORY_PATH;
 
    // create a working package
    ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
    Package workingPackage = ef.createPackageInstance();
    workingPackage.setName("my working package");
    workingPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
 
    // creating a code engineering set
    CodeEngineeringSet javaGenerationSet = CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(
				CodeEngineeringConstants.Languages.JAVA, null, name, project, workingPackage, workingDir);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Step #2. Adding model elements to <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="142f730a-857e-439e-91e1-e2d9f727c166"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject();
 
 	// create a new element
 	ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
 	Class classA = ef.createClassInstance();
 	classA.setName("ClassA");
	classA.setOwner(project.getModel());
	List<BaseElement> modelsForSample = new ArrayList<BaseElement>();
	modelsForSample.add(classA);
	javaGenerationSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(modelsForSample);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong>Step #3. Performing the <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em> generation</strong></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1a7d6f5-8cf4-4795-960a-8fc7e1d2d6d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    CodeEngineeringManager.generate(javaGenerationSet);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Performing the reverse engineering</h3><p>The example shows how to perform a simple java code reverse.</p><p><strong>Step #1. Creating <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9933ea61-dd6a-4c46-b658-9eef350f9f35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject(); 
    String name = "sample CE project";
    String workingDir = OPENAPI_DATA_DIRECTORY_PATH;// e.g C:\myworkingPackage
 
    // create a working package
    ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
    Package workingPackage = ef.createPackageInstance();
    workingPackage.setName("my working package");
    workingPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
 
    // creating a code engineering set
    CodeEngineeringSet ces = CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(
				CodeEngineeringConstants.Languages.JAVA, null, name, project, workingPackage, workingDir);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Here the null dialect is used for the java language, because java doesn't have any dialect.</p><p><strong>Step #2. Adding the source code to <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eccd7ee9-f67e-4c1a-95af-e288323986bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 	ces.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(new File(workingDir + File.separator + "test directory")); // starting from C:\myworkingPackage\test directory\]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This sets the given instance of the code engineering set working directory and adds all files from that directory. Set java classpaths for the project:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6ba7648-854c-4ea6-a859-f54175d7b845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    String[] claspath = new String[] { path1, path2, path3, path4 }; 
    JavaCodeEngineeringManager.setJavaClasspath(project, claspath);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Step #3. Performing the reverse of <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d41202c-94aa-42f4-bf2e-6a98fb396354"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     CodeEngineeringManager.reverse(ces, false);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573565 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00758: Script validation rule

- page_id: `249573565`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573565/Script+validation+rule
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:43:39.622+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Groovy, Javascript, or Jython scripting language can be used to create a validation rule which are used to validate elements in your model. The advantage of using script validation rules is that it is not necessary to compile and store them in a classpath. Oftentimes, the code is compact and simple.

###### How to create a a script validation rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Constraints' space='MT'].
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.
4. Specify constrained element(s).
5. Specify the specification language (Groovy, Javascript, or Jython).
6. Enter the validation code in the selected language.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation rules' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Groovy, Javascript, or Jython scripting language can be used to create a validation rule which are used to validate elements in your model. The advantage of using script validation rules is that it is not necessary to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="448794bc-8c8a-476a-8727-833273737189">compile and store them</ac:inline-comment-marker> in a classpath. <span>Oftentimes,</span> the code is compact and simple.</p><p> </p><h4>How to create a  a script validation rule</h4><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b66a4fbf-3ce3-492a-a322-9533858e6cfc"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Constraints" ri:space-key="MT" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a constraint]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.</li><li>Specify constrained element(s).</li><li>Specify the specification language (Groovy, Javascript, or Jython).</li><li>Enter the validation code in the selected language.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" ri:space-key="MT" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573410 space=DEVG version=10 -->
## PAGE 00760: SessionManager API

- page_id: `249573410`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573410/SessionManager+API
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2026-04-15T19:52:50.035+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Model Modification
- labels: ['create-session', 'close-session']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The `[com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html)` is a low-level API used to control model modification sessions manually.

All changes to model elements must be performed within an active session, defined by:

- [createSession](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html#createSession(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project,java.lang.String))(Project, String)
- [closeSession](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html#closeSession(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project))(Project)

A session defines the scope of model changes and ensures that all modifications are:

- Grouped into a single command
- Recorded in the command history
- Available for undo/redo

Only one session can be active per project at a time.

#### **When to Use This API**

Use `SessionManager` only when you need explicit control over the session lifecycle, such as:

- Integrating with legacy APIs or existing code
- Performing advanced or conditional session handling
- Working outside of the Editing API

For most use cases, prefer: **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing API' space='']**

```java

```

#### **Completing a Session**

After performing model changes, the session must be closed:

```java

```

Once closed:

- All changes are applied to the model
- They are recorded as a single command (using the session name)
- Undo/redo becomes available

#### **Important Notes**

- Only one session can be active at a time
- Calling createSession(...) while a session is already active throws IllegalStateException
- Failing to close a session may leave the model in an inconsistent state

#### **Best Practices**

- Always pair createSession(...) with closeSession(...)
- Use try/finally to guarantee session closure

```java

```

- Use meaningful session names (they appear in command history)
- Prefer the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing API' space='']`Editing` API unless manual control is required

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html">com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager</a></code> is a low-level API used to control model modification sessions manually.</p><p>All changes to model elements must be performed within an active session, defined by:</p><ul><li data-uuid="438c83e3-241a-4647-b5eb-1e53486fbf9c"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html#createSession(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project,java.lang.String)">createSession</a>(Project, String)</code></li><li data-uuid="9c5f0b1c-0a3f-4ac1-a117-4e6190cde0a7"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html#closeSession(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project)">closeSession</a>(Project)</code></li></ul><p>A session defines the scope of model changes and ensures that all modifications are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a4e9c511-2d5a-49c1-a9f2-225cb5ef2fc9">Grouped into a single <strong>command</strong></li><li data-uuid="3e92b22d-c4bc-425e-88d1-74dc973707cc">Recorded in the <strong>command history</strong></li><li data-uuid="0a8bc240-656d-42e1-815c-b4454c431175">Available for <strong>undo/redo</strong></li></ul><p>Only one session can be active per project at a time.</p><h2><span><strong>When to Use This API</strong></span></h2><p>Use <code>SessionManager</code> only when you need explicit control over the session lifecycle, such as:</p><ul><li data-uuid="89a5754f-5152-46db-b9db-92662256213c">Integrating with legacy APIs or existing code</li><li data-uuid="d0a61119-93ba-4396-8f8b-e27dcaffc780">Performing advanced or conditional session handling</li><li data-uuid="7a8e7795-4a9b-44b5-8e8c-3e811cc2f39b">Working outside of the <code>Editing</code> API</li></ul><p>For most use cases, prefer: <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing API" /></ac:link></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0bd881c-3475-431a-9f45-7e67883ec64d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager;

// Access singleton instance
SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();

// Only one session can be active per project
if (!sessionManager.isSessionCreated(project)) {
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Edit");
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><span><strong>Completing a Session</strong></span></h2><p>After performing model changes, the session must be closed:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88ddf6d9-7c8a-4b0a-b97b-f3fc6ae1516d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Once closed:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c2e78c2f-2012-46bb-8f2f-44fcd2e031fc">All changes are applied to the model</li><li data-uuid="8236bf36-8735-45b4-86a1-274c20d218d0">They are recorded as a single command (using the session name)</li><li data-uuid="2d2d27b1-9e8a-4a8c-aec9-a1318cc864cc">Undo/redo becomes available</li></ul><h2><strong>Important Notes</strong></h2><ul><li data-uuid="ea0291e2-b224-4407-987e-dc23d3a003f3">Only one session can be active at a time</li><li data-uuid="a953ab01-b067-42d9-8b63-8f4ebe2e6fec">Calling <code>createSession(...)</code> while a session is already active throws <code>IllegalStateException</code></li><li data-uuid="92a78295-1e97-4998-9246-06dc5d5392c6">Failing to close a session may leave the model in an inconsistent state</li></ul><h2><strong>Best Practices</strong></h2><ul><li data-uuid="846497fb-1587-49dd-becc-c4e54ce82569">Always pair <code>createSession(...)</code> with <code>closeSession(...)</code></li><li data-uuid="d77f5579-38af-4158-a118-7fc37514896d">Use <code>try/finally</code> to guarantee session closure</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5aa3e597-f600-43d9-aaf7-231521087112"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager;

SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();

sessionManager.createSession(project, "Edit");
try {
    // perform model changes here
} finally {
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="e44494d1-9b8a-4610-8893-f2f33805da3b">Use meaningful session names (they appear in command history)</li><li data-uuid="a77e515b-9ed4-4883-87df-ba77e4b6751e">Prefer the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing API" /><ac:link-body><code>Editing</code> API</ac:link-body></ac:link> unless manual control is required</li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00762: Showing Element seletion dialog

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### **Selectin****g Elements via Element Selection Dialog**

```java

```

You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>/openapi/examples/elementselection*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h4><strong>Selectin</strong><strong>g Elements via Element Selection Dialog</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cda0261-8c8f-4386-b713-bff08cdad38c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.create(...) methods to create the element selection dialog.
    Frame dialogParent = MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(); 
    ElementSelectionDlg dlg = ElementSelectionDlgFactory.create(dialogParent);
 
    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initSingle(...) methods to initialize the dialog with a single element selection mode.
    ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initSingle(....);
 
    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initMultiple(...) methods to initialize the dialog with a multiple element selection mode. 
    ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initMultiple(....);
 
    // Display the dialog for the user to select elements. 
    dlg.setVisible(true);
 
    // Check if the user has clicked "Ok". 
    if (dlg.isOkClicked())
    {
         // Get the selected element in a single selection mode. 
        BaseElement selected = dlg.getSelectedElement();
 
        // Get selected elements in a multiple selection mode. 
        BaseElement selected = dlg.getSelectedElements();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65809b8c-f725-4fb0-8cf4-7b92378b5837"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;/openapi/examples/elementselection</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573554 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00763: Showing notifications, adding text into Message Window

- page_id: `249573554`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573554/Showing+notifications+adding+text+into+Message+Window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:47:50.275+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Using and extending UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use API described bellow to show Notifications (a balloon windows on the bottom right corner of application window) and appending text into a Message Window.

**NotificationManager**

This is an entry point to display notifications in MagicDraw. Two types of notifications can be displayed: application level and container level. Container lever notifications are displayed in special container that implements *com.nomagic.ui.notification.NotificationsContainer* interface.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.NotificationManage.*

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t*o add some information into a Message Window,

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.Notification.getContext()* controls where notification is logged in the Message Window - into Environment or Project tab.

****

**GUILog**

Use *GUILog.log(java.lang.String)* to put a text into Project Message Window message.

Use *GUILog.log(Notification, boolean)* to log information from notification into a Message Window.

Use *GUILog.openLog()* to open Message Window.

For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>**/openapi/examples/notifications*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Showing question, error, warning dialogs' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Two types of notifications can be displayed: application level and container level. Container lever notifications are displayed in special container that implements <em>com.nomagic.ui.notification.NotificationsContainer</em> interface. </p><p>Use <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.NotificationManage. </em></p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t</em>o add some information into a Message Window,</p><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.Notification.getContext()</em> controls where notification is logged in the Message Window - into Environment or Project tab.</p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">GUILog</strong></p><p>Use <em>GUILog.log(java.lang.String)</em> to put a text into Project Message Window message.</p><p>Use <em>GUILog.log(Notification, boolean)</em> to log information from notification into a Message Window.</p><p>Use <em>GUILog.openLog()</em> to open Message Window.</p><p><br /></p><p>For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em><em>/openapi/examples/notifications</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c6dfaab-2bb5-4c81-aaca-b9acf1614fa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Showing question, error, warning dialogs" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573553 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00764: Showing question, error, warning dialogs

- page_id: `249573553`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573553/Showing+question+error+warning+dialogs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:13:08.465+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Using and extending UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog* class to show various simple UI dialogs.

**Error dialog**

Use *GUILog.showError(java.lang.String)*

Use*GUILog.showError(java.awt.Frame, java.lang.String, java.lang.Throwable)*to show error dialog with exception stack trace

**Warning dialog**

Use *GUILog.showWarning(java.lang.String)*

**Question dialog**

Use *GUILog.showQuestion(java.lang.String)*

**Message dialog**

Use *GUILog.showMessage(java.lang.String)*

Use *GUILog.showHTMLMessage(java.lang.String,**GUILog.URLActionHandler)* to show html message with some active links

**Log messages into Message Window**

Use *GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t*o add some information into a Message Window

See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Showing notifications, adding text into Message Window' space=''] for more information about showing Notifications and appending text into a Message Window

For more information**about other API choices see javadoc*.*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Showing notifications, adding text into Message Window' space='']
