<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125157 space=DEVG version=17 -->
## PAGE 00765: Specializations

- page_id: `296125157`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/296125157/Specializations
- version_number: 17
- version_date: `2026-04-15T20:42:23.968+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > New Relationship Creation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### 1. Subclassifications

Use the *[Subclassifications](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subclassifications.html).[setSuperClassifier()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subclassifications.html#setSuperClassifier(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Classifier,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Classifier))* methods to establish a subclassification relationship by assigning one or more super classifiers to an element; One variation accepts a single super classifier, while another allows passing a list of super classifiers.

```java

```

###### 2. Redefinitions

Use the *[Redefinitions](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html)* utility class to establish a redefinition relationship between features; the *[addRedefined()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#addRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))* method assigns a single redefined feature, while *[setRedefined()](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#setRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))* allows specifying multiple redefined features as a collection.

```java

```

###### 3. Subsettings

Use the*[Subsettings](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html)*utility class to define a subsetting relationship between features;the*[addSubsetted](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#addSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))()*method assigns a single subsetted feature, while*[setAnyKindSubsetted](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#setAnyKindSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))()***allows specifying multiple subsetted features as a collection.

```java

```

4. FeatureTypings

Use the*[FeatureTypings](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html)*utility class to assign a type to an element;the*[setType](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#setType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))()*method applies one or more types by accepting a collection, while*[addType](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#addType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))()*allows adding a single type incrementally to the element.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h5>1. Subclassifications</h5><p>Use the <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subclassifications.html">Subclassifications</a>.<a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subclassifications.html#setSuperClassifier(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Classifier,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Classifier)">setSuperClassifier()</a></em> methods to establish a subclassification relationship by assigning one or more super classifiers to an element; One variation accepts a single super classifier, while another allows passing a list of super classifiers.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0f5d4c3-b0b4-44d0-b2cc-0bca903e3a83"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subclassifications;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;

Project project = ...; 
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project); 
PartDefinition drone = elementsFactory.createPartDefinition(); 
PartDefinition singleRotor = elementsFactory.createPartDefinition(); 

Subclassifications.setSuperClassifier(singleRotor, drone);
//alternatively you can use
Subclassifications.setSuperClassifier(singleRotor, List.of(drone));]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h5>2. Redefinitions</h5><p>Use the <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html">Redefinitions</a></em> utility class to establish a redefinition relationship between features; the <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#addRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)">addRedefined()</a></em> method assigns a single redefined feature, while <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Redefinitions.html#setRedefined(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)">setRedefined()</a></em> allows specifying multiple redefined features as a collection.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aaac09ce-f77e-49e0-a7d2-c39b431ad820"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Redefinitions;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PortUsage;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;
 
Project project = ...; 
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project);
PortUsage rescueDronePort = elementsFactory.createPortUsage();
PortUsage basePort = elementsFactory.createPortUsage();

Redefinitions.addRedefined(rescueDronePort, basePort);
//alternatively
Redefinitions.setRedefined(rescueDronePort, List.of(basePort));]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h5><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">3. Subsettings</span></h5><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Use the<span> </span></span><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html">Subsettings</a></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>utility class to define a subsetting relationship between features; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the<span> </span></span><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#addSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)">addSubsetted</a>()</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>method assigns a single subsetted feature, while<span> </span></span><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#setAnyKindSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)">setAnyKindSubsetted</a>()</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em> </em>allows specifying multiple subsetted features as a collection.<br /><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67690263-b0ec-4556-9cde-ded4cfe3bfa0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.AttributeUsage;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;

Project project = ...; 
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project);
AttributeUsage mass = elementsFactory.createAttributeUsage();
AttributeUsage unitMass = elementsFactory.createAttributeUsage();

Subsettings.addSubsetted(mass, unitMass);
//alternatively
Subsettings.setAnyKindSubsetted(mass,List.of(unitMass));]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;font-weight: 600;"><br />4. FeatureTypings</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Use the</span><em> <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html">FeatureTypings</a></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>utility class to assign a type to an element; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the </span><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#setType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)">setType</a>()</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>method applies one or more types by accepting a collection, while<span> </span></span><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/FeatureTypings.html#addType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)">addType</a>()</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>allows adding a single type incrementally to the element.<br /><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="643bf8e8-4185-47fe-bb0f-6f1b7b115d9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.FeatureTypings;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.ItemDefinition;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.ItemUsage;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;

Project project = ...;
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project);
ItemDefinition searchCmd = elementsFactory.createItemDefinition();
ItemUsage searchCmdOut = elementsFactory.createItemUsage();

FeatureTypings.setType(searchCmdOut,List.of(searchCmd));
//alternatively
FeatureTypings.addType(searchCmdOut,searchCmd);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h5><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /><br /><br /></span></h5>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298095513 space=DEVG version=11 -->
## PAGE 00766: Specializations Utility Class

- page_id: `298095513`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/298095513/Specializations+Utility+Class
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2026-04-15T20:19:56.340+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html)` utility class provides helper methods for navigating specialization relationships between KerML `Type` elements.

It can be used to:

- retrieve direct subtypes
- retrieve direct supertypes
- iterate over supertypes
- find a matching supertype using a predicate
- retrieve the first non-implied general type
- collect subtypes recursively

A `[Specialization](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Specialization.html)` represents a relationship in which one `[Type](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html)` (the specific type) specializes another `[Type](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html)` (the general type). A type may have multiple direct supertypes and multiple direct subtypes.

#### **Overview**

Use this utility class when you need to inspect type hierarchies, such as:

- finding all types that specialize a base type
- finding the parent types of a given type
- locating a supertype that matches some condition
- traversing an inheritance hierarchy recursively

The utility works with KerML `Type` and`[Specialization](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Specialization.html)` elements and supports filtering subtype traversal by specialization kind.

#### **Discovering Direct Subtypes**

##### [streamOfSubTypes](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#streamOfSubTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.lang.Class))(Type type, Class<? extends Specialization> specializationKind)

Returns a stream of direct subtypes of the given type, filtered by specialization kind.

```text
System.out.println(subType.getName()));
]]>
```

In this example, `Specialization.class` includes all direct subtypes connected through standard specialization relationships.

#### **Discovering Direct Supertypes**

##### [streamOfSuperTypes](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#streamOfSuperTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))(Type type)

Returns a stream of all direct supertypes of the given type.

```text
System.out.println(superType.getName()));
]]>
```

##### [getSuperTypes](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#getSuperTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))(Type type)

Returns all direct supertypes as a `List<Type>`.

```text

```

##### [forEachSuperType](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#forEachSuperType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.util.function.Consumer))(Type type, Consumer<Type> consumer)

Applies the provided consumer to each direct supertype.

```text
System.out.println(superType.getQualifiedName()));
]]>
```

This is useful when you want to process supertypes directly without creating an intermediate collection.

#### **Excluding Implied General Types**

##### [getFirstNonImpliedGeneralType](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#getFirstNonImpliedGeneralType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))(Type type)

Returns the first direct general type that is not implied.

```text

```

This method is useful when you want to work only with explicitly modeled generalization relationships and ignore implied ones.

#### **Matching Supertypes with Predicates**

##### [firstSuperTypeMatching](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#firstSuperTypeMatching(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.util.function.Predicate))(Type type, Predicate<Type> predicate)

Returns the first direct supertype that matches the given predicate.

```text
t.getName() != null && t.getName().startsWith("Base")
);
]]>
```

##### [firstSuperTypeMatching](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#firstSuperTypeMatching(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.util.function.Predicate,java.lang.Class))(Type type, Predicate<Type> predicate, Class<T> typeClass)

Returns the first direct supertype that both matches the predicate and is an instance of the specified type.

```text
"BasePart".equals(t.getName()),
        PartDefinition.class
);
]]>
```

#### **Traversing Subtypes Recursively**

##### [getSubTypesRecursively](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#getSubTypesRecursively(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.lang.Class))(Type type, Class<? extends Specialization> specializationKind)

Returns all recursive subtypes of the given type, excluding the type itself.

```text

```

##### [getSubTypesRecursivelyIncludingSelf](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#getSubTypesRecursivelyIncludingSelf(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.lang.Class))(Type type, Class<? extends Specialization> specializationKind)

Returns all recursive subtypes of the given type, including the type itself.

```text

```

This method is useful when you want to process the entire hierarchy starting from the root type.

#### **Notes**

- getSubTypesRecursively() excludes the starting type, while getSubTypesRecursivelyIncludingSelf() includes it.
- The recursive traversal protects against revisiting already visited types.
- Direct supertype operations work on owned specializations of the given type.
- If you specifically need only explicit generalization relationships, use getFirstNonImpliedGeneralType() .

#### **Summary**

The `[Specializations](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html)` utility class is the main helper for exploring type specialization hierarchies in KerML. It provides concise APIs for discovering direct subtype and supertype relationships, matching supertypes, and traversing subtype trees recursively.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations</a></code> utility class provides helper methods for navigating specialization relationships between KerML <code>Type</code> elements.</p><p>It can be used to:</p><ul><li>retrieve direct subtypes</li><li>retrieve direct supertypes</li><li>iterate over supertypes</li><li>find a matching supertype using a predicate</li><li>retrieve the first non-implied general type</li><li>collect subtypes recursively</li></ul><p>A <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Specialization.html">Specialization</a></code> represents a relationship in which one <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html">Type</a></code> (the specific type) specializes another <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html">Type</a></code> (the general type). A type may have multiple direct supertypes and multiple direct subtypes.</p><h2><strong>Overview</strong></h2><p>Use this utility class when you need to inspect type hierarchies, such as:</p><ul><li>finding all types that specialize a base type</li><li>finding the parent types of a given type</li><li>locating a supertype that matches some condition</li><li>traversing an inheritance hierarchy recursively</li></ul><p>The utility works with KerML <code>Type</code> and<code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Specialization.html">Specialization</a></code> elements and supports filtering subtype traversal by specialization kind.</p><h2><strong>Discovering Direct Subtypes</strong></h2><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#streamOfSubTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.lang.Class)">streamOfSubTypes</a>(Type type, Class&lt;? extends Specialization&gt; specializationKind)</h3><p>Returns a stream of direct subtypes of the given type, filtered by specialization kind.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d2d3b4e-535a-45ec-ab5f-e9b178225643"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Specialization;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type baseType = ...;

Specializations.streamOfSubTypes(baseType, Specialization.class)
        .forEach(subType -> System.out.println(subType.getName()));
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In this example, <code>Specialization.class</code> includes all direct subtypes connected through standard specialization relationships.</p><h2><strong>Discovering Direct Supertypes</strong></h2><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#streamOfSuperTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)">streamOfSuperTypes</a>(Type type)</h3><p>Returns a stream of all direct supertypes of the given type.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3cbdd74b-5c88-4111-879b-7b48c24d699f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type type = ...;

Specializations.streamOfSuperTypes(type)
        .forEach(superType -> System.out.println(superType.getName()));
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#getSuperTypes(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)">getSuperTypes</a>(Type type)</h3><p>Returns all direct supertypes as a <code>List&lt;Type&gt;</code>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8006b25c-d7f3-4b53-a313-e318b0fa72fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type type = ...;

for (Type superType : Specializations.getSuperTypes(type))
{
    System.out.println(superType.getName());
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#forEachSuperType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.util.function.Consumer)">forEachSuperType</a>(Type type, Consumer&lt;Type&gt; consumer)</h3><p>Applies the provided consumer to each direct supertype.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0bf914a8-e19c-4b45-8bd1-e28077efc03a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type type = ...;

Specializations.forEachSuperType(type, superType ->
        System.out.println(superType.getQualifiedName()));
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This is useful when you want to process supertypes directly without creating an intermediate collection.</p><h2><strong>Excluding Implied General Types</strong></h2><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#getFirstNonImpliedGeneralType(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)">getFirstNonImpliedGeneralType</a>(Type type)</h3><p>Returns the first direct general type that is not implied.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39692095-a1bb-479e-8afa-7cf0e218f7a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type type = ...;

Type explicitGeneral = Specializations.getFirstNonImpliedGeneralType(type);
if (explicitGeneral != null)
{
    System.out.println(explicitGeneral.getName());
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This method is useful when you want to work only with explicitly modeled generalization relationships and ignore implied ones.</p><h2><strong>Matching Supertypes with Predicates</strong></h2><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#firstSuperTypeMatching(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.util.function.Predicate)">firstSuperTypeMatching</a>(Type type, Predicate&lt;Type&gt; predicate)</h3><p>Returns the first direct supertype that matches the given predicate.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="703cede6-7f4c-48ee-a63a-29e57ae3e25d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type type = ...;

Type match = Specializations.firstSuperTypeMatching(
        type,
        t -> t.getName() != null && t.getName().startsWith("Base")
);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#firstSuperTypeMatching(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.util.function.Predicate,java.lang.Class)">firstSuperTypeMatching</a>(Type type, Predicate&lt;Type&gt; predicate, Class&lt;T&gt; typeClass)</h3><p>Returns the first direct supertype that both matches the predicate and is an instance of the specified type.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1782108c-be06-40d9-84a6-5fcdf069e338"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;

Type type = ...;

PartDefinition partDefinition = Specializations.firstSuperTypeMatching(
        type,
        t -> "BasePart".equals(t.getName()),
        PartDefinition.class
);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Traversing Subtypes Recursively</strong></h2><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#getSubTypesRecursively(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.lang.Class)">getSubTypesRecursively</a>(Type type, Class&lt;? extends Specialization&gt; specializationKind)</h3><p>Returns all recursive subtypes of the given type, excluding the type itself.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a55af5f3-e076-4a0f-ad4b-35f76616851f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Specialization;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type baseType = ...;

for (Type subType : Specializations.getSubTypesRecursively(baseType, Specialization.class))
{
    System.out.println(subType.getName());
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html#getSubTypesRecursivelyIncludingSelf(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.lang.Class)">getSubTypesRecursivelyIncludingSelf</a>(Type type, Class&lt;? extends Specialization&gt; specializationKind)</h3><p>Returns all recursive subtypes of the given type, including the type itself.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="689b2cef-6271-4e3f-ac49-612202e4e162"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Specializations;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Specialization;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type baseType = ...;

for (Type typeInHierarchy : Specializations.getSubTypesRecursivelyIncludingSelf(baseType, Specialization.class))
{
    System.out.println(typeInHierarchy.getName());
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This method is useful when you want to process the entire hierarchy starting from the root type.</p><h2><strong>Notes</strong></h2><ul><li><code>getSubTypesRecursively()</code> excludes the starting type, while <code>getSubTypesRecursivelyIncludingSelf()</code> includes it.</li><li>The recursive traversal protects against revisiting already visited types.</li><li>Direct supertype operations work on owned specializations of the given type.</li><li>If you specifically need only explicit generalization relationships, use <code>getFirstNonImpliedGeneralType()</code>.</li></ul><h2><strong>Summary</strong></h2><p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Specializations.html">Specializations</a></code> utility class is the main helper for exploring type specialization hierarchies in KerML. It provides concise APIs for discovering direct subtype and supertype relationships, matching supertypes, and traversing subtype trees recursively.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573598 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00767: Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties

- page_id: `249573598`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573598/Specifying+batch+mode+program+classpath+and+required+system+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-05-05T09:46:36.998+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Running programs in batch mode
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw or other modeling tools run as a simple Java program.

In order to launch the program from the command line, the launcher is started using a *java* command line. The classpath has to include jar files from the lib folder and its subfolders (additional requirements exist for different command line implementations).

There is one mandatory property that has to be specified as well:

- -Desi.system.config=data/application.conf

The property is specified as a relative path to*<modeling tool installation directory>, therefore,* you either have to make your current directory before launching your program or specify absolute paths for the above-listed properties.

There are two types of command lines.

1. Core related batch program: A program that does not call/depend on any functionality from any plugins. Should only depend/call core tool functionality.
  - Must extend *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine*
  - Must contain the Java *main* method and implement *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#execute*
2. Plugin related batch program: A program that can call/depend on any running modeling tool plugin
  - `com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action` - specifies the batch mode action (implementation of *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction*) you want to execute, for example, `-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample`
  - Must launch using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher*
  - The *CommandLineAction* must be registered in *CommandLineActionManager.getInstance().addAction(new CommandLineActionExample());* e.g. in your plugin *init()* method
  - The plugin where *CommandLineAction* is registered must load and run, in order for the command line program to launch it. (this means plugin.xml and plugin .jar files must be in the /plugins directory)

Also, it is possible to set up the command line to use the floating license server or license file - see "Providing licensing information" at [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Licensing information' space=''].

The implemented examples can be found in :

- Core batch program: <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\imagegenerator
- Plugin batch program: <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin

The examples of complete command lines that launch the batch mode programs:

```text
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/*;openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]>
```

```text
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/* ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]>
```

```bash
cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-cp lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/*\:openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]>
```

```bash
cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-cp lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/* \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]>
```

You can modify the previous examples and create more sophisticated batch files. For example, you can define the directory to run the tool from, or specify to use the same java, as the tool uses itself. The exemplary scripts that can be used to start the command line program are shown in the following script examples. The scripts read and use the same classpath as the modeling tool when launching it manually(this is the preferred way to define classpaths).

These scripts can be launched from any directory. Can be modified to read other properties files (e.g. csm.properties, cea.properties)

A script that starts the Windows Core batch command line program on Windows:

```text

```

A script that starts the Windows Plugin batch command line program on Windows:

```text

```

The shell script that starts a Core batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:

```bash

```

The shell script that starts a Plugin batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:

```bash

```

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Licensing information' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw or other modeling tools run as a simple Java program.</p><p>In order to launch the program from the command line, the launcher is started using a <em>java</em> command line. The classpath has to include jar files from the lib folder and its subfolders (additional requirements exist for different command line implementations).</p><p>There is one mandatory property that has to be specified as well:</p><ul><li><code>-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf</code></li></ul><p>The property is specified as a relative path to<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;, therefore,</em> you either have to make your current directory before launching your program or specify absolute paths for the above-listed properties.</p><p>There are two types of command lines.</p><ol><li>Core related batch program:<br />A program that does not call/depend on any functionality from any plugins. Should only depend/call core tool functionality.<br /><ul><li><p>Must extend <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine</em></p></li><li><p>Must contain the Java <em>main</em> method and implement <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#execute</em></p></li></ul></li><li>Plugin related batch program:<br />A program that can call/depend on any running modeling tool plugin<br /><ul><li><p><code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action</code> - specifies the batch mode action (implementation of <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction</em>) you want to execute, for example, <code>-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample</code> </p></li><li><p>Must launch using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher</em> </p></li><li><p>The <em>CommandLineAction</em> must be registered in <em>CommandLineActionManager.getInstance().addAction(<span style="color: rgb(0,0,128);">new <span>CommandLineActionExample</span></span>());</em> e.g. in your plugin <em>init()</em> method</p></li><li><p>The plugin where <em>CommandLineAction</em> is registered must load and run, in order for the command line program to launch it. (this means plugin.xml and plugin .jar files must be in the /plugins directory)</p></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Also, it is possible to set up the command line to use the floating license server or license file - see &quot;Providing licensing information&quot; at <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Licensing information" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The implemented examples can be found in :</p><ul><li>Core batch program: <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\imagegenerator</em></li><li>Plugin batch program: <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin</em></li></ul><p>The examples of complete command lines that launch the batch mode programs:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99475978-37bf-4c51-bc15-1f59efb94d7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Core batch program command line for Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\MagicDraw>java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/*;openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68eeab44-55ae-4fbd-a0c6-034bea80270a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Plugin batch program command line for Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\MagicDraw>java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/* ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63cfa508-e9b8-49ee-b849-2f19c8a54bf4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Core batch program command line for Mac OS-X and Linux</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[> cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-cp lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/*\:openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee546ddc-2f6b-40a3-b63f-52533f990fbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Plugin batch program command line for Mac OS-X and Linux</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[> cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-cp lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/* \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can modify the previous examples and create more sophisticated batch files. For example, you can define the directory to run the tool from, or specify to use the same java, as the tool uses itself. The exemplary scripts that can be used to start the command line program are shown in the following script examples. The scripts read and use the same classpath as the modeling tool when launching it manually(this is the preferred way to define classpaths). </p><p>These scripts can be launched from any directory. Can be modified to read other properties files (e.g. csm.properties, cea.properties)</p><p><br /></p><p>A script that starts the Windows Core batch command line program on Windows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dce90f91-c03a-4ceb-9799-f0f15ffdcb23"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Core batch program Windows batch file that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[@echo off
setlocal EnableExtensions
 
:: either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
set "MAGICDRAW_HOME=C:\Program Files\MagicDraw"

if "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" == "" (
    echo MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder
    exit /B 1
)

setlocal enableDelayedExpansion
:: change slashes if needed
set MAGICDRAW_HOME=!MAGICDRAW_HOME:\=/!
setlocal disableDelayedExpansion
  
pushd "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" 
  
:: Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
For /F "tokens=1* delims==" %%A IN (bin\magicdraw.properties) DO (
    IF "%%A"=="CLASSPATH" set MD_CLASSPATH=%%B
)

:: Modify classpath to a valid Windows style classpath. Replace \: with ;
set MD_CLASSPATH=%MD_CLASSPATH:\:=;%
  
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
     -cp "%MD_CLASSPATH%;openapi\examples\imagegenerator\imagegenerator.jar" ^
	 -Desi.system.config="data\application.conf" ^
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
     @bin/vm.options ^
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages %*
	 
popd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>A script that starts the Windows Plugin batch command line program on Windows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a8e4ea4-9487-4856-a3be-e70f1477257a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Plugin batch program Windows batch file that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[@echo off
setlocal EnableExtensions
  
:: either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
set "MAGICDRAW_HOME=C:\Program Files\MagicDraw"
 
if "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" == "" (
    echo MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder
    exit /B 1
)
 
setlocal enableDelayedExpansion
:: change slashes if needed
set MAGICDRAW_HOME=!MAGICDRAW_HOME:\=/!
setlocal disableDelayedExpansion
   
pushd "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%"
   
:: Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
For /F "tokens=1* delims==" %%A IN (bin\magicdraw.properties) DO (
    IF "%%A"=="CLASSPATH" set MD_CLASSPATH=%%B
)
 
:: Modify classpath to a valid Windows style classpath. Replace \: with ;
set MD_CLASSPATH=%MD_CLASSPATH:\:=;%
   
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
     -cp "%MD_CLASSPATH%" ^
     -Desi.system.config="data\application.conf" ^
	 -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
     @bin/vm.options ^
     -Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher %*
      
popd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The shell script that starts a Core batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5be5d473-9a0b-48d2-a3c0-0a76396dbbe3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Core batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

# either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
MAGICDRAW_HOME="/home/myuser/Desktop/MagicDraw"

if [ -z "$MAGICDRAW_HOME" ]; then
    echo "MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder"
    return
fi
 
cd "$MAGICDRAW_HOME"

# Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
MD_CLASSPATH=`grep "CLASSPATH" bin/magicdraw.properties | cut -d'=' -f 2`

if [ "$OS" = Windows_NT ]; then
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/;/g")
    cp_delim=";"
else
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/:/g")
    cp_delim=":"
fi
 
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
     -cp "$MD_CLASSPATH${cp_delim}openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar" \
     -Desi.system.config="data/application.conf" \
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
     @bin/vm.options \
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages "$@"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The shell script that starts a Plugin batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85328b8b-15bf-4f75-8650-aaf9919a4c60"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Plugin batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

# either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
MAGICDRAW_HOME="/home/myuser/Desktop/MagicDraw"

if [ -z "$MAGICDRAW_HOME" ]; then
    echo "MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder"
    return
fi
 
cd "$MAGICDRAW_HOME"

# Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
MD_CLASSPATH=`grep "CLASSPATH" bin/magicdraw.properties | cut -d'=' -f 2`

if [ "$OS" = Windows_NT ]; then
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/;/g")
else
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/:/g")
fi
 
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
     -cp "$MD_CLASSPATH" \
     -Desi.system.config="data/application.conf" \
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
     @bin/vm.options \
     -Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher "$@"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Licensing information" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573524 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00768: Specifying the appearance of cells and the row or column headers

- page_id: `249573524`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573524/Specifying+the+appearance+of+cells+and+the+row+or+column+headers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:58:02.659+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with diagrams > Working with Dependency Matrix > Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['cell-renderer', 'column-header-cell-renderer', 'row', 'header-cell-renderer', 'dependency-matrix-configurator', 'table-cell-renderer', 'matrix-cell-view', 'abstract-matrix-cell']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To customize the appearance of a dependency matrix, you have to register cell renderer components. There are three types of the renderers:

- A cell renderer
- A column header cell renderer
- A row header cell renderer

To register a cell renderer component, use the instance of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class, created while registering a custom dependency matrix. All these renderers should implement the standard *javax.swing.table.TableCellRenderer* interface.

The cell renderer is used to represent dependencies in a dependency matrix. The renderer component of a single cell gets either the value of the *Integer* class or the instance of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.MatrixCellView* class. The *java.lang.Integer* class represents dependency count. And the *MatrixCellView* class has a reference to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell* class, which has all the information about the dependencies in the cell.

The row and column cell renderers works the same way. To get the particular row or column header element inside the cell, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.ui.table.renderer.RendererHelper.getHeaderElement(javax.swing.JTable, int, int)* method.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To customize the appearance of a dependency matrix, you have to register cell renderer components. There are three types of the renderers:</p><ul><li>A cell renderer</li><li>A column header cell renderer</li><li>A row header cell renderer</li></ul><p>To register a cell renderer component, use the instance of the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator</em> class, created while registering a custom dependency matrix. All these renderers should implement the standard <em>javax.swing.table.TableCellRenderer</em> interface.</p><p>The cell renderer is used to represent dependencies in a dependency matrix. The renderer component of a single cell gets either the value of the <em>Integer</em> class or the instance of the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.MatrixCellView</em> class. The <em>java.lang.Integer</em> class represents dependency count. And the <em>MatrixCellView</em> class has a reference to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell</em> class, which has all the information about the dependencies in the cell.</p><p>The row and column cell renderers works the same way. To get the particular row or column header element inside the cell, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.ui.table.renderer.RendererHelper.getHeaderElement(javax.swing.JTable, int, int)</em> method.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573452 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00769: Standard profiles implementation

- page_id: `249573452`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573452/Standard+profiles+implementation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:35:29.637+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > Working with stereotypes and tagged values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard profiles implementation are available as part of API.

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile* to access "MagicDraw Profile" elements

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile* to access "StandardProfile" elements

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ValidationProfile***to access "Validation Profile" elements

MagicDraw plugins (like SysML) can also expose implementations of profiles. Check their APIs.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Standard profiles implementation are available as part of API.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile</em> to access &quot;MagicDraw Profile&quot; elements</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile</em> to access &quot;StandardProfile&quot; elements</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ValidationProfile</em><em> </em>to access &quot;Validation Profile&quot; elements </p><p><br /></p><p>MagicDraw plugins (like SysML) can also expose implementations of profiles. Check their APIs.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573640 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00770: Starting execution

- page_id: `249573640`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573640/Starting+execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.338+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1 > Alf Plugin Developer Guide > Cameo Simulation Toolkit > Java APIs > Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With Cameo Simulation Toolkit, you can start executing an element using the following Open APIs

- SimulationManager.execute(Element element) : SimulationSession
- SimulationManager.execute(Element element, Boolean isMainSession, Boolean start) : SimulationSession

Once you use the aforementioned Open APIs, Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates corresponding simulation sessions. The conditions when using the Open APIs to execute an element are as follows

- The specified element could be any executable element, such as a Class, a Behavior, or an simulation configuration element.
- If the executed element is intended to be the main element of the execution, set the "isMainSession" flag to true. Then, the created session will become the main session of the execution. The main session is the first session created once the execution starts. If the main session is terminated, all executions will be terminated accordingly.
- If the "start" flag is set to true, the created session will start itself automatically (similar to the 'autorun' option in an SimulationConfig).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>With Cameo Simulation Toolkit, you can start executing an element using the following Open APIs</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="12a1efe7-0e1f-4d71-b787-b371ebf298cc"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ee8cf1d-fc1b-45b8-96f9-007dadbadfc4">SimulationManager.execute(Element element) : SimulationSession</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="21e3b246-3329-4c35-ad35-45db8faa59ca"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9dd29a01-11b3-4d69-a624-de481b5d9573">SimulationManager.execute(Element element, Boolean isMainSession, Boolean start) : SimulationSession</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p>Once you use the aforementioned Open APIs, Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates corresponding simulation sessions. The conditions when using the Open APIs to execute an element are as follows</p><ul><li>The specified element could be any executable element, such as a Class, a Behavior, or an simulation configuration element.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="469dc2f0-9737-41d3-a2a0-95d515839ccc"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0e768531-ccd4-45a4-a498-fc8a90ebe099">If the executed element is intended to be the main element of the execution, set the &quot;isMainSession&quot; flag to true. Then, the created session will become the main session of the execution. The main session is the first session created once the execution starts. If the main session is terminated, all executions will be terminated accordingly.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>If the &quot;start&quot; flag is set to true, the created session will start itself automatically (similar to the 'autorun' option in an SimulationConfig).</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573600 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00771: Starting MagicDraw or other modeling tool as part of another application

- page_id: `249573600`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573600/Starting+MagicDraw+or+other+modeling+tool+as+part+of+another+application
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:10:36.753+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To integrate MagicDraw or other modeling tool into your application you should launch MagicDraw directly from your Java application. This would allow you to interact with MagicDraw or other modeling tool classes directly.

In order to do that you must:

- Include lib/ folder and all of its subfolder into your project classpath
- Import class com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application and call the appropriate start(...) method. (see Javadoc)
- Make sure there is only one instance of the modeling tool running in the same JVM(multiple instances cannot be started in the same JVM)

For launching the modeling tool straight from the command line, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running programs in batch mode' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To integrate MagicDraw or other modeling tool into your application you should launch MagicDraw directly from your Java application. This would allow you to interact with MagicDraw or other modeling tool classes directly.</p><p>In order to do that you must:</p><ul><li>Include lib/ folder and all of its subfolder into your project classpath</li><li>Import class com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application and call the appropriate <em>start(...)</em> method. (see Javadoc)</li><li>Make sure there is only one instance of the modeling tool running in the same JVM(multiple instances cannot be started in the same JVM) </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>For launching the modeling tool straight from the command line, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Running programs in batch mode" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573644 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00772: Stopping execution

- page_id: `249573644`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573644/Stopping+execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.356+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1 > Alf Plugin Developer Guide > Cameo Simulation Toolkit > Java APIs > Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can stop an execution by terminating its main session. Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to terminate any simulation session using the following Open API

- SimulationManager.terminateSession(SimulationSession session) : void

Terminating a session will also cause all children of the specific session to be terminated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can stop an execution by terminating its main session. Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to terminate any simulation session using the following Open API</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.terminateSession(SimulationSession session) : void</li></ul><p>Terminating a session will also cause all children of the specific session to be terminated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094772 space=DEVG version=6 -->
## PAGE 00773: Subsettings Utility Class

- page_id: `298094772`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/298094772/Subsettings+Utility+Class
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-04-16T04:59:00.572+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In KerML, *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Subsetting](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html)* expresses that the value set of one feature — the subsetting feature — is a subset of another — the subsetted feature. Every value of the subsetting feature is therefore also a value of the subsetted feature.

*[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html)*is the utility class for programmatic subsetting operations.

#### **Getting Subsettings**

[*getAnyKindSubsetting*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#getAnyKindSubsetting(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))returns the features, in the context of a given type, that subset a specified feature. The *AnyKind* variants also take [Redefinition](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html) into account.

```java
subsettors = Subsettings.getAnyKindSubsetting(vehicleParts, electricVehicle);
// → [EV::engines, EV::batteryPacks] if both subset parts in EV's context]]>
```

[isAnyKindSubsetting](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#isAnyKindSubsetting(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))checks whether one feature subsets another, directly or through redefinition.

```java

```

#### **Setting Subsetted**

[*setAnyKindSubsetted*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#setAnyKindSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))replaces all existing owned subsetting relationships on a feature with a new one pointing to the specified subsetted feature. Passing *null* removes all explicit subsettings.

```java

```

[*addSubsetted*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#addSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature))appends a new plain [Subsetting](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html)without removing existing ones.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In KerML, <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Subsetting</a></em> expresses that the value set of one feature — the subsetting feature — is a subset of another — the subsetted feature. Every value of the subsetting feature is therefore also a value of the subsetted feature.</p><p><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings</a> </em>is the utility class for programmatic subsetting operations.</p><h2><strong>Getting Subsettings</strong></h2><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#getAnyKindSubsetting(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)"><em>getAnyKindSubsetting</em> </a>returns the features, in the context of a given type, that subset a specified feature. The <em>AnyKind</em> variants also take <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html">Redefinition</a> into account.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee1eb410-f496-4883-962f-68343cd26786"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature;

// Which features in ElectricVehicle subset Vehicle::parts?
List<Feature> subsettors = Subsettings.getAnyKindSubsetting(vehicleParts, electricVehicle);
// → [EV::engines, EV::batteryPacks] if both subset parts in EV's context]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#isAnyKindSubsetting(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)">isAnyKindSubsetting </a>checks whether one feature subsets another, directly or through redefinition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71bd710c-bbe4-49c3-81d9-7f7fd8db32bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;

// Check if a feature subsets another one.
boolean subsets = Subsettings.isAnyKindSubsetting(subsettingFeature, subsettedFeature);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Setting Subsetted</strong></h2><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#setAnyKindSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)"><em>setAnyKindSubsetted</em> </a>replaces all existing owned subsetting relationships on a feature with a new one pointing to the specified subsetted feature. Passing <em>null</em> removes all explicit subsettings.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f152b96-c4d7-40aa-a395-6e8bfbf0af9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;

// Vehicle::engines subsets Vehicle::parts
Subsettings.setAnyKindSubsetted(engines, parts);

// Remove all explicit subsettings
Subsettings.setAnyKindSubsetted(engines, null);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Subsettings.html#addSubsetted(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature)"><em>addSubsetted</em> </a>appends a new plain <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Subsetting.html">Subsetting </a>without removing existing ones.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d531ba21-f9be-4f17-ac6b-6e0b060bc9ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Subsettings;

// engines already subsets parts — add another subsetting
Subsettings.addSubsetted(engines, motorisedComponents);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573611 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00774: Supported Changes from UML 2.4.1 to UML 2.5

- page_id: `249573611`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573611/Supported+Changes+from+UML+2.4.1+to+UML+2.5
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:15.273+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins migration > Plugins migration to MagicDraw 18.0 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33

##### Metamodel Changes

All the changes are grouped by the categories:

44

###### New property names added

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Classifier::conveyingFlow | - |
| DeployedArtifact::deploymentForArtifact |  |
| PackageableElement::import |  |

###### Properties renamed

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| NamedElement::classifierinheritingClassifier | - |
| Classifier::classnestingClass |  |
| ParameterableElement::parameterSubstitutionowningTemplateParameterSubstitution |  |

###### Derivation changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Property::/classifier : Classifier [0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Feature::featuringClassifier, subsets RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext} | All these properties have been made automatically derived and read-only unions. |
| InputPin::/action : Action[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| Action::/input : InputPin[*]{ordered, readOnly, union, subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| OutputPin::/action : Action[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| NamedElement::/namespace : Namespace[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| DirectedRelationship::/source : Element [1..*]{readOnly, union, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} | Both properties have been made automatically derived and read-only unions. This feature has been supported in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |
| DirectedRelationship::/target : Element [1..*]{readOnly, union, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} |  |
| NamedElement::/clientDependency | The Client Dependency property of a named element has been made automatically derived. This feature has been supported in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |

###### Order changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| AcceptEventAction::result : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Action::output} | The ability to order output pins holding the values received from an event occurrence, has been available in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |
| Association::/endType : Type [1..*]{ordered, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} | The set of classifiers that are used as end types of the association, is no longer ordered. |
| Classifier::/attribute : Property [0..*]{ordered, union, subsets Classifier::feature} | The ability to order the attributes, which are direct properties of a classifier has been available in earlier versions of MagicDraw.Though the Attribute property cannot be edited, its value displays the same order of the attributes as is defined in other places of the classifier specification, such as the Owned Attribute property or the Attributes property group. Thus to change the Attribute property value, change the value of a relevant property. |
| ConnectableElement::/end : ConnectorEnd [0..*]{ordered} | A set of connector ends that attach to the particular connectable element, is no longer ordered. |
| DurationObservation::event : NamedElement [1..2]{ordered} | Event elements are now automatically ordered. |
| ReplyAction::♦ replyValue : InputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Action::input} | The set of input pins can now be ordered. In the Reply Value property value cell, click the button to open the dialog for reordering. |
| UnmarshallAction::♦result : OutputPin [1..*]{ordered, subsets Action::output} | The set of output pins can now be ordered. In the Result property value cell, click the button to open the dialog for reordering. |

###### Other changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| LoopNode::♦loopVariable : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Element::ownedElement} | The Loop Variable property has been made composite. |
| OutputPin::loopNode : LoopNode[0..1]{subsets Element::owner} |  |
| Activity::structuredNode : StructuredActivityNode [0..*]{readOnly, subsets Activity::node, Activity::group} | The property has been made read-only in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |

###### Properties removed

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Property::/default : String[0..1] | The Default property no longer appears in a property specification. The value of the Default Value property will be used instead of the removed property value. |

##### Notation Changes

From now on, the inherited members are denoted with the caret "^" sign.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Profile changes

Standard/system profiles *StandardProfileL2* and *StandardProfileL3* have been merged into a single one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']
