<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573615 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00775: Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1

- page_id: `249573615`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573615/Supported+UML+Specification+Changes+from+Version+2.3+to+2.4.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:35.380+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins migration > Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Supported UML specification changes include:

44

###### Metamodel Changes

| Metamodel Change | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| DATA TYPES ADDED |  |
| Real | Added to the PrimitiveTypes package. You can now use values of a real type in your models. |
| METACLASSES ADDED |  |
| LiteralReal | Specifies the real value. |
| METACLASSES CHANGED |  |
| DestructionEvent → DestructionOccurrenceSpecification | The DestructionEvent class has been renamed to DestructionOccurrenceSpecification. It represents the destruction of an instance described by the lifeline that contains this instance.DestructionOccurrenceSpecification is a specialization of MessageOccurrenceSpecification. |
| METACLASSES REMOVED |  |
| ExecutionEvent | These metaclasses have been removed from the Interactions package due to unsupported event types. |
| CreationEvent |  |
| ReceiveOperationEvent |  |
| ReceiveSignalEvent |  |
| SendOperationEvent |  |
| SendSignalEvent |  |
| PROPERTIES ADDED |  |
| Package::URI:String[0..1] {id} | Provides the package with an identifier that can be used for many purposes. URI is the universally unique identification of the package following the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396 http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt and it must comply with those syntax rules. You can now specify this property for packages, profiles, and models. For the notation of the URI property, see . |
| Property::isID:Boolean=False | Indicates that the property can be used to uniquely identify an instance of the containing class, when the value is set to true. The default value is false. For the notation of the isID property modifier, see . |
| InteractionUse::returnValue:ValueSpecification [0..1] | Specifies the value returned by the executed interaction. |
| InteractionUse::returnValueRecipient:Property [0..1] | Specifies the recipient of the value which is returned by the executed interaction. |
| EnumerationLiteral::/classifier:Enumaration[1] | The classifier of this EnumerationLiteral should now be equal to the enumeration that contains this enumeration literal. Redefines InstanceSpecification::classifier. |
| ASSOCIATIONS REMOVED |  |
| ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification::event: ExecutionEvent[1] | The association from ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification to ExecutionEvent has been removed, as the ExecutionEvent metaclass does not exist in the UML 2.4.1 |
| OccurenceSpecification::event:Event[1] | The specification of the occurring event is not referenced any more. |
| BehavioredClassifier::ownedTrigger:Trigger[0..*] | Trigger descriptions owned by a classifier are not referenced any more. |
| SUBSETTING CHANGES |  |
| Interface::redefinedInterface {subsets redefinedElement → redefinedClassifier} | An interface now references all the interfaces that are redefined by this interface. |
| SUBSETTING ADDEDNote: These features were already available in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |  |
| Duration::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} | Element or elements can be selected or created only under the owned element’s scope. |
| LinkEndData::qualifier:QualifierValue[*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| LinkAction::endData:LinkEndData[2..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| CreateLinkAction::endData:LinkEndCreationData[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| DestroyLinkAction::endData:LinkEndDestructionData[2..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| TimeExpression::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| ValuePin::value:ValueSpecification[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| State::deferrableTrigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::edge:ActivityEdge[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| ValueSpecificationAction::value: ValueSpecification[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| AcceptEventAction::trigger:Triger[1..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Stereotype::icon:Image[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| TimeEvent::when:TimeExpression[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| InteractionUse::argument:ValueSpecification[*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode [0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| SequenceNode::executableNode: ExecutableNode[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Transition::trigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Classifier::/feature {subsets Namespace::member} | The /feature property of Classifier is now included as a part of the member property. |
| Feature::/featuringClassifier {subsets NamedElement::memberNamespace} | The /featuringClassifier property of Feature is now included as a part of the memberNamespace property. |
| Property::owningAssociation {subsets RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext} | A property now references the owning association of this property. |
| DEFAULT VALUE CHANGES |  |
| DurationObservation::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] =True | The default value has been removed. In effect, the value of the firstEvent property is now by default empty. |
| DurationConstraint::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] =True |  |
| PackageableElement::visibility:VisibilityKind[1] =False → Public | The default value of this property has been changed to Public.This means that packageable elements are now by default public. |
| MULTIPLICITY CHANGES |  |
| EnumerationLiteral::enumeration:Enumeration [0..1] → [1] | The Enumeration must now be specified. It has become mandatory. |
| Multiplicity of ExecutionSpecification association with ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification: [1] → [0..2] | ExecutionSpecification is now associated with two ExecutionOccurrenceSpecifications:The start ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the start of an action or behavior.The finish ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the finish of an action or behavior. |
| DERIVATION CHANGES |  |
| Constraint::/context → context | The context property of the constraint is no longer derived. It is now a settable property. |
| Message::/signature → signature | The signature of the message is no longer derived. However, it remains read-only. |

###### Notation changes

1. If there is a value defined for the URI property of a package, model, or profile, it is automatically displayed on the corresponding shape. The following figure depicts an example of the package notation with the URI property value defined: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. If the isID property is set to true for the selected attribute, the {id} modifier is displayed in the property modifiers group on the class shape. The following figure depicts an example of the attribute notation when the isID property is set to true: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. The name of a synchronous message is now displayed on a diagram pane, even if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the synchronous message name notation: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Brackets “()” are no more added to a call message name if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the call message name notation: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

property.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>DEFAULT VALUE CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>DurationObservation::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] <span>=True</span></td><td colspan="1" rowspan="2">The default value has been removed. In effect, the value of the firstEvent property is now by default empty.</td></tr><tr><td>DurationConstraint::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] <span>=True</span></td></tr><tr><td>PackageableElement::visibility:VisibilityKind[1] =False → Public</td><td>The default value of this property has been changed to Public.This means that packageable elements are now by default public.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>MULTIPLICITY CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>EnumerationLiteral::enumeration:Enumeration [0..1] → [1]</td><td>The Enumeration must now be specified. It has become mandatory.</td></tr><tr><td>Multiplicity of ExecutionSpecification association with ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification: [1] → [0..2]</td><td>ExecutionSpecification is now associated with two ExecutionOccurrenceSpecifications:<br /><ul><li>The start ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the start of an action or behavior.</li><li>The finish ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the finish of an action or behavior.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>DERIVATION CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Constraint::/context → context</td><td>The context property of the constraint is no longer derived. It is now a settable property.</td></tr><tr><td>Message::/signature → signature</td><td>The signature of the message is no longer derived. However, it remains read-only.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="bold">Notation changes</h4><ol><li><p>If there is a value defined for the URI property of a package, model, or profile, it is automatically displayed on the corresponding shape. The following figure depicts an example of the package notation with the URI property value defined:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_uri_notation.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>If the isID property is set to true for the selected attribute, the {id} modifier is displayed in the property modifiers group on the class shape. The following figure depicts an example of the attribute notation when the isID property is set to true:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="131"><ri:attachment ri:filename="isID_property_notation.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>The name of a synchronous message is now displayed on a diagram pane, even if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the synchronous message name notation:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchCall_message_name.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>Brackets “()” are no more added to a call message name if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the call message name notation:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="153"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_message_name.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573633 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00776: SysML classes for open API

- page_id: `249573633`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573633/SysML+classes+for+open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:30:31.745+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Classes which are available for open API are included in SysML plugin open API documentation. Find these in *<SysML plugin installation directory>\openapi\docs*.

The*com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLUtilities* class was added to the open APIs. It provides utility methods for easier work with SysML projects.

Methods and classes marked as deprecated do not support the development of external plugins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Classes which are available for open API are included in SysML plugin open API documentation. Find these in <em>&lt;SysML plugin installation directory&gt;\openapi\docs</em>.</p><p>The<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLUtilities</em> class was added to the open APIs. It provides utility methods for easier work with SysML projects.</p><p>Methods and classes marked as deprecated do not support the development of external plugins.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=301368522 space=DEVG version=8 -->
## PAGE 00777: SysML ModelHierarchyVisitor

- page_id: `301368522`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/301368522/SysML+ModelHierarchyVisitor
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-04-30T23:03:32.758+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Visitor Pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The [SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.html) interface extends [KerMLModelHierarchyVisitor](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/KerMLModelHierarchyVisitor.html) and [SysMLModelVisitor](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLModelVisitor.html) to provide a enhanced implementation of the visitor pattern implementation for traversing SysML model elements. This interface is designed to handle hierarchical traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.

Key Features

- Hierarchical Traversal: SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor uses the inheritance structure of the SysML/KerML metamodel to determine which visitXXX method to call. This “hierarchy” refers to metaclass inheritance , not model‑level containment or decomposition. For example: This provides a clean, predictable way to distinguish elements by their kind without using `instanceof` or manual type checks.
  - ActionUsage → dispatches to visitActionUsage(...)
  - If not overridden, it falls back to visitUsage(...)
  - Then to visitFeature(...)
  - Then to visitElement(...)

For example, you can put some code into [visitActionUsage(actionUsage, sysMLVisitorContext)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.html#visitActionUsage(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.ActionUsage,C)) and it will be executed for all subclasses of ActionUsage (AcceptActionUsage, SendActionUsage etc)

```java
{

    @Override
    public void visitActionUsage(ActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
		super.visitActionUsage(element, context);
        System.out.println("Visiting ActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    @Override
    public void visitAcceptActionUsage(AcceptActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
		super.visitAcceptActionUsage(element, context);
        System.out.println("Visiting AcceptActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    // Implement other required methods similarly
}]]>
```

#### Using the Custom Visitor

Once you have created a custom visitor, you can use it to traverse SysML model elements. Here is an example of how to use the custom visitor:

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.html">SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor</a> interface extends <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/KerMLModelHierarchyVisitor.html">KerMLModelHierarchyVisitor</a> and <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLModelVisitor.html">SysMLModelVisitor</a> to provide a enhanced implementation of the visitor pattern implementation for traversing SysML model elements. This interface is designed to handle hierarchical traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.</p><p>Key Features</p><ul><li data-uuid="82e41c48-0e43-4af9-bb02-f2d7f3100195">Hierarchical Traversal:  <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.html">SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor</a> uses the inheritance structure of the SysML/KerML metamodel<span> to determine which </span>visitXXX<span> method to call. This “hierarchy” refers to </span>metaclass inheritance<span>, not model‑level containment or decomposition.</span><p><span>For example:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="95014cdd-faed-42cb-9054-6c1ddb4ee59f"><p><span>ActionUsage → dispatches to visitActionUsage(...)</span></p></li><li data-uuid="50380f7b-3caf-4e43-ab16-892e90612d5a"><p><span>If not overridden, it falls back to visitUsage(...)</span></p></li><li data-uuid="8ca15f44-c7cf-4864-a23f-5392612d0de4"><p><span>Then to visitFeature(...)</span></p></li><li data-uuid="163a30da-9345-41ad-ba4c-84079174d777"><p><span>Then to visitElement(...)</span></p></li></ul><p><span>This provides a clean, predictable way to distinguish elements by their kind without using <code>instanceof</code> or manual type checks.</span></p></li></ul><p>For example, you can put some code into <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.html#visitActionUsage(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.ActionUsage,C)">visitActionUsage(actionUsage, sysMLVisitorContext)</a>  and it will be executed for all subclasses of ActionUsage (AcceptActionUsage, SendActionUsage etc)</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="962eff3e-9b60-4917-b4f5-d0592084f833"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLVisitorContext;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.*;

public class CustomSysMLVisitor implements SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor<SysMLVisitorContext> {

    @Override
    public void visitActionUsage(ActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
		super.visitActionUsage(element, context);
        System.out.println("Visiting ActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    @Override
    public void visitAcceptActionUsage(AcceptActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) {
		super.visitAcceptActionUsage(element, context);
        System.out.println("Visiting AcceptActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    // Implement other required methods similarly
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2 style="text-align: left;">Using the Custom Visitor</h2><p style="text-align: left;">Once you have created a custom visitor, you can use it to traverse SysML model elements. Here is an example of how to use the custom visitor:<br /> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c89bf51-17da-4b91-acbd-f15dd1f23640"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Package;

public class Main
{
	public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception
	{
		MyCustomVisitor myVisitor = new CustomSysMLVisitor();
		Package root = ..;
		for (Element element : root.getOwnedElement())
		{
			element.accept(myVisitor);
		}
		System.out.println("Traversal complete.");
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00778: SysML ModelVisitor

- page_id: `301368519`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/301368519/SysML+ModelVisitor
- version_number: 13
- version_date: `2026-04-30T22:56:57.753+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model > Visitor Pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The [SysMLModelVisitor](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLModelVisitor.html)interface extends [KerMLModelVisitor](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/KerMLModelVisitor.html)to provide a visitor pattern implementation for traversing SysML model elements. This interface is designed to handle the traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner. Every [com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html) has the [accept(AbstractVisitor)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/BaseElement.html#accept(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.AbstractVisitor)) method for visiting it. 
 
Key Features :

- Visitor Pattern: The visitor pattern allows for the traversal of SysML model elements, ensuring that each element is visited in a structured manner.

The [SysMLVisitorContext](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/SysMLVisitorContext.html)class is used to manage the state during traversal of SysML model elements. It provides methods to track visited elements and other relevant information..

```java
{
    @Override
    public void visitAcceptActionUsage(AcceptActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) 
	{
        System.out.println("Visiting AcceptActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    @Override
    public void visitActionDefinition(ActionDefinition element, SysMLVisitorContext context) 
	{
        System.out.println("Visiting ActionDefinition: " + element.getName());
    }

    @Override
    public void visitActionUsage(ActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context) 
	{
        System.out.println("Visiting ActionUsage: " + element.getName());
    }

    // Implement other required methods similarly
}]]>
```

#### Using the Custom Visitor

Once you have created a custom visitor, you can use it to traverse SysML model elements. Here is an example of how to use the custom visitor:

```java

```

It provides methods to track visited elements and other relevant information..<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ae17603-9d30-40e1-bee6-954d6026bb50"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLModelVisitor;

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573630 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00779: SysML Profile

- page_id: `249573630`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573630/SysML+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:30:19.110+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to import *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to use this API class.

****

**Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)**

Usage includes “SysMLProfile.Stereotype.*STEREOTYPE_NAME*”.

For example, SysMLProfile.RequirementStereotype.*ID*returns a string of “Id”.

****

**Get a stereotype element**

Usage includes:

“SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(project).stereotype().getStereotype()” where project refers to a project that uses SysML Profile.

“SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(element).stereotype().getStereotype()“ where element refers to the element in a project that uses SysML Profile.

For example, SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(project).block().getStereotype() returns the reference to the «Block» stereotype object.

**Check if an element is stereotyped**

Usage includes SysMLProfile.Stereotype.*isInstance*(Elem) where "Elem" is the element you want to check.

For example, given an element “Elem”, SysMLProfile.BlockStereotype.*isInstance*(Elem) returns *True* if the element “Elem” has «Block» stereotype applied and returns *False* otherwise.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to import <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to use this API class.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Usage includes “SysMLProfile.Stereotype.</span><em style="text-align: left;">STEREOTYPE_NAME</em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">”.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">For example, SysMLProfile.RequirementStereotype.</span><em>ID</em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>returns a string of “Id”.</span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a stereotype element</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Usage includes:</p><p style="text-align: left;">“SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(project).stereotype().getStereotype()” where project refers to a project that uses SysML Profile.</p><p style="text-align: left;">“SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(element).stereotype().getStereotype()“ where element refers to the element in a project that uses SysML Profile.</p><p style="text-align: left;">For example, SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(project).block().getStereotype() returns the reference to the «Block» stereotype object.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Check if an element is stereotyped</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Usage includes  SysMLProfile.Stereotype.<em>isInstance</em>(Elem) where &quot;Elem&quot; is the element you want to check.</p><p style="text-align: left;">For example, given an element “Elem”, SysMLProfile.BlockStereotype.<em>isInstance</em>(Elem) returns <em>True</em> if the element “Elem” has «Block» stereotype applied and returns <em>False</em> otherwise.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573632 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00780: SysML Profile API Changes

- page_id: `249573632`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573632/SysML+Profile+API+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:30:46.624+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based > SysML v1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML Profile API changes were made in relation to the SysML 1.4 support.

The following constants were moved from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile*:

public static final String CONSTRAINTPROPERTY_STEREOTYPE = "ConstraintProperty";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_STEREOTYPE = "QuantityKind";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DEFINITIONURI_PROPERTY = "definitionURI";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = "description";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_SYMBOL_PROPERTY = "symbol";

public static final String UNIT_STEREOTYPE = "Unit";

The following methods were moved from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile*:

getConstraintProperty()

getQuantityKind()

getUnit()

isQuantityKind()

isUnit()

isConstraintProperty()

The constant NESTEDCONNECTOREND_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY changed to ELEMENTPROPERTYPATH_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SysML Profile API changes were made in relation to the SysML 1.4 support.</p><p>The following constants were moved from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em>:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String CONSTRAINTPROPERTY_STEREOTYPE = &quot;ConstraintProperty&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_STEREOTYPE = &quot;QuantityKind&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DEFINITIONURI_PROPERTY = &quot;definitionURI&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = &quot;description&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_SYMBOL_PROPERTY = &quot;symbol&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String UNIT_STEREOTYPE = &quot;Unit&quot;;</p><p>The following methods were moved from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em>:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getConstraintProperty()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getQuantityKind()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getUnit()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isQuantityKind()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isUnit()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isConstraintProperty()</p><p>The constant NESTEDCONNECTOREND_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY changed to ELEMENTPROPERTYPATH_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573629 space=DEVG version=5 -->
## PAGE 00781: SysML v1

- page_id: `249573629`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573629/SysML+v1
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-02-05T18:50:18.630+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML-based
- labels: ['open-api']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard stereotypes in SysML plugin are defined in SysML Profile and MD Customization for SysML Profile. Both profiles have their corresponding API classes: *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/SysMLProfile.html)*and *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.html)*, respectively. Each class allows you to:

- Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag).
- Get a stereotype element.
- Check if an element is stereotyped.

Find JavaDoc of SysML plugin in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/docs/sysml_javadoc.zip*

For example, *<modeling_tool_version_number>_no_install\openapi\docs\sysml_javadoc.zip*

Find JavaDoc of Requirements plugin in <modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/docs/requirements_javadoc.zip

For example, *<modeling_tool_version_number>**_no_install\openapi\docs\requirements_javadoc.zip*

Extract a .jar file and double-click the*index.html*to open it.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling Tools Developer Guide' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Standard stereotypes in SysML plugin are defined in SysML Profile and MD Customization for SysML Profile. Both profiles have their corresponding API classes: <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/SysMLProfile.html"> com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile </a></em>and <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.html">com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</a></em>, respectively. Each class allows you to:</p><ul><li>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag).</li><li>Get a stereotype element.</li><li>Check if an element is stereotyped.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Find JavaDoc of SysML plugin in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;/openapi/docs/sysml_javadoc.zip</em></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a03c3705-33fb-422d-a0ec-c8eced569dd6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, <em>&lt;modeling_tool_version_number&gt;_no_install\openapi\docs\sysml_javadoc.zip</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Find JavaDoc of Requirements plugin in &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;/openapi/docs/requirements_javadoc.zip</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1134b5c-9d74-4041-ac03-9813137509ed"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, <em>&lt;modeling_tool_version_number&gt;</em><em>_no_install\<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">openapi\docs\requirements_javadoc.zip</span></em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Extract a .jar file and double-click the </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">index.html</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> to open it.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Related pages</span></strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="efbbc27d-b595-4074-9030-22a314abc9ed"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling Tools Developer Guide" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=286557293 space=DEVG version=14 -->
## PAGE 00782: SysML v2

- page_id: `286557293`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/286557293/SysML+v2
- version_number: 14
- version_date: `2026-04-15T23:48:52.427+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SysML v2 implementation is based on the OMG KerML 1.0 and SysML 2.0 metamodels. This documentation does not provide a detailed description of all KerML/SysML metamodel elements and their properties. Instead, the implementation fully aligns with the OMG specifications, which can be found on the Object Management Group website:

- https://www.omg.org/spec/KerML/1.0/About-KerML
- https://www.omg.org/sysml/sysmlv2/

Users are expected to work with interfaces from the following packages:

- [com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/package-summary.html)
- [com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/package-summary.html)

Implementation classes (e.g., *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.impl.ElementImpl](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/validation/ElementValidationRuleImpl.html)*) must not be used directly in user code.

The root interface for all model elements is *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html)*. 
It extends both:

- [com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/BaseElement.html)
- [com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.model.ModelElement](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

All model structures are derived from *[Element](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html)* and follow the definitions provided in the OMG KerML and SysML specifications.

All attributes and references defined in the SysML specification are implemented and accessible via standard getter and setter methods.

Typical interaction with the model includes:

- Navigating elements via containment and references
- Accessing properties using getters
- Modifying values using setters within a controlled context

The model has the following concurrency characteristics:

- Read operations are thread-safe
- Write operations are not thread-safe

All write operations must be externally synchronized or executed within the appropriate transaction or session mechanism.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


## PAGE 00783: SysML v2 Textual Support

- page_id: `314181697`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/314181697/SysML+v2+Textual+Support
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-06-30T22:27:34.719+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Overview

[SysMLTextualNotationUtils](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/textual/texteditor/ui/SysMLTextualNotationUtils.html)provides support for working with SysML textual notation inside the modeling environment.

#### Key Features

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Export | Generate textual SysML from a Namespace |
| Import | Build model elements from textual SysML |
| Editor Launch | Open textual editor for any element |
| Read Content | Get live editor text |
| Write Content | Update editor text programmatically |

#### Getting Started

| Pages | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Describes how to export and import SysML Text |
|  | Describe how to read and write textual editor content |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00784: Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `301367391`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/301367391/Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 14
- version_date: `2026-04-17T04:44:49.514+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working with SysML v2 Projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The*[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html)* class class provides utility methods for creating and opening SysML v2 projects in Teamwork Cloud.

#### Project Creation

Creates a new SysML v2 project in Teamwork Cloud.

##### Example

```text

```

##### Notes

- Project is created locally in UPS before being uploaded to Teamwork Cloud
- Project name must be unique in Teamwork Cloud and in UPS
- Throws an exception if the upload fails

#### Project Opening

Opens an existing Teamwork Cloud project using its qualified name.

##### Example

```text

```

##### Notes

- Project must exist in Teamwork Cloud
- Qualified name must match exactly
- Throws an exception if loading fails

#### Summary

| Operation | Method |
| --- | --- |
| Create project | createTWCProject(projectName) |
| Open project | loadTWCProject(projectName) |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;"><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper</a></em> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">class <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">class provides utility methods for creating and opening</span></span> SysML v2 projects in Teamwork Cloud.</p><h2>Project Creation</h2><p>Creates a new SysML v2 project in Teamwork Cloud.</p><h3>Example</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53376c73-15a9-4e91-8d76-b015fe316a71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;

Project project = SysMLProjectHelper.createTWCProject("MyProject"); 
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Notes</h3><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project</a> is created locally in UPS before being uploaded to Teamwork Cloud</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project</a> name must be unique in Teamwork Cloud and in UPS</li><li>Throws an exception if the upload fails</li></ul><h2>Project Opening</h2><p>Opens an existing Teamwork Cloud project using its qualified name.</p><h3>Example</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b21765d-30e4-4dba-95c7-7362bd2df06e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.core.SysMLProjectHelper;

SysMLProjectHelper.loadTWCProject("MyProject");  
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Notes</h3><ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html">Project</a> must exist in Teamwork Cloud</li><li>Qualified name must match exactly</li><li>Throws an exception if loading fails</li></ul><h2>Summary</h2><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p>Operation</p></th><th><p>Method</p></th></tr><tr><td><p>Create project</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html#createTWCProject(java.lang.String)">createTWCProject(projectName)</a></code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Open project</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/core/SysMLProjectHelper.html#loadTWCProject(java.lang.String)">loadTWCProject(projectName)</a></code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573327 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00786: Testing plugin

- page_id: `249573327`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573327/Testing+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:34.191+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > General > Plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can test if your plugin sample works by checking described messages or by checking the *magicdraw.log* file.

To check messages

1. Restart MagicDraw (or other modeling tool you are using). On startup, a message should appear: [ATTACHMENT filename='message_My_Plugin_init.png']
2. Then exit a program. Another message should appear: [ATTACHMENT filename='message_My_Plugin_close.png']

To check the *magicdraw.log* file

- On the Help menu, click About MagicDraw . Then, in the Environment tab, click the Log File link.
- You can find the*magicdraw.log* file in a configuration file storage:
  - For Windows operating system, it is *C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\<MagicDraw version number>*
  - For other operating systems, it is *<user.home>/.magicdraw/<MagicDraw version number>*

After successful startup, the following information should be provided at the end of the *magicdraw.log* file:

```bash

```

Also all plugins and their statuses are listed in the program **Environment****Options** dialog, in the Plugins tab.

Looking at the file is the best way to find problems when the plugin does not work.

Another message should appear: <br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_My_Plugin_close.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p> </p><p>To check the <em>magicdraw.log</em> file</p><hr /><p> </p><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">On the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Help</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> menu, click </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">About MagicDraw</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">. Then, in the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Environment</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> tab, click the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Log File</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> link. </span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">You can find the<em> magicdraw.log</em> file in a configuration file storage:</span><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">For Windows operating system, it is <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">C:\Users\&lt;username&gt;\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\&lt;MagicDraw version number&gt;</span></em></span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For other operating systems, it is <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">&lt;user.home&gt;/.magicdraw/&lt;MagicDraw version number&gt;</span></em></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><p>After successful startup, the following information should be provided at the end of the <em>magicdraw.log</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea4d7537-8edd-4505-8e99-32eb1b6c723f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[LOAD PLUGINS:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296124994 space=DEVG version=24 -->
## PAGE 00787: Textual Import/Export

- page_id: `296124994`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/296124994/Textual+Import+Export
- version_number: 24
- version_date: `2026-06-29T21:27:19.283+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > SysML v2 Textual Support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML v2 textual notation is a **human-readable, formal syntax** for representing SysML v2 models as text. It provides an alternative to graphical modeling by allowing modelers to define, inspect, and manipulate models using structured language constructs.

The**[SysMLTextualNotationUtils](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/textual/texteditor/ui/SysMLTextualNotationUtils.html)**provides a stateless API for converting SysML v2 models to and from their textual representations.

**1. Import: Text to Model**

Creates model elements from a textual source. Code below parses SysML v2 text and materializes it within anew namespace in the`[ModelElementProject](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/project/ModelElementProject.html)`.

Failures may happen because of syntax/parsing errors, semantic validation issues, or model conflicts within the target project.

```java

```

After successful import in the containment tree we will see the model elements:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**2. Export: Model to Text** 
Code below converts a`[Namespace](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Namespace.html)`into a SysML v2-compliant string.

```java

```

It provides an alternative to graphical modeling by allowing modelers to define, inspect, and manipulate models using structured language constructs.</p><p>The<span> </span><strong class="Yjhzub"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh2/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/magic/sysml/textual/texteditor/ui/SysMLTextualNotationUtils.html">SysMLTextualNotationUtils</a></strong><span> </span>provides a stateless API for converting SysML v2 models to and from their textual representations.</p><p> <strong class="Yjhzub">1. Import: Text to Model</strong></p><p>Creates model elements from a textual source. Code below p<span class="T286Pc">arses SysML v2 text and materializes it within a<span> new namespace in the </span><code class="o8j0Mc"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/project/ModelElementProject.html">ModelElementProject</a></code>. </span></p><p><span class="T286Pc">Failures may happen because of syntax/parsing errors, semantic validation issues, or model conflicts within the target project.</span><span> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a29ba23a-ff2a-451f-99b1-497fa4920be1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Import</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.project.ModelElementProject
````html
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.textual.texteditor.ui.SysMLTextualNotationUtils;

ModelElementProject project = ...;

String sysmlText = """
package Example {
    part def Engine {
        attribute power : ScalarValues::Real;
    }
}
""";

SysMLTextualNotationUtils.importTextual(project, sysmlText);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span class="Yjhzub">After successful import in the containment tree we will see the model elements:</span></p><p><span class="Yjhzub"><ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-3-23_16-37-45.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p><br /></p><p><strong class="Yjhzub">2. Export: Model to Text</strong><br /><span class="T286Pc">Code below converts a<span> </span><code class="o8j0Mc"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Namespace.html">Namespace</a></code><span> </span>into a SysML v2-compliant string.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffe0a810-e20f-4430-9ed0-9e39a5926b4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Export</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.magic.sysml.textual.texteditor.ui.SysMLTextualNotationUtils;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace;

Namespace namespace = ...;
String text = SysMLTextualNotationUtils.exportTextual(namespace);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573676 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00788: Token-based authentication

- page_id: `249573676`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573676/Token-based+authentication
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-20T14:41:41.795+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs > Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Use the following procedures to authenticate your application or script with Teamwork Cloud.

##### Pre-configuring Authentication server

Authentication server implements the OpenID Connect standard with several customizations. To access the OpenID Connect configuration, go to *https://<auth_server_host>:<port>/authentication/.well-known/openid-configuration*. Here is a sample of a returned configuration:

```text
:8443/authentication/api/validate",
    "jwks_uri":"https://:8443/authentication/jwks.json",
    "subject_types_supported":["public"],
    "id_token_signing_alg_values_supported":["RS256"],
    "signout_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/api/signout",
    "issuer":"https://:8443/authentication",
    "authorization_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/authorize",
    "token_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/api/token"
}]]>
```

Configure Authentication server to accept new client applications by changing these parameters in the **authserver.properties** file:

1. Add URL of the client app to the whitelist, separating URLs with commas: authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist . This can be either a full URL where users should be redirected back from the Authentication server, or just the beginning of it. The authorization endpoint will not accept redirect uri parameters that cannot be found in the whitelist.
2. Add new client IDs separated with commas: authentication.client.ids . You might need to uncomment this line first. The authorization endpoint will not accept client_id parameters that cannot be found in this list.

There are a few deviations from the standard OpenID Connect specification:

- When invoking the token endpoint, HTTP header X-Auth-Secret with secret must be passed with the value from webappplatform.properties , parameter authentication.client.secret .
- ID tokens have expiration time (configuration property authentication.token.expirity from authserver.properties ), they must be refreshed through the token endpoint by passing refresh tokens.

To call TWC REST API with a generated authentication token, the token should be sent in the header of the request:

```text
]]>
```

##### Authentication with user interaction

The basic Authorization flow should be as follows:

1. Redirect the user to Authentication server (***authorization_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP GET parameters:

```text
client_id=
response_type=code]]>
```

2. After the user signs in, receive HTTP GET request with a code parameter.

3. Send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (***token****_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

```text
client_id=
grant_type=authorization_code
code=]]>
```

4. Receive the JSON response with ID Token that can be used to authorize with Teamwork Cloud and refresh token that later should be used to refresh ID Token.

5. Refresh the ID Token by sending HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (***token****_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

```text
client_id=
grant_type=refresh_token   
refresh_token=]]>
```

##### Authentication without user interaction

To get a token without user interaction, i.e. using some predefined username and password and make only server-server calls, the system needs to send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server with HTTP headers X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

- Headers: ]]>

Authorization: Basic xxxxxxxxx, where xxxxxx is base64 encoded username:password

- Query parameters: ]]>

If your client ID is added into the **authentication.client.permanent**list, the returned token will have a longer expiration time, configured in the parameter **authentication.permanent.token.expirity**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f592144-074b-424f-be69-8530c09c134b" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use the following procedures to authenticate your application or script with Teamwork Cloud.</p><h3>Pre-configuring Authentication server</h3><p>Authentication server implements the OpenID Connect standard with several customizations. To access the OpenID Connect configuration, go to <em>https://&lt;auth_server_host&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/authentication/.well-known/openid-configuration</em>. Here is a sample of a returned configuration:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce45cafd-e140-4db1-8df6-865312107668"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "response_types_supported":["id_token","code"],
    "validation_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/validate",
    "jwks_uri":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/jwks.json",
    "subject_types_supported":["public"],
    "id_token_signing_alg_values_supported":["RS256"],
    "signout_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/signout",
    "issuer":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication",
    "authorization_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/authorize",
    "token_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/token"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Configure Authentication server to accept new client applications by changing these parameters in the <strong>authserver.properties</strong> file:</p><ol><li>Add URL of the client app to the whitelist, separating URLs with commas: <strong>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</strong>. This can be either a full URL where users should be redirected back from the Authentication server, or just the beginning of it. The authorization endpoint will not accept redirect uri parameters that cannot be found in the whitelist.</li><li>Add new client IDs separated with commas: <strong>authentication.client.ids</strong>. You might need to uncomment this line first. The authorization endpoint will not accept<em> </em><strong>client_id </strong>parameters that cannot be found in this list.</li></ol><p>There are a few deviations from the standard OpenID Connect specification:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a2485283-f574-4483-91f0-c461ad6de081">When invoking the token endpoint, HTTP header X-Auth-Secret with secret must be passed with the value from <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">webappplatform.properties</span></strong>, parameter <strong>authentication.client.secret</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="040a448f-4855-4da6-b43d-7b21c1fbb430">ID tokens have expiration time (configuration property <strong>authentication.token.expirity </strong>from <strong>authserver.properties</strong>), they must be refreshed through the token endpoint by passing refresh tokens.</li></ul><p>To call TWC REST API with a generated authentication token, the token should be sent in the header of the request: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19b3247d-b03e-4724-b739-cb2353ae6439"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Token <received_id_token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Authentication with user interaction</span></h3><p>The basic Authorization flow should be as follows:</p><p>1. Redirect the user to Authentication server (<em><strong>authorization_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP GET parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14ce61e5-bd41-436a-9f42-6f6b75a27d22"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
response_type=code]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>2. After the user signs in, receive HTTP GET request with a code parameter.</p><p>3. Send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (<em><strong>token</strong><strong>_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36f8bd4f-e472-4799-8623-8255439ecc88"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
grant_type=authorization_code
code=<code_received_after_user_signs_in>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>4. Receive the JSON response with ID Token that can be used to authorize with Teamwork Cloud and refresh token that later should be used to refresh ID Token.</p><p>5. Refresh the ID Token by sending HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (<em><strong>token</strong><strong>_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95aee7b0-dc5f-4adf-88ca-f4f957778264"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
grant_type=refresh_token   
refresh_token=<refresh_token_value>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Authentication without user interaction</h3><p>To get a token without user interaction, i.e. using some predefined username and password and make only server-server calls, the system needs to send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server with HTTP headers X-Auth-Secret and parameters:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Headers: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb2c4bba-b35c-44aa-a2d2-5463ca3ba029"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[X-Auth-Secret: <secret from authentication.client.secret >]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><span>Authorization: Basic xxxxxxxxx, where xxxxxx is base64 encoded username:password</span></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Query parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e66a619-e9f7-47f4-b0d2-073a8a58f310"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[grant_type=client_credentials
client_id=<your_client_id>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p>If your client ID is added into the <strong>authentication.client.permanent </strong>list, the returned token will have a longer expiration time, configured in the parameter <strong>authentication.permanent.token.expirity</strong>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249573677 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00789: Token-based authentication sample for Teamwork Cloud REST API

- page_id: `249573677`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/249573677/Token-based+authentication+sample+for+Teamwork+Cloud+REST+API
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T15:36:14.873+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs > Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Teamwork Cloud REST API has an endpoint, which implements token-based authentication described in the [Token-based authentication](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/DEVG/Token-based+authentication) page.

##### Setting up token-based authentication

To set up Teamwork Cloud and Authentication server for token-based authentication using REST API endpoint

1. Set up Authentication server to work with your SAML/SSO, if needed.
2. Open <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf /authserver.properties .
3. Find key**authentication****.client****.ids** and add **,twc****-rest****-api** at the end of the value. Save and close the file. The comma before **twc-rest-api** is a separator.
4. Open <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration /application.conf .
5. Find the esi .auth block and set the server value to your authentication server IP. Save and close the file.
6. Restart the Authentication server and Teamwork Cloud server.

##### Using token-based authentication

To use token-based authentication

1. Open REST API at the following URL: https ://<ip> :8111 /osmc /authen /login on a browser.
2. You should be redirected to the Authentication server login page.
3. Enter your credentials. The browser shows you a token, usually starting with eyJ … . [ATTACHMENT filename='Login Token.png']
4. Copy the token and use it to log on to REST API. The token is used in an Authorization header with the **Token** Type.

For example (using a token with cURL):

```text

```

The result is as follows:

```text
GET /osmc/login HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:8111
> User-Agent: curl/7.55.1
> Accept: */*
> Authorization: Token
eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJBZG1pbmlzdHJhdG9yIiwiYXVkIjoidHdjLXJlc3QtYXBp
IiwiaXNzIjoiaHR0cHM6XC9cLzEyNy4wLjAuMTo4NTU1XC9hdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbiIsImV4cCI6M
TYwNjI5OTc3NywiaWF0IjoxNjA2Mjk4ODc3fQ.bA-
S5hHeSlV8AFoQVzzfIseC3qlmqQoBQREiapHN6I5CcvwetKdSVztWKkssSGjm31Y1zqoULio7_1Ma
mtGBbbzvA1WWQYFRiYk0D612yNDv4uNHBbNLNEv61TYNLwdPwPh0atVRehkh-
LSgjipXTvXj4mZViE0NHKIG9U7htA9Zzvxvck2JDXe_eU2-
4TCNm8II89ROaEb1tZ5nD84ieRbzJWqrcVTdqU2YfbIUeew5Nir8obkLYgixBXFKWsTHi3jNuoBx3
KcAIyZqL6cjtsCER4wbk4PEEDC57UVsOcsXWr6yvXIoVdJMOiDHo_fJMkgOjDqSyIL-2B21O-Y-GA
>
< HTTP/1.1 204 No Content
< Content-Length: 0
< Content-Type: application/octet-stream
< Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:08:44 GMT
< Accept-Ranges: bytes
< Server: Restlet-Framework/2.2.3
< Vary: Accept-Charset, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language, Accept
< Set-Cookie: twc-rest-current-user=Administrator; Path=/osmc; Expires=Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:23:44 GMT
< Set-Cookie: twc-rest-session-id=f40ef933-5461-4058-a1e7-9b8d4021aa8a; Path=/osmc; Expires=Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:23:44 GMT
<
* Connection #0 to host 127.0.0.1 left intact]]>
```

##### **Limitations**

This REST API endpoint only displays the ID token. Usually, ID token is not very long-living. You can configure the ID token expiration in *authserver.properties* file (using property **authentication.token.expirity**).

Usually the ID token needs to be refreshed as described in the page [Token-based authentication](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/DEVG/Token-based+authentication). However, this REST API does not display a refresh token, which is needed to refresh the ID token.

As a workaround, long-living ID tokens can be generated by adding **,twc****-rest****-api** to the *authserver.properties* file property **authentication.client.unlimited**. In such case, ID token expiration will be calculated using property **authentication.unlimited.token.expirity**.

Use this feature with caution and make sure that such long-living ID token is adequately protected.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6991d4b5-86b2-4655-b034-a4b93df236c3" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Teamwork Cloud REST API has an endpoint, which implements token-based authentication described in the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/DEVG/Token-based+authentication">Token-based authentication</a> page.</p><h3>Setting up token-based authentication</h3><p>To set up Teamwork Cloud and Authentication server for token-based authentication using REST API endpoint</p><hr /><ol><li>Set up Authentication server to work with your SAML/SSO, if needed.</li><li>Open <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em><em>/authserver.properties</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find key<strong> authentication</strong><strong>.client</strong><strong>.ids</strong> and add <strong>,twc</strong><strong>-rest</strong><strong>-api</strong> at the end of the value. Save and close the file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cc7d612-5d17-451e-92a6-e30cd135e6e4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The comma before <strong>twc-rest-api</strong> is a separator.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Open <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/TeamworkCloud/configuration</em><em>/application.conf</em>.</li><li>Find the <strong>esi</strong><strong>.auth</strong> block and set the server value to your authentication server IP. Save and close the file.</li><li>Restart the Authentication server and Teamwork Cloud server.</li></ol><h3>Using token-based authentication</h3><p>To use token-based authentication</p><hr /><ol><li>Open REST API at the following URL:<em> </em><em>https</em><em>://&lt;ip&gt;</em><em>:8111</em><em>/osmc</em><em>/authen</em><em>/login</em> on a browser.</li><li>You should be redirected to the Authentication server login page.</li><li>Enter your credentials. The browser shows you a token, usually starting with <em>eyJ</em><em>…</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Login Token.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Token-based authentication sample for Teamwork Cloud REST API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy the token and use it to log on to REST API.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11e1ad8f-eb11-44ad-a098-14fd04c40a9c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The token is used in an Authorization header with the <strong>Token</strong> Type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>For example (using a token with cURL):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="304de95e-49e5-4fdf-bf75-ac7afe1c6f20"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -v -k -H "Authorization: Token
eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJBZG1pbmlzdHJhdG9yIiwiYXVkIjoidHdjLXJlc3QtYXBp
IiwiaXNzIjoiaHR0cHM6XC9cLzEyNy4wLjAuMTo4NTU1XC9hdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbiIsImV4cCI6M

S5hHeSlV8AFoQVzzfIseC3qlmqQoBQREiapHN6I5CcvwetKdSVztWKkssSGjm31Y1zqoULio7_1Ma
mtGBbbzvA1WWQYFRiYk0D612yNDv4uNHBbNLNEv61TYNLwdPwPh0atVRehkh-
LSgjipXTvXj4mZViE0NHKIG9U7htA9Zzvxvck2JDXe_eU2-
4TCNm8II89ROaEb1tZ5nD84ieRbzJWqrcVTdqU2YfbIUeew5Nir8obkLYgixBXFKWsTHi3jNuoBx3
KcAIyZqL6cjtsCER4wbk4PEEDC57UVsOcsXWr6yvXIoVdJMOiDHo_fJMkgOjDqSyIL-2B21O-Y-GA"
https://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/login]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The result is as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26de0aad-bf8a-4fcb-81d6-3a3adf6b8314"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[> GET /osmc/login HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:8111
> User-Agent: curl/7.55.1
> Accept: */*
> Authorization: Token
eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJBZG1pbmlzdHJhdG9yIiwiYXVkIjoidHdjLXJlc3QtYXBp

mtGBbbzvA1WWQYFRiYk0D612yNDv4uNHBbNLNEv61TYNLwdPwPh0atVRehkh-
LSgjipXTvXj4mZViE0NHKIG9U7htA9Zzvxvck2JDXe_eU2-

* Connection #0 to host 127.0.0.1 left intact]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target"><strong style="font-size: 16.0px;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Limitations</strong></h3><hr /><p>This REST API endpoint only displays the ID token. Usually, ID token is not very long-living. You can configure the ID token expiration in <em>authserver.properties</em> file (using property <strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">authentication.token.expirity</span></strong>).</p><p>Usually the ID token needs to be refreshed as described in the page <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/DEVG/Token-based+authentication">Token-based authentication</a>. However, this REST API does not display a refresh token, which is needed to refresh the ID token.</p><p>As a workaround, long-living ID tokens can be generated by adding <strong>,twc</strong><strong>-rest</strong><strong>-api</strong> to the <em>authserver.properties</em> file property <strong>authentication.client.unlimited</strong>. In such case, ID token expiration will be calculated using property <strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">authentication.unlimited.token.expirity</span></strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e997075-5626-4025-9b88-a394914eeff4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use this feature with caution and make sure that such long-living ID token is adequately protected.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=301368105 space=DEVG version=20 -->
## PAGE 00790: Transaction Listeners and Model Changes

- page_id: `301368105`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/301368105/Transaction+Listeners+and+Model+Changes
- version_number: 20
- version_date: `2026-04-28T19:41:57.525+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > SysML v2 > Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In SysML v2 Open API, model changes are performed inside a transaction to clearly define the start and end of modification operations.

[TransactionListeners](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html) let you observe those changes and add additional logic before the transaction is completed. This is useful when a change in one part of the model should trigger related updates elsewhere.

#### Transaction lifecycle

A transaction goes through these phases:

- transactionStarted(...) – called once when the transaction begins
- transactionPreCompleted(...) – called before completion and may be invoked multiple times
- transactionCompleted(...) – called once after the transaction is finalized

For details, see [TransactionListener](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html).

The most important phase for reacting to model changes is [transactionPreCompleted(...)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)).

#### Modifying the model during a transaction

The [transactionPreCompleted(...)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)) method receives model changes recorded during the transaction as a collection of [PropertyChangeEvent](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.desktop/java/beans/PropertyChangeEvent.html) instances.

At this point:

- the model has already been updated
- the transaction is still open
- additional model changes can still be applied

You can safely make additional model changes inside [transactionPreCompleted(...)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)).

If a listener makes additional changes, the transaction is re-evaluated and listeners are invoked again with the updated set of changes. This allows implementing follow-up logic within the same transaction.

#### Example: Registering a transaction listener and reacting to element ownership changes

Register transaction listeners through [TransactionManager](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionManager.html). The following example listens for ownership changes on [OwningMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html) and updates the moved element name.

```text
events, TransactionOptions options)
	{
		processMovedElements(events);
	}

	private static void processMovedElements(Collection events)
	{
		for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
		{
			if (EFeatureNames.ELEMENT__OWNED_RELATIONSHIP.equals(event.getPropertyName())
				&& event.getNewValue() instanceof OwningMembership owningMembership)
			{
				Element element = owningMembership.getOwnedMemberElement();
				if (element != null)
				{
					element.setDeclaredName(element.getDeclaredName() + "_moved");
				}
			}
		}
	}
}, OperationKind.EDITING_EXECUTE);
]]>
```

#### How it works

- the ownership relationship ( OwningMembership ) is updated
- the change is exposed as a PropertyChangeEvent
- the listener inspects the change
- the moved element is retrieved using getOwnedMemberElement()
- an additional update is applied using setDeclaredName(String)

#### Important behavior

- transactionPreCompleted(...) may be called multiple times during a single transaction
- additional changes made by listeners become part of the same transaction
- transactionCompleted(...) is called only after the transaction reaches a stable state
- the model must not be modified in transactionCompleted(...)

#### Things to watch for

Because listeners can modify the model, make sure the logic does not create unintended cycles.

Typical problems include:

- repeatedly updating the same property
- triggering the same condition multiple times

Listener logic should always converge to a stable state.

#### Related APIs

- TransactionListener
- TransactionManager
- OperationKind
- TransactionManagers
- ModelElementProject
- ModelElement
- Element
- TransactionOptions

updated</li><li>the transaction is still open</li><li>additional model changes can still be applied</li></ul><p>You can safely make additional model changes inside <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionListener.html#transactionPreCompleted(java.util.Collection,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions)">transactionPreCompleted(...)</a>.</p><p>If a listener makes additional changes, the transaction is re-evaluated and listeners are invoked again with the updated set of changes. This allows implementing follow-up logic within the same transaction.</p><h2>Example: Registering a transaction listener and reacting to element ownership changes</h2><p>Register transaction listeners through <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/transaction/TransactionManager.html">TransactionManager</a>. The following example listens for ownership changes on <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/OwningMembership.html">OwningMembership</a> and updates the moved element name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15f57a91-181c-4d61-a5e7-f21516b26d3d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionManager;
````html
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionManagers;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.project.ModelElementProject;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionListener;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.TransactionOptions;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.OwningMembership;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;

import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.transaction.OperationKind;

import java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent;
import java.util.Collection;

ModelElementProject project = ...;
TransactionManager transactionManager = TransactionManagers.getTransactionManager(project);

transactionManager.addListener(new TransactionListener()
{
	@Override
	public void transactionPreCompleted(Collection<PropertyChangeEvent> events, TransactionOptions options)
	{
		processMovedElements(events);
	}

	private static void processMovedElements(Collection<PropertyChangeEvent> events)
	{
		for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
		{
			if (EFeatureNames.ELEMENT__OWNED_RELATIONSHIP.equals(event.getPropertyName())
				&& event.getNewValue() instanceof OwningMembership owningMembership)
			{
				Element element = owningMembership.getOwnedMemberElement();
				if (element != null)
				{
					element.setDeclaredName(element.getDeclaredName() + "_moved");
				}
			}
		}
	}
}, OperationKind.EDITING_EXECUTE);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>How it works</h2><ul><li>the ownership relationship (<a
````
